PREFACE

During the last decade or so, my friend Shri Narinder Sagar
has been urging me to bring oul a good classical text in English on
the subject of MUHURTA JYOTISH (Electional Astrology) as read-
ers did ask for this subject time and again.

Hence the credit for the present rendering of "Muhurta
Chintamani" in Engiish with copious explanatory notes and details
goes only to Shri Sagar but for whose thankful insistence, this work
would not have been feasible.

In the ultimate analysis, like any branch of learning, even
“Muhurta Jyotish" essentially aims at maximising human welfare: suc-
cess and happiness. And for achieving this goal, "Muhurta Jyotish"
deals with a system to determine specific epochs of time (or
muhurtas) which would ensure successful and happy endings of vari-
ous activities or events of life if initiated during those auspicious pe-
riods. Thus Muhurta Jyotish can help identify more favourable peri-
ods for starting and successful operation of various significant activi-
ties of life including business, industry, government or other social
institutions or functions like marriage, journey or even starting the
medical treatment of an ailment.

in this backdrop, “Muhurta Chintamani" a 16th century text,
was chosen for translation as, it is by far the most exhaustive and
time-tested treatise on the subject. Its author shri Acharya Ram as
well as his nephew shri Govind Daivagya wrote two separate com-
mentaries on this text. These commentaries have no doubt been very
useful, however, additional information and concepts have been pro-
fusely drawn from various sources including Ratnasar, Samarsar,
Vyavahar Tatwa, Raj Marland, Darvagya Manohar, Muhurta Parjat,
Narad Puran, Muhurta Deepak. Vivah Vrindavan. Jyotirveda Bhavnam
, 10 explain certain concepts and axioms in the book.

There is another aspect of this book which 1 must rcemphasize.
Though this text deals with “Louhurta", it has some very significant

and rare principles for use in predictive astrology making use of ihe
pentad: week day, date (vedic), star. ‘yoga’ and ‘karan’ besides plan-
etary movements through zodiac.

During the course of my presentation in the form of exhaus-
tive notes, tables or charts or other mathematical aids. adequate care
has been to ensure that original essence of the text is not lost or mis-
placed.

The author has chosen to present the whole text in thirteen (13)
sub-divisions concerning various portfolios of life to determine the
most auspicious muhurtas for marriage, journey and other socia! or
economic activities and events like starting business. industry, pro-
fession, service, investments, medicine or investments/market fluctua-
tions. Even chosing a town to setthe down has been given importance.

Whenever necessary remedial measures to dispel inauspicious
influences have been given.

I have made a very modest attempt in presenting this commen-
iary as | am aware of the fact that the book demand a2 very detailed
and Jucid explanations. In this exercise some ‘nouns’ have not been
translated to keep the original theme intact, though their English
equivalents have been slated in brackets. As and when felt necessary
even divergent views have been given to widen the understanding and
comprehension.

As I close, my heart goes to thank Prof. D.P. Chauhan but for
whose affection and devoted. attention, this book would not be in the
present form. I owe to him more than just a few words of forma!
gratitude. I must however, thank him for all he has done to improve
the language in many ways.

10/9/96 Girish Chand Sharma
91 Bhagwan Nagar.
Hapur(UP)

CHAPTER 1
AUSPICIOUS AND INAUSPICIOUS MUHURTAS

May Gajanana (the elephant mouthed god Ganesha, the son of
Shiva's consort Paravati) remove all the obstacles of my way. He is
adorned for a moment with the emergence of the second teeth or the
second tusk (Ganesha, the elephant mouthed god has only one teeth
or tusk) as he has thrown in sport by his hand into his mouth the
forepart of the Ketaki flower in the ear of Gauri or Paravati (she wore
the Ketaki flower in her ear as a jewel).

Notes: In all the books written in Sanskrit there has been the
tradition to offer obeisance for welfare at the beginning of the book
Here obeisance has been paid to Shri Ganesha. the son of Paravati.
Shiva’s consort Ganesha ji is endowed with the qualities of

2 Muhurta Chinta Mani

removing all the obstacles and ensuring successful completion of a
deed. The present book has been annotated in Sanskrit by Shri Govind
ji. The book is known by the name of Peeyushdhara (The Current
of Nectar). In Peeyushdhara may be seen the harmonization of Shr
Govind ji’s multifaceted knowledge of Shruti, Smriti, Purana, Nyaya,
Vyakarana, Mimamsa, Dharma Shastra and Astrology. According to
him the word  (Bhanga) in the shloka in '' is wrong
from the literary point of view and the expression should be

‘art’ would be better”, says Shri Govind ji. He has said so with a
view to observing the rules of literature. But there will be no differ-
ence in meaning due to the word  or

Rama, the son of Ananta Daivagya, for the successful comple-
tion of rites (conception, christening, first caling of grains ctc.) and
deeds (trading, buying and selling, travelling etc.) for the proper
knowledge of Muhurtas (elections of time etc.) and for expressing in
brief the decp mcaning of things, composed this book named
‘Muhurta Chintamani’.

Notes: Indian thinkers hold the view that a deed begun at a
favourable moment will confer favourable results. Therefore Astrolo-
ey has made provision for thc Muhurtas for the various rites like
those of conception. chnstcning (naming). child's first cating of grains.
making hole in the car ( ), beginning of education. beginning
of teaching letters. keeping fast, marriage, yajna etc. and the various
deeds like travelling. construction of ncw house. making jewels tak-
ing loan. ploughing. sowing secds. cutting crops etc. it is for the
proper knowledge of these that Ramacharva has composed the hook
named Muhurta Chintamani
LORDS OF TITHIS Lord: af: '

3 Auspicious and Inauspicious Muhurtas

etc. are respectively Agni, Brahma, Gauri, Ganesha, Sarpa, Kartikeya,
Surya (the Sun). Shiva, Durga, Yamaraja, Vishwe Deva, Vishnu,
Kamadeva, Shiva and Chandrama (the moon).

Notes: On full Amavasya (the 30th Hindi Tithi) the longitude
of the sun and that of the moon are the same. At the end of
Amavasya the moon, duc to her fast movement, goes towards the East
from the sun and in this way, the moon's being away from the sun
by 12°, each time there is onc tithi Pratipada etc. that is, when the
moon goes away from 0° to 12° from the sun, there is the Pratipada
tithi of the Shukla Paksha (the bright half of the month). The rest of
the tithis are formed by the moon’s going away from the sun by 12°
each time. When the distance between the sun and the moon is 180°
there is the full disc of the moon in the sky It is because of this
that the 1Sth tithi is named as ‘Poornima® or ‘Paurnamasi'. Afier
‘Poornima’ (Full moon tithi) when 15 tithis are completed. the dis-
tance between the sun and the moon again becomes 0° and this day
is the day of Amavasya (full dark tithi). It brings an cnd of the
month

In the present Shloka are stated the lords of the tithis. The lords
of the tithis in both the Pakshas (forinights) - the bright half and the
dark half - are the same. The first tithi of the bright half of the month
is called Pratipada. In Indian Panchangas (Almanacs) it is indicated
by the figure 1 (one) and in the same way the other tithis are also
represented by figures or numbers 1, 2, 3 and so on. Poornima has
been allotted the number of 15. The tithis after Poomima are not rep-
resenicd by the numbers of 16, 17 and so on. they are again started
with Number ! (onc) and are represented by numbers |, 2. 3 and so
on Only the Amavasva of the month is represented by the number
of 30.

4 Muhurta Chinta Mani

2. Dwitiya Brahma (the creator of the universe)

3. Tritiya Gauri (Lord Shiva’s consort)

. Chaturthi Ganesha (Parwati’s son)

7. Saptami The Sun (He is also a living god)

meval energy)

The worship, fast and the establishing of the image with life
(in Sanskrit   - Prana Pratishtha) of the gods - these are
to be done on the tithis of which they are the Lords or presiding
deities. Thus, if one wants to worship Ganesha. it should be done
on the 4th tithi the lord or presiding deity of which is Ganesha and
$0 on.

5 Auspicious and Inauspicious Muhurtas

‘NANDA’ ETC. NAMES OF TITHIS AND SIDDHA YOGA:
In both the bright half of the month and the dark half of the month,
Nanda, Bhadra, Jaya, Rikta and Poorna (in three frequencies) are re-
spectively the names of the tithis. The three frequencies of the bright
half of the month are respectively inauspicious, medium in auspicious-
ness and fully auspicious and; the three frequencies of the dark half
of the month are regarded respectively fully auspicious, medium in
auspiciousness and inauspicious. If, in both the bright and the dark
half of the month, the tithis Nanda etc. in order (of all the three fre-
quencies) happen to fall on Friday, Wednesday, Tuesday, Saturday
and Thursday, then on that day there is the Siddha Yoga.

Notes: In the 15 tithis of the bright half and the dark half of
the month, 1/6/11 are Nandas, 2/7/12 are Bhadras, 3/8/13 are Jayas,
4/9/14 are Riktas and 5/10/15 are Poormas (in the Dark half of the
month the Pooras are 5/10/30.

In the bright half of the month the tithis Pratipada (1),
Dwitiya (2), Tritiya (3), Chaturthi (4) and Panchmi (5) have been said
10 be inauspicious, Here inauspiciousness or auspiciousness depends
upon the decreasing or the increasing of the moon. The moon disap-
pears completely in the night of the Amavasya (30 tithi} and in the
nights of the 1/2/3/4/5 tithis she is seen to be slowly increasing.
Therefore owing to the weakening of the moon these tithis of the
bright half of the month which are of the first frequency are said to
be inauspicious. The second frequency of the tithis of the bright half
of the month, that is 6/7/8/9/10 witness increase in the kalas of the
moon, The strength of the moon increases to a certain degree. There-
fore, these tithis are neither auspicious nor inauspicious or they are
medium in auspiciousness. In the 3rd frequency of the tithis of the
bright half of the month, that is 11/12/13/14/15 the moon is endowed
with all the kalas and therefore these tithis have been spoken of as
auspicious.

On the other hand in the dark half of the month in the first
frequency of the tithis, that is, 1/2/3/4/5 the moon decreases slowly.
But she is scen in her greater number of kalas. Therefore the tithis
of the first frequency of the dark half of the month that is 1/2/3/4/5
have been said to be fully auspicious. in the 2nd frequency of the
tithis of the dark half of the month 6/7/8/9/10 the moon gradually
decreases in strength, and so these tithis have been said to be medi-
um in auspiciousness. And in the 3rd frequency of the tithis of the

6 Muhurta Chinta Mani

dark half of the month, the moon’s being completely devoid of
strength, therefore thesc tithis 11/12/13/14/15 have been said to be
inauspicious.

If the Nanda tithis (1/6/11) happen to fall on Friday, Bhadra
tithis (2/7/12) on Wednesday. Jaya tithis (3/8/13) on Tuesday, Rikta
tithis (4/9/14) on Saturday and Poorna tithis (5/£0/15) on Thursday,
then on that day there happens to be Siddha Yoga. This Yoga is
counted among the most auspicious yogas and it is regarded as suc-
cess giving in one’s efforts.

Brahma rishi Vashistha has said that as the Ekadashi tithi (11)
destroys all the sins if on this tithi one keeps fast, gives alms, per-
forms worship, listens to the religious stories, performs the comple-
lion rites of some fast '! etc., in the same way the above men-
tioned Siddha tithis confer auspicious effects by destroying the de-
fects rclating to Muhurtas such as those of Masa Shunya (zero
month), Masa Dagdha (bunt month).

Inauspicious day such as the day of Saturday happening to fall
on the inauspicious tithi of Rikta becomes a day of Siddha Yoga and
confers auspicious effects.

7 Auspicious and Inauspicious Muhurtas

AMRITA YOGA AND DAGDHA YOGA ON DAYS SUN-
DAY ETC. If on days Sunday, Monday and so on there happen to
fall respectively Nanda, Bhadra, Nanda. Jaya. Rikta, Bhadra and
Poorna, then there is Amrita Yoga. In the same way. if there hap-
pens to fall the Nakshtra of Bharani on Sunday, Chitra on Monday,
Utiara shadha on Tuesday. Dhanishtha on Wednesday. Uttara phalguni
on Thursday, Jyeshtha on Friday and Rewali on Saturday. Daghda
Yoga happens to be on that day

Notes: If it happens that there 1s the Nanda tith (1/6/11) on
Sunday. Bhadra tithi (2/7/12) on Monday, Nanda tithi (1/6/11) on
Tuesday, Jaya tithi (3/8/13) on Wednesday. Rikta uithi 4/9/14 on
Thursday, Bhadra tithi (2/7/12) on Friday and Poorna tith: (3/10/15
or 5/10/30) on Saturday. there is formed on these davs what 15 called
Amrita Yoga. {According to Shn Govind ji Devarisht Narada‘s shloka:

should be interpreted in this way “Tf at happens that there is the
Sanda Tithe on Sunday. Bhadra  on Mondays Nanda Tithi on
Tuesday. Jaya Tithi on Wednesday. Rikta Tuhi on Thursday. Bhadra
Titht on Friday and Poorna Tithi on Saturday. then Amrita Yoga is
formed. this ts the meaning’)

But certain writers have interpreted that these davs are Mrita
Yoga forming if these Tithis fall on them Actually. they have wrong-
ly mterpreted the expression   «The meaning of the
expression should be this - If Poorna uthi falls on Saturday. the
yoga formed will be Amiita The expression wher 1 15 analysed 1s

9 Auspicious and Inauspmeious Muhurtas

KARKACHA AND SAMVARTAKA YOGA: If Shashthi etc
tithis in right order of tithis fall on Saturday etc in reverse order of
days, there is Adhama Yoga (Krakacha) on these days. There is
Adhama (Samvartaka) yoga also if Pratipada (1) falls on Wednesday
and Saptami (7) falis on Sunday. And teeth should not be cleaned
with Neem stick etc on the Shashthi (6) etc. Tithis.

Notes: If it happens that the Shashthi (6) tithi falls on Satur-
day, Saptami (7) on Friday. Astami (8) on Thursday, Navami (9) on
Wednesday, Dashmi (10) on Tuesday, Ekadashi (11) on Monday and
Dwadashi (12), on Sunday, the yoga formed on these days is not aus-
picious and good. Shri Govind ji (the writer’s brother) has given this
yoga the name of ‘Karkacha yoga’. And the yoga that is formed if
Pratipada (1) happens to fall on Wednesday and Saptami (7) on Sun-
day has been given the name of ‘Samwartaka’. Teeth are not to be
cicaned with stick of any tree (such as Neem tree) in these yopas.

According to ‘Kashi Khanda Granatha’ collection of wood
should not be made on Pratipada (1), Amavasya (30), Shashthi (6)
and Navami (9) and on Sunday.

On Shashthi (6). Ashtmi (8), Chaturdashi (14), Amavasya (30)
tithis are prohibited respectively for oiling (the hair), eating flesh,
shaving and sexual intercourse with one's wife. Ubatan (paste made
of turmeric  used to cleanse the body) should not be applied on
the body on Trayodashi (13), Dashmi (10) and Dwitiya (2) and bath
should not be taken after rubbing Amla (a fruit) on the body on
Amavasya (30), Saptami (7) and Navami (9).

Notes: Anti-yogas (yogas which remove the evil effects of the
inauspicious yogas) are also there in this connection.

That is. if the Shashthi 1ithi (6) falis on Saturday oil may be
applied on the body. flesh may be eaten on the Durgashtami (8) of
the month of Ashwina (coincident with October). shaving may be

10 Muhurta Chinta Mani

done on Chaturdashi at a place of pilgrimage and sexual intercourse
may be indulged in on the Amavasya of Decpawali (one of the four
chief festivals of the Hindus falling on the 30th  of the Hindi
month of Kartika)

One more fact is to be kept in mind here that the tithi which
femains on that day for two or three hours after Sunrise is not the
one on which the above said things should not be done. Thus. sup-
pose the Shashthi tthi (6) remains (on Saturday) only for 2 hours
after the Sunrise, then oil may be applied on the body 2 hours after
Sunrise.

DAGDHA, VISHA, HUTASHANA AND YAMAGHANTA
YOGAS. If Dwadashi tithi (12) happens to fall on Sunday, Ekadashi
(11) on Monday, Panchami (5) on Tuesday, Tritiva (3) on Wednes-
day. Shashthi (6) on Thursday. Ashtami (8) on Fridav and Navami
(9) on Saturday, the yoga formed on these days is Dagdha. Again.
if Chaturthi tithi (4) happens to fall on Sunday, Shashthi (6) on Mon-
day, Saptam: (7) on Tucsday. Dwitiya (2) on Wednesday. Ashtami
(8) on Thursday, Navami (9) on Friday and Saptami (7) on Satur-
day. the yoga formed on these davs 15 calied “Visha’. [n the same
way. if the Dwadashi titht (12) happens to fall on Sunday. Shashihi
(6) on Monday, Saptami (7) on Tuesday. Ashtami (8) on Wednes-
day. Navami (9) on Thursday. Dashmi (19) on Friday and Ekadashi
(11) on Saturday, the yoga formed on these days is called “Hutashana™
yoga. Similarly, if the Makha Nakshtra happens to be there on Sun-
day. Vishakha on Monday. Aridra on Tuesday. Moola on Wednes-
day. Kntika on Thursday. Rohini on Friday and Hasta on Saturday.
the yoga formed on these 0955 in these Nakshtras 1 Yama Ghanta

11 Ausprcious and Inausperous Muhurtas

ZERO TITHIS OF CHAITRADI MASAS or MONTHS IN
BOTH THE PAKSHAS (The bright and the dark half of the month):-
Pratipada and Dwitiya (1 and 2) tithis in the month of Bhadrapada
(August). Dwitiya and Tritiva (2 and 3) in Shravana (August).
Dwadasht (12) in Baisakha (April), Chaturthi and Panchami (4, 5) in
Pausha (December) Sapiami and Ashtami (7, 8) in Margashirsa (De-
cermber) and Ashtami and Navami (8. 9) in Chaitra (March) are piven
the name of Zero tithis in both the Pakshas.

In the dark half of the month of Kanika (November) Ashadha
(July). Phalguna (February) Jyeshtha (May) and Magha (January) the
Panchami, tithi (5), the Shashthi (6), the Chaturthi (4) the Chaturdashi
(14) and Panchami (5) are respectively the zero tithis and so are
respectively the Chaturdashi (14), the Saptami (7), the Tritiya (3), the
Trayodashi (13) and the Shashthi (6) in the bright half of the month.

Notes: Shradhdha (the offering of food and water to the manes
on certain days as prevalent among the Hindus) may be done in the
Zcro tithis, bul no auspicious deed should be performed on these
uthis. The doing of auspicious deeds brings destruction of the fami-
iv and also of money,

Masas (Months) are of four kinds Solar months. Lunar months,
Sidercul months and Savana months. The Solar months are determined
according to the transit ime of Sun's entry into 12 signs (like 81105.
Taurus cic) cach of an angular distance of 30°. Lunar month is the
time period from completion of new Moon (conjunction of Moon with
the Sun) to the next ncw-Moon There are two kinds of Lunar months
used in India The new Moon ending and the full Moon ending. The
new Moon endiay famnar Month covers the period from one new-
Moon to the newt This as Known as amanta (Amavassal or Sukitad)
system. The day next 0 Annas se the first dav of the month The
full-Moon ending Luna: month kaown as Purnimanta covers che
period from  full Moon tu the nest and beers from the dav after
“yh 4oor,

12 Muhurta Chinta Mani

Vikram Samvat begins from the first tithi of Chaitra Shukla
(Shukla Pratipada) according to the Chaitradi system, and the Vikrama
Samvat started from 57 B.C.

The Lunar month which begins (on the Pratipada of the bright
half of the month) when the Sun is in the sign of Pisces and which
ends (on the Amant or Amavasya) when the Sun is in the sign of
Aries is called the ‘Shuddha’ (pure) Chaitra (March) Masa or month.
The names of pure months are as follows:-

1, Chaitra 5. Shravana 9 Marga Shirsa
2. Vaisakha 6. Bhadrapada 10. Pausa

3. Jyeshtha 7. Asvina 11. Magha

4. Ashadha 8. Kartika 12. Phalguna

As long as the number of Aries Sankranti etc in a Solar year
(12 Solar months) is equal to the number of Amanta (Amavasya),
the months are considered to be pure months. When in a year the
number of Amantas becomes greater than the number of Sankrantis,
the additional month is called ‘the extra’ month or ‘Mala’ month.
Similarly, when in a Solar year the amanta number becomes fewer
than the Sankranti number, the one month which is fewer is calied
the “Kshaya’ month.

The difference between the two sunrises is called one savana
day. 30 Savana days make one Savana month and 12 Savana months
make one Savana year.

The ume between the nse of a Nakshtra or star in the horizon
and another nse of it on the next day is called one Sidereal day. 30
such Sidereal days make one Sidereal month and 12 Siderea) months
make one Sidereal year.

In this, book the ‘amanta’ or the Sukhladi system has been re-
garded as the base of calculation. Where some other system will be
used, it will be separately indicated

13 Auspicious and Inauspicious Muhurtas

INAUSPICIOUSNESS ARISING OUT OF THE CONJUNC-
TION OF TITHI AND NAKSHTRA: And in the same way if it hap-
pens that the Ashlesha Nakshtra fails on the Dwadashi Tithi, the
Uttarashadha on the Pratipada (1), Anuradha on the Dwitiya (2),
Makha on the Panchami (5), the three Uttaras (Uttara Phalguni, Uttara
Shadha and Uttara Bhadrapada) on the Tritiya (3), Rohini on Ekadashi
(11), Swati and Chitra on Trayodashi (13), Hasta and Moola on
Saptami (7), Kritika on Navami (9), Poorva Bhadrapada on Ashtami
(8), and Rohini on Shashthi, then the tithi is inauspicious for per-
forming auspicious deeds.

ZERO NAKSHTRAS (STARS) IN THE MONTHS CHAITRA
ETC.: The Nakshtras (stars) Rohini and Ashwini in the month of
Chaitra, and Swati in Vaishakha, Uttara-shadha and Pushya in
Jyeshtha. Poorva Phalguni and Dhanishtha in Ashadha, Uttarashadha
and Shravana in Shravana, Shatabhisa and Rewati in Bhadrapada,
Poorvabhadrapada in Asvina, Kritika and Makha in Kartika. Chitra
and Vishakha in Marga shirsa. Aridra. Ashwini and Hasta in Pausha.
Shravana and Moola in Magha and Bharani and veshtha in Phalguna-
are called or named as Zero Nakshtras (stars) If an auspicious deed
is done in such Zero Nakshtras it causes loss of wealth.

14 Muhurta Chints Mani

ZERO SIGNS IN THE MONTHS CHAITRA ETC.: In the
months of Chaitra, Vaishakha, Jyeshtha, Ashadha, Shravana,
Bhadrapada, Ashvina, Kartika, Marga shirsa, Pausha, Magha and
Phalguna, the signs respectively of Aquarius, Pisces, Taurus, Gemini,
Aries, Virgo, Scorpio, Libra, Sagittarius, Cancer, Capricom and Leo
are zero signs.

COMBINATIONS WHICH ARE TO BE LEFT OUT IN DO-
ING ALL AUSPICIOUS DEEDS: The Panchami Tithi (5) with the
Hasta Nakshtra falling on Sunday, Saptami (7) with Ashwini on Tues-
day, Shashthi (6) with Mrigasira on Monday, Ashtami (8) with
Anuradha on Wednesday, Dashmi (10) with Rewati on Friday,
Navami (9} with Pushya on Thursday and Ekadashi (11) with Rohini
on Saturday-these combinations are to be avoided in doing all auspi-
cious deeds.

Notes: if there is the conjunction of only the Nakshtra and the
day, it is auspicious and should be adopted in doing all auspicious
deeds; but it becomes inauspicious if there is the conjunction of the
above stated tithis. This yoga is referred to as Madhu Sarpisha yoga.
Maharishi Vashistha has spoken of another kind of Madhu Sarpisha
yoga which is named as ‘Halahala’ yoga. According to Vashistha the
conjunction of Sunday-Panchami-Kritika, Monday-Dwitiya Chitra,
Tuesday-Poornima-Rohini, Wednesday-Saptami-Bharani, Thursday-
Trayodashi-Anuradha and Saturday-Ashtami-Rewati-makes what is
called the inauspicious yoga of Halahala.

METHOD OF FINDING OUT THE ANANDA ETC. yogas: If
Ananda etc. yogas are to be known on Sunday count from Ashwini
to the Nakshtra on Sunday. if these are to be known on Monday
court from Mrigshira to the Nakshtra on Monday. if on Tuesday.
trom Ashlesha, on Wednesday from Hasta. on Thursday. from
Anuradha. on Friday from Uttarashadha and. on Saturday from
Shatbhisa (Abhijit also).

Notes: It may be better understood in the following way. If
there 1s the mse of Ashwini Nakshtra on Sunday the first soga will
be the Ananda yoga. And if there is the nse of the Bharam Nakshtra
on Sunday the yoga formed on this day will be Kala-Danda. In this
way the rise of various Nakshtras on Sunday will be responsible for
making the various 28 yogas (Nakshtras arc 28 when Abhijit Nakshtra
is also included) If we have to see the 0245 (combinations) of Mon-

of Aridra on Monday, the yoga formed on this day will be Kala-
Danda, if of Punarvasu, the yoga formed on this day wil! be
Dhoomra. Other yogas will be formed in the same way due to the
rise of different Nakshtras on Monday. The 28 yogas will be formed
on all the Seven days of the week due to the rise of different 28
Nakshtras on these days.

Here is an example for more clarification. Suppose there is the
rise of the Vishakha Nakshtra on Thursday. To know what will be
the yoga on this day we shall count from the Anuradha Nakshtra to
the Vishakha Nakshtra. The number comes to be 28. Therefore. the
conjunction of Vishakha Nakshtra and Thursday will form the 28th
yoga which is Pravardhamana yoga.

THINGS TO BE REJECTED IN DOING ALL THE AUSPIi-
CIOUS DEEDS: The Lagna or the Ascendant and the Navamsha oc-
cupied by the Moon and the malefic planets (the emaciated Moon,
the Sun, Mars, and Saturn), the third part of a Ghati or 20 Palas (4
minutes) before and after mid day and mid night-these are to be re-
jected in doing all the auspicious deeds. Similarly, the Navamsha of
a malefic planet, three days before an eclipse, seven days after a por-
tent (like an earthquake etc.) and an eclipse and the whole day on
which an auspicious portent occurs—these should also be avoided in
doing, all auspicious deeds. And the Nakshtra cleaved by planets, the
Nakshtra in which there is the planetary war and the Nakshtra in
which there is some Utpaata or portent—these three Nakshtras are
to be rejected for six months in doing all the auspicious deeds.

Notes: The Sun, Mars, Saturn, Rahu, Ketu, emaciated Moon and
Mercury together with a matefic planet, are regarded as maiefic
planets.

Reversal in natural events is called a portent.

be
tw

Portents are of three types:
1. Celestial Portents,

2. Atmospheric Portents, and
3. Terrestrial Portents.

In Shloka 485 Chapter 46 of ‘Brihat Samhita’ the writer speaks
of portents in these words: “The celestial portents are the unnatural
behaviour of planets and asterisms, meteors, thunders, storms and ha-
los, the atmospheric ones are arcrial cities, rainbows etc and; the ter-
restrial ones arise from the moving and the Stationery objects.

Those portents which are generated by the peculiar nature of
the seasons and do not lead to evil consequences are called auspi-
cious portents, According to ‘Brihat Samhita’ the auspicious portents
in different seasons are as follows:

1. Spring (Basant) seasons (Constituted by the Lunar months
of Chaitra and Vaishaka or roughly March and April): Thunder-bolts.
lightning, carth tremor, bright glare at twilight, sounds of foud thun-
der, halos, dust, smoke in forests, the Sun's being crimson at the time
of rising and setting, the exudation of food, sweet juice, oil and an

23 Auspicious and Inauspicious Muhurtas

over-abundant yield of flowers and fruits on trecs and increased sen-
suality on the part of bulls and birds.

2. Summer (Greesham) season (constituted by Jyeshtha and
Ashadha, roughly May and June): The sky tarnished by shooting stars
and falling meteors, the orbs of the luminaries (the Sun and the
Moon) being tawny, blaze and explosion without fire, smoke dust and
strong wind filling the sky, the twilight red like red lotus, the sky
looking like the ocean with surging waves and the drying up of the
waters of the river.

3. Rainy (Varsha) season {constituted by Shravana and Bhado,
roughly July and August): Rainbow halos, lightning, sprouting of
withered trees, tremor, upheaval, unnatural appearances, rumbling and
splitting of the earth, rising, flowing upwards and flooding of lakes,
rivers and wells respectively and falling off of mountains and houses.

4. Autumn (sharad) season (constituted by Ashwina and Kartika,
roughly September and October). The sight of celestial damsels, spir-
its, Gand harvas, aerial Chariots, marvellous things, and of planets,
stars and constellations in the day time in the sky, sounds of singing
and musical instruments in the forests and on mountain plateaus, in-
crease of crops and diminution of water.

5. DEWY (Hemant) season (constituted by Marga shirsha and
Pausha, roughly November and December): Cold wind, frost, cries
of birds and beasts, sight of demons yakshas and such other invis-
ible beings, aerial voices being heard, the quarters along with the sky,
forests and mountains getting darkened with smoke and the high ris-
ing and setting of the Sun.

6. Winter (Shishira) Season (Constituted by Magha and
Phalguna, roughly January and February): Snow fall, unnatural winds,
appearance of deformed and super human beings. the sky looking like
dark coltyrium, and colourful with the fall of stars and meteors. wom-
en. cows, goats horses, beasts and birds grving birth to freakish voung
ones and unnatural changes in leaves, sprouts and creepers.

In the countnes or regions in which these auspicious portents
happen naturally, where these happen daily. they cannot be called
portents. Thus in the hilly countries or regions the falling of snow
should not be called an auspicious portent.
The cleaving of a Nakshtra: When. moving in his own orbit or

24 Muburta Chinta Mani

sphere, the celestial latitude of a planet gets in the same direction
with the celestial latitude of a Nakshtra, the planet is said to be cleav-
ing that Nakshtra. Such a Nakshtra is also to be rejected.

Planetary war: When the planets move in the sky along their
orbits lying over one another, they appear to our eyes to move on
one surface or plane as a result of their great distance from the earth.
According to the degree of their seeming approachment there are four
kinds of war. 1. Bheda (occultation, cleaving) 2. Ullekha (grazing)
3. Amsumardana (clashing of the rays) 4. Apsavva (passing south
ward). When two planets appear to have a single orb on account of
their apparent over lapping the fight is called Bheda. In this the orb
of the lower planet abstracts completely the upper one. When the mms
of the two orbs graze each other, it is called (7609. When the rays
of the two planets situated closely appear te be clashing with onc
another it is Amsumardana. When a planct covering another moves
towards the south it is Apasavya.

THE NAKSHTRAS IN WHICH ECLIPSES OCCUR: The
Nakshtra in which there is the full eclipse of the Sun is to be reject-
ed for six months, for 3 months if there is the half eclipse. for one
month if there is the one fourth part eclipse. If the eclipsed Sun or
Moon sets then three days before the eclipse, and if the eclipsed Sun
or Moon rises then three days afler it, no auspicious deed is to be
done. And if there is the half eclipse at other time than the time of
rising or setting then 3 days before and 3 days after and the full dav
of the eclipse, should be avoided for doing all auspicious deeds

DETAILS OF BHADRA: in the Shukla Paksha or the bright
half of the month there is Bhadra on the 8th and i5th tithis in the
first half part of the tithi; and Bhadra is there also on the 4th and
the ith tithis in the latter haif part of these tithis. Again, in the
Krishna Paksha or the Dark half of the month there is Bhadra on
the 3rd and the 10th tithis in the latter half part of these tithis; and
Bhadra is there also on the 7th and the i4th tithis in the first half
part of these tithis.

Notes: Bhadra is the name of a ‘Karana’. Karana is the half of
the tithi-maana, that is, there are two karanas in a tithi, Karanas are
of two kinds the first is movable and the second is immovable. 1.
Bava, 2. Balava, 3. Kaulava, 4. Taitil, 5. Gara, 6. Vanija, and 7,
Bhadra (Vishti is another name of Bhadra). These seven Karanas oc-
cur four times in every Paksha (half part of the month). Due to their
four times occurrence, they are called chara or movable. 1, Shakuni,
2, Nag, 3. Chatuspada and 4. Kinstughana-these four karanas are fixed
karanas. There is Shakuni in the latter half of the 14th uthi of the
Krishna Paksha, there are respectively Naga and Chatushpada in the
first and the Second half of the 30th tithi (Amavasya) of the Krishna
Paksha or the Dark half of the month: and there is Kinstughana in

3? Muhuna Chinta Maru

the first half of the Pratipada (the first uthi) of the Shukla Paksha or
the Bright half of the month. These Karanas do not have their oc-
currence in the other tithis. Therefore they are called Sthir Karanas
or fixed Karanas.

Vf afier knowing the full maana or value of a tithi  is divided

and there is Chatushpada im the 2nd half of this titht. and there is
Kinstughana in the first half of Proupada (the [st Hindi tthi) in the
Shukla Paksha These four are the fixed Karanas Then there 15 Bava
Karana in the second half of the Pratipada of the Shukla Paksha. there
is Balava Karana in the first half of the 2nd uthi. there is Kaulava
karana in the second half of this (the 2nd) uthi. there is Tattil Karana
in the first half and Gara in the second half of the Third uth, and
then there is Vanika in the first half and Bhadra or Vishti in the sec-
ond half of the fourth tithi. These seven are the movable karanas. ,
Then from the Panchami tithi to the Saptami tithi (the Sth and the
7th dina: sahis respectively) of the bright half of the month  "| pass
the © Karanas from Bava to Vanika and then there will be the Bhadra

Again. 0 Karanas from Bava to Vanika will pass from the second
half of the Ashtami tithi to the first half of the Ekadashi tithi (the

In ‘Ratna kosha’ there are given the names of the Bhadra -
Karana and the effects are similar to the names These names respec-
tively are: (1) Hansi (2) Nandini (3) Trishira (4) Sumukhi (5) Karalika
(6) Vaikriti (7) Raudramukhi and (8) Chaturmukin. It means that the
above quoted names of Bhadra are to be supposed in the first half
and the second half of the tithis mentioned in the Shloka. For exam-
ple, the name of the Bhadra of the first half of the Ashtami tithi will
be Hansi and the name of the Bhadra of the first half of the Purniina
uthi (the 15th Hindi tithi) will be Nandini. The nares of Bhadra are
to be understood in this order.

DETERMINING THE MOUTH AND TAIL OF BHADRA;
AND ITS EFFECTS: in the bright half of the month on the above
said Bhadra tithis (8th, 15th, 4th and 11th tithis) in the night order
of the tithis that is 4th, 8th, 11th and 15th tithis respectively 5 Ghatis
at the beginning of the Sth, 2nd, 7th and 4th Prahara; and in the dark
half of the month on the above said Bhadra tithis (3rd, 10th, 7th and
the 14th tithis) in the right order of the tithis, that is 3rd, 7th, 10th
and 14th tithis respectively 5 Ghatis at the beginning of the 8th, 3rd,
6th and Ist Prahara, there is the mouth of Bhadra. This Mouth is in-
auspicious. In the same way in the bright half of the month in the
above said tithi order, the 3 ghatis at the beginning of the 8th, 5th,
6th and 3rd Prahara; and in the dark half of the month on the above
said Bhadra tithis, in the right order of tithis 3 Ghatis at the begin-
ning of the 7th, 2nd, Sth and 4th Prahara, there is the tail of Bhadra.
This tail is auspicious. If the Bhadra of the last half of the tithi is in
the day and that of the first half of the tithi is in the night, it is aus-
picious.

Notes: The 8th part of the ful] maana or value of a tithi is
called ‘Prahara’. 5 Ghatis mouth and 3 Ghatis tail-+this has been writ-
ten afier supposing 60 ghatis as the full maana or value of a tithi. If
the full maana or value of a given tithi is less or more than 60 ghatis,
then the mouth and tail are to be known by proportional mathemat-
ics. Here is an example. Suppose the full Maana or value of the
Chaturthi tithi (the 4th Hindi tithi) of the bright half of the month is
55 ghatis and 54 Palas or 3354 Palas. Then the ghatis, Palas and
Vipalas of the mouth of Bhadra on this tithi will be known by this
formula.

35 Auspicious and Inauspicious Muhurtas

Therefore on the Chaturthi tithi of the bright half of the month
the 4 ghatis 39 palas and 30 vipalas (4/39/30) of the Sth Prahara there
will be the mouth of Bhadra, that is, there will be the Mouth ghatis
of Bhadra from 27 ghatis 57 palas 10 32 ghatis 36 palas and 20
vipalas

If the full maana or value of a tithi is divided by 8, the quo-
tient will be the value of the passed Prahara at a given time.

Tithi Maana~+ 8 = Quotient + Remainder + 8 = passed Prahara,
Passed Prahara - 1 = the present Prahara.

In the following shloka of ‘Ratna Mala’ there is a very fine
method of the existence of the Mouth ghati in the 5th etc Prahara of
the tithis Chaturthi etc (the 4th Hindi tithi etc.) and of the existence
of the Tail ghati in the 8th etc Prahara of these tithis.

The order of the mouth of Bhadra in the tithis Chaturthi (the
4th Hindi tithi) etc..as as follows: The mouth of Bhadra is in the 5
ghatis of the beginning of the Sth Prahara (the number of the West
direction), 2nd (the number of the Agni Kona), 7th (the number of
the North direction), 4th (the number of Nairiritya Kona), 8th (the
number of the Ishaan Kona), 3rd (the number of the South direction),
Sth (the number of the Vayavya kona) and [st (the number of the
East direction) Prahara There is the tail of Bhadra in the 3 ghatis of
the ending of the 4th Prahara from the Prahara of a tithi in which
there is its mouth.

THE LIVING AND EFFECTS OF BHADRA: When the Moon
is in the signs of Aquarius, Pisces, Cancer or Leo Bhadra lives in
the Mrityu Loka (that is on the Earth); when she (the Moon) is in
the signs of Anes, Taurus, Gemini, or Scorpio, Bhadra lives in the
Swargaloka (the abode of gods) and when she is in the signs of Vir- |
go, Sagittarius, Libra and Capricorn Bhadra tives in the Patal Loka
(the Hades or the Underworld). The effects of Bhadra are felt in the
Loka (world) in which it lives.

THE THINGS WHICH SHOULD NOT BE DONE IN THE
COMBUST ETC. STAGES OF JUPITER AND VENUS: When Ju-
piter and Venus are in old age, in the combustion stage or in child-
hood stage and when there is the “Kshaya’ (the month that has not
occurred) Month or the Malla (additional month) Month, the follow-
ing things should not be performed, the building and the using of a
small well, garden, tank, well and house: the beginning and ending
of a vrata (fast), coming in of a newly wedded wife, Mahadaana,
some yajna, Ashtaka Shraddha, Godan (the Keshanta Samskara of a
Brahmachari) ealing newly grown grains, water tank, the first
Shrawanikarma, Vedavrata, Neelvrashotsarga, Atipanna Samaskara (the
jat Karmadi Samskaras of children which have not been performed
in proper time), Deva Pratishtha (performing the life giving ceremony
of a god) learning the first lesson from the guru (teacher), putting
on the Sacred thread, marriage, the first cutting of hair, the first vis-
it to a god or the first going to a place of pilgrimage, renunciation
of the world, Agnihotra (or burning the sacred fire), the first visit to
a king. coronation, journey, yajna of four months, Samavartana. pierc-
ing the ear and the testing of some celestial objects.

Notes: Jupiter and Venus are said to be old before they are
combust and they are in their infancy after rising.

THE DEFECTIVE PERIOD WHEN JUPITER IS THERE IN
THE SIGN OF LEO OR CAPRICORN. OR WHEN HE IS RETRO-
GRADE OR ATICHARI OR WHEN HE COMBINES WITH THE
SUN: All the deeds thai should not be performed when Jupiter is

38 Muhurta Chinta Mani

combust (these are described in Shiokas 46 and 47) should also be
left out (postponed) when Jupiter is in the sign of Leo or Capricorn.
According to some learned Astrologers all these deeds are also to be
left out when Jupiter is retrograde and is Atichari. In the same way
all these deeds are to be left out during the period when Jupiter and
the Sun remain in the same sign or when a Paksha (fortnight or one
half of the month) is of 13 days. During these periods things made
of ivory or elephant teeth (tusk) and jewels having gems studded in
them are not to be got made and also not to be worn.

Notes: The remaining of Jupiter for one year in one sign with
his medium or average speed is called Samvatsar. If Jupiter moves
into the next sign before the Samivatsar is completed with his spashta
or true speed, that is called Atichaar.

The month in which there is not the occurrence of two tithis
{this is called the Kshava of a tithi) has a paksha of 13 days.

LEAVING OUT THE PERIOD IN WHICH JUPITER IS IN
THE SIGN OF LEO IN THREE WAYS: First, when Jupiter is there
in the sign of Leo and he is also in the Navamsha of Leo, marriage
etc. are not to be performed. (This Navamsha, of Leo extends from
4-13". 20' to 4 -16°-40"). Auspicious deeds like marriage ete are to
be  out not at other places than al those that are there from the
Norther Bank of Godavari to the Southern Bank of the Northern
Ganga (that is. the regions of Central India, Madhya Bharata and
Rajasthan). If the Sun is in the sign of Aries. then there is no defect
of Jupiter's situation in the sign of Leo.

Notes Geographically Rajasthan Gujarat. Madhya Pradesh. some
Northern pace of Maharashtra. and some Southern Paris of Unissa.
Unarapradesh and Bihar fall in between the Ganga and the Godavan

The feaving out of the defectr.e penod when the Sun ts in the
sign of Aries is confirmed in Jyourtibandha’ aiso

39 Auspicious and Inauspicious Muhurtas

The girl married when the Sun is in the sign of Aries or when
Jupiter is in the sign of Leo is of an excellent conduct, is truthful
and is endowed with jewels, diamonds happiness and fame.

DETERMINATION OF THE DEFECTS ARISING OUT OF
JUPITER’S SITUATION IN THE SIGN OF LEO: The period in
which Jupiter is situated in the sign of Leo is to be rejected in all
countries (regions) in the five quarters from Magha Nakshtra, that is,
four quarters of the Magha Nakshtra and one quarter of
Poorvaphalguni. This period is not defective and not to be rejected
in the remaining four quarters (that is 3 quarters of Poorvaphalguni
and one of Uttara Phalguni Nakshtra) except in the region between
the rivers Ganga and Godawari. But if the Sun is in the sign of Ari-
es in this region also (the region between Ganga and Godawari) mar-
riage and engagement etc are auspicious. The period when Jupiter is
in the sign of Leo should be rejected while performing auspicious
deeds in all places in the region of Kalinga (on the Southern Bank
of the river Vaitarni of Unissa and on the Northern Bank of the riyv-
er Krishna to the regions of Raj Mahendri Vishakhapattana and
Ganjaam district) Gaur (from Madhya or Central Bengal to the North-
ern limit of Urissa or Bardwana) and in that of Gurjars or Gujarat.
(In these regions the period when Jupiter is in the sign of Leo is not
to be rejected even when the Sun is in the sign of Aries).

Notes: 1 is concluded from the above Shlokas that

1, Jupiter in the sign of Leo and also in the Navamsha of Leo
1S to be rejected in all places for performing auspicious deeds.

2. Auspicious deeds like marriage etc may be performed in the
regions to the North of river Ganga and to the South of river
Godawari when Jupiter is in the sign of Leo

3. In auspicious deeds like marriage etc even Juptter in the sign

40 Muhurta Chinta Mani

of Leo and also in the Navamsha of Leo is auspicious when the Sun
is in Aries.

It is the opinion of some leamed Astrologers that Jupiter in the
sign of Leo is defective and should be rejected only in the year in
which there is the Magha Nakshtra on the Pauranmasi in the month
of Magha.

AVOIDANCE OF THE PERIOD FOR PERFORMING AUS-
PICIOUS DEEDS WHEN JUPITER IS IN THE SIGN OF CAPRI-
CORN OR WHEN HE 15 IN HIS FALL: In the region to the east
of the river Reva (or Narmada which flows in the regions of Jabalpur
etc.), to the west of the river Gandaki (which rises from the
Himalayas and joins the river Ganga near Patna city of the State of
Bihar after flowing to the west of Nepal: this river is called by dif-
ferent names in different places such as at some places it is called
Narayani, at others Shaaigrami and at others Gandaki), to the north
and the south of the river Sonbhadra (this river joins the river Ganga
at Patna) auspicious deeds may be performed when Jupiter is in his
fall or he is in the sign of Capricorn, the performance of such deeds
should not be avoided; but auspicious deeds are not 10 be performed
and should be avoided in the regions of Konkana (the region of
Southern India which compriscs Canara, Ratnagiri, Kolava, Bombay.
Thana etc.), Magadha (the Southern part of the modern state of
Brhar), Gaur region (Central Bengal and Urissa). the Sindha region
(Kutchh and Bahayalpura Vela which is now called Bombay).

Notes: It is said in “Vayavahar Chandrika’ that the whole of
Jupiter in the sign of Capricorn should not be rejected in perfornung
auspicious deeds. He is to be rejected only in the lowest degrees (5°)
of this sign of his fall (Capricorn).

THE LUPTA SAMVATSARA AND ITS AVOIDANCE IN
PERFORMING AUSPICIOUS DEFDS If Fupiter is Atichan in oth-

4! Auspicious and Inauspicious Muhurtas

er signs except those of Taurus, Aries, Pisces and Aquarius, and if
after becoming retrograde he does not move back to the former sign
(the sign in which he has become Atichari) then that Varsha (the
Guru Varsha related to the former sign which has been crossed due
10 his being Atichari) will be called Lupta-Samvatsar (or Mahatichaar).
This Lupta samvatsar is to be completely rejected in performing aus-
picious deeds.

Notes: The Bhoga Kaala (the traversed period) of Jupiter of one
sign when he moves with his medium speed is called Samvatsar.
These, by the names of Prabhava etc, are the Guru Varshas or Guru
years. If Jupiter with his Spashta or true gati (speed) moves into one
sign in every year then they are the Shuddha Varshas (pure years)
related to their respective signs.

According to the above Shloka if Jupiter becomes Atichari in
the signs of Taurus, Aries Aquarius and Pisces and being retrograde
does not move back to his former sign, and in this way causes a
Lupta Samvatsar, that Lupta Samvatsar is not to be rejected in do-
ing auspicious deeds.

Atichaar: The reaching of a planet to a certain place before its
fixed time, that is, the time in which he would have reached with
his natural speed, is called Atichaar. Therefore if a planet enters a
certain sign before its normal time of entering it with his medium
speed, then it will be called the Atichaar of thal planet. This Atichaar
is not regarded as auspicious. The Atichaars particularly of Jupiter,
the Sun and the Moon are inauspicious—effects—giving because the
Atichaar of the Moon causes Kshaya tithi (non-occurrence of a tithi),
that of the Sun causes Kshaya Maas {non-occurrence of a month),
and that of Jupiter causes Kshaya Varsha (non occurrence of a year}.

If Jupiter moves with his natural or medium speed his time of
moving in one sign is one year or one Samvatsar. These Varshas
(vears) which are named as Prabhaya etc. are called Guru Varshas
(or Jupiter years). If the moving time of Jupiter in one sign is one
year then that vear is the Shuddha Varsha (pure year) related to that
sign. Contrary to this, if he moves in two signs in one year then such
a Jupiter. because of his not completing one year with his natural or
Medium speed. is called Atichaari. If such a Jupiter does not become
retrograde and does not come back to his former sign. then the
Varsha or year of the former sign will be called Lupta-Samwvatsar or

42 Muhuna Chinta Mani

Viluptabd. If he becomes retrograde and comes back to the former
sign from which he has become Atichaari. then there dees not occur
any Viluptabd. In such a condition it should be known by making
calculations as to whether when the retrograde Jupiter comes back
to his former sign, the total time of his Direct Movement and that
of his Retrograde Movement comes to one year or not. If this total
time is not one year inspite of Jupiter's retrogression, then that Varsha
or year is also regarded as Lupta-Samvatsar or Luptabd. The learned
Astrologers of Maithili call it Mahatichaar.

VAAR PRAVARTI (THE TIME AT WHICH A DAY BE-
GINS: One fourth (1/4) is to be obtained of the distance (in yojanas;
a yojana is a measure of distance) of one’s own city from the cities
or the regions through which the Prime meridian line has passed (Ac-
cording to the ancient sages the cities and the regions through which
it has passed are Kalicut, Sholapur, Ujjain, Kurukshetra, Jalagaon,
Indore, Sawai Madhopur, Jaipur, Pilani, Ludhiana, Jammu etc), wheth-
er one’s own city is situated to the west or to the east of this line.
This one fourth part is to be subtracted from the number of these
very yojanas. The figure which is arrived at after subtraction is sup-
posed to be so many palas and these palas are added to 15 ghatis
(If one’s own city is to the west of the Prime Meridian line, then
these Palas are added to 15 ghatis; if it is to the east then these are
to be subtracted from 15 ghatis. How much less the number of the
ghatis and palas obtained in this way is from the Dinaardha (Half of
the Dinmaana) of one’s own city that much number of ghatis and
palas will be the Vaar Pravarti after the rising of the Sun. If the num-
ber is greater. then Vaar Pravarti will be as much before the rising
of the Sun as this number is greater than the Dinaardha of one’s own
city

Notes Here is an example to know the Vaarpravarti. Suppose
Varanasi is al a distance of 63 yojanas (1 vojana = 5 kosha and one
kosha = about |'% miles) from the Prime Meridian Line. One fourth
part of it will be 63 = 4 = 15- 45. This figure when it is subtracted
from 63 gives 47 - 15 These are palas and these are subtracted from
15 ghatis. The remainder comes to be 14/12/45 Then suppose in a

43 Auspicious and Inauspicious Muhurtas

given year, in a given month and on a given day the Dinaardha is
15 ghatis and 24 palas. We want to know the Vaar Pravarti of this
day. Here we find that our remainder is less than the Dinaardha. The
difference between the two is 1/11/15. Therefore, on this particular
day the Vaar Pravesh occurred 1/11/15 (one ghati, 11 palas and 15
vipalas) after the rising of the Sun. If this difference comes to be
zero then Waar Pravesh occurs just af the rising of the Sun.

THE METHOD OF KNOWING THE KAALHORESH: The
ghatis and palas of the Ishta Kaala (the time the Kaala Hora of which
is to be known) from the time when there is the Vaar Pravriti are
doubled and the figure so obtained is to be put at two places. The
doubled ghatis and palas of one place are to be divided by 5. The
remainder after dividing is to be subtracted from the doubled ghatis
and palas and 1 is to be added to it and the figure so obtained will
give the K2al Hora of the Ishta Kaal. If the total comes to be more
than 7, then it is divided by 7 and the remainder will be the number
of the day the lord of which will be the Kaal Horesh (counting from
the day on which the Kaal Hora is to be known).

Notes: Suppose it is to be known as to who will be the Horapati
or Horesh at 6 Ishta ghati on Monday, that is Kaal Horesh is to be
known after 6 ghatis from Vaar Pravarti. Then according to the Rule
Ishta ghati is doubled which gives 12. This figure is pul at two places.
the figure of the second place is divided by 5. The remainder which
is 2 is to be subtracted from the doubled Ishta ghati 12 which gives
10. Then by adding 1 to 10 we get 11, This figure is divided by 7
as it is more than 7. The remainder after dividing comes to be 4.
Counting from the Lord of Monday (the Moon) the Lord of the 4th
day, Thursday (Jupiter) will be the Kaal Horesh.

There are 24 Horas in every day as 2 ghatss=1 Hora, that
ts, one Hora is equal to 1 Hour. The First Hora will be of the day
which is there on that particular day. Thus on Sunday the First Hora
will be of the Sun, the Lord of Sunday.

Muhurta Chinta Mant

SIGNIFICANCE OF THE KAALHORA The deed which has
been advised in the Shastras to be performed on a particular day may
be done (on any day) in the Kaal Hora of the Lord of that day. the
deed which has been advised to be done in a particular Nakshtra. that
may be done in the Tithyamsha or Muhurta or in the 15th part of
the Lord of that Nakshtra (There are 15 Muhurtas in a day). These
are called Swami Tithyamsha. A Tithyamsha is equal to the 15th part
of Dinmaan and is considered to be the part of the Nakshtra.

The consideration of the defect of Vaar Shoola and Nakshtra
Shoola is also to be made both in the Hora and in the Muhurta; and
Parighdanda (the special defect of the time of a journcy) according
to Shioka 36 of Chapter 11) is not to be violated even in the Muhurla
of the Nakshtra.

Notes: A particular deed is to be performed on that very day
on which it has been advised to be done according to the Shastras
or classics on Astrology. But if the deed is necessarily to be done
on a certain day which is not suitable ror that deed, then it may be
done even on that unsuitable day in the Kaal Hora of the day which
is suitable for that deed.

THE NAKSHTRAS, STHIR OR DHRUWA (FIXED) AND
THE THINGS WHICH SHOULD BE DONE IN THEM: The three
Uttaras (Uttara Phalguni, Uttarashadha and Uttara-Bhadrapada) Rohini
and the day of Sunday these are called Sthir or Dhruwa. In these
Nakshtras and on this day things of fixed and permanent nature,
things concerning one’s house, sowing of seeds things or rites done
for getting peace and the planting of smal! garden etc. can be auspi-
ciously done.

Notes: If things of permanent significance are performed in the
Sthir and Dhruwa Nakshtras, the results obtained will also be of per-
manent nature. Brahmarishi Vashistha has said, “ 9g Fa 
 at anf ," that is. the things which are advised to be

done in the Nakshtras called Mridu are also auspicious-effects giv-
ing when they are begun in these-Sthir, Dhniwa-Nakshtras.

50 Muhurta Chinta Mani

THE NAKSHTRAS CHARA (Movable) ETC. AND THE
THINGS WHICH SHOULD BE DONE IN THEM: The Nakshtras
Swati, Punarvasu, Shravana, Dhanistha, Satbhisha and the day of
Monday-these are called Chara and Chais. In these Nakshtras and on
this day such things as riding an elephant etc. walking in a garden
and things which are too be done in the Nakshtras called Laghu may
be auspiciously performed.

THE NAKSHTRAS UGRA-KRUR (CRUEL) ETC. AND THE
THINGS WHICH SHOULD BE PERFORMED IN THEM: The
Nakshtras, the three Poorvas (Poorva Phalguni, Poorvashadha, and
Poorva Bhadrapada) Bharani, and Magha and the day of Tuesday
these are called ugra and Krur (of strong and Malefic Nature). In
these Nakshtras and on this day ambush (killing some one), burning
(of the House etc.), deception (cheating some one), poisoning (giv-
ing poison to some one or to one’s own self), using weapons (using
or making weapons)-these things may be effectively done. And the
things which are to be performed in the Nakshtras called ‘Daruna’
(Horrible) may also be performed in those called Ugra, Krur etc.

THE NAKSHTRAS MISHRA (MIXED) AND SADHARANA
(ORDINARY) ETC. AND THE THINGS WHICH SHOULD BE
PERFORMED IN THEM: The Nakshtras Vishakha and Kritika and
the day of Wednesday are called Mishra (Mixed) and Sadharana (or-
dinary) (of mixed and ordinary nature). In these Agnihotra (the bumn-
ing of the sacred fire), deeds of adulteration (mixing) Vrashotsarga
(giving of an ox for getting one’s desire fulfilled) may be auspiciously
done. And the things which are to be done in the Nakshtras called
ugra (cruel) may also be effectively performed in them.

Benefic) and Maitra (friendly). In these the learning and the singing
of songs, getting clothes prepared and wearing them, playing games,
may be auspiciously done.

$3 The Nakshtras

Adhomukha Nakshtras (The, Nakshtras having their mouths down-
wards). Ardra, Pushya, Shravana, Dhanishtha, Shatabhisha, and the
Nakshtras which are called Dhruwa (Uttara Phalguni, Uttarashadha,
Uttarabhadrapada, Rohini)-these are called Urdhavamukhi Nakshtras
(or the Nakshtras which have their mouths upwards). And Anuradha,
Hasta, Swati, Poonarvasu, Jyestha and Ashwini, these are called
Tiryaka Mukha Nakshtras (or the Nakshtras which have their mouths
curved). It is auspicious to do the same kind of things in a Nakshtra
that are like it. ;

Notes: The Adhomukha Nakshtras; The mouth of such
Nakshtras is regarded to be downward. refore these are called
Adhomukha Nakshtras. In these Nakshtras the beginning of digging
well, small weil, tank, small tank and mine; the taking out of buried
wealth, the study of Mathematics and Astrology, the beginning of
Sculpture, entering a cave, the knowing of the science of spirits, jour-
ney in a tube railway etc. are regarded as auspicious.

The Urdhwamukha Nakshtras: It is thought that these have their
mouths upwards. Therefore, they are called Urdhwamukha Nakshtras.
In these Nakshtras such deeds may be auspiciously done as the be-
ginning of building a house or a temple, hoisting of flags, making
of Mandap, auspicious deeds like marriage ¢tc., building of bound-
ary walls, the ceremony of coronation ¢tc.

The Tiryaka Mukha Nakshtras: These Nakshtras are also called
Parshavamukha, Sammukha Mukha, Sameep mukha ctc. In these
Nakshtras it is auspicious to make. purchases of elephant, horse, camel,
ox etc. and to train them. Ploughing, the deeds of singing and play-
ing on musical instrum«ants etc., learning to drive motor bus, making
correspondence and making journeys on water, on land and in the
sky these things are auspicious effect giving.

Notes: The Nakshtras Punarvasu, Pushya, and those which are
called Dhniwa have been specially recommended for wearing new
clothes. These Nakshtras are not recommended for a woman who is’
fortunate in having her husband alive because if she wears new
clothes in these Nakshtras she remains deprived of having her hus-
band’s company (sexual intercourse). For men and widowed women
wearing clothes etc. in these Nakshtras is not prohibited. It is oblig-
atory for woman whose husband is alive that she should not take bath
on the day on which falls the Shatbhisha Nakshtra. Shri Chandeshwar
says in this connection

That is, the women who take bath in the Nakshtra of
Shatbhisha, remain sterile or widow for seven lives and are ugly.

THE EFFECTS WHEN A NEW CLOTH IS BURNT, TORN
AND SOAKED IN MUD: The clothes (so burnt etc.) be divided imo
9 parts. Then it is to be imagjned that there are gods in the four cor-
ners, demons in the three parts of the middie and men in the remain-
ing parts of the sides. If the new cloth is burm or is torn or is
soaked in mud in the pert of the demons then it is inauspicious. If it

auspicious. If it so happens in the borders of the part of all the three,

THE RULE WITH REGARD TO WEARING CLOTHES ON
A DAY WHICH IS TO BE REJECTED: With the permission of the
Brahmin the clothes which are obtained in a marriage or happily giv-
en by a king or by an entinent person should be worn even in a diy
or a Nakshtra etc. which is rejected for this purpose, it is said by
the learned astrologers.
Notes: In this Shioksa cloth is only a representative object.

MUHURTA WITH REGARD TO PLANTING OF TREES,
SEEING ROYALTY, PREPARING WINE AND TRADING OF
CATTLE ETC.: The planting of creepers and trees is auspicious in
the Nakshtras of Vishakha, Moola, Shatbhisha, the Nakshtras called
Mridu, Dhniwa and Kshipra. The seeing of Royalty is auspicious in
the Nakshtras of Shravana, Dhanistha, the Nakshiras called Mridu,
Dhriwa and Kshipra. Preparing and drinking of wine is auspicious
in the Nakshtra of Shatbhisha, the Nakshtras called. Tikshna and Ugra.
The purchase and sale of cows is auspicious in the Nakshtras of
Revati, Vishakha, Punarvasu, Jyestha, Shatbhisha, Dhanistha and the
Nakshtras called Kshipra

THE MUHURTAS WITH REGARD TO THE ENTERING
AND GOING OUT OF ANIMALS FROM CATTLE PROTECTION
AND COW SHEDS: In such an auspicious ascendant when there is
no planet in the 8th House, in the Nakshtra of one’s own yoni (de-
scribed in chapter 6) in the Nakshtras called chara it is excellent to
protect animals. And in the Rikta Tithi, Ashiami (the 8th Hindi tithi)
Amavasya (the 30th tithi), on Tuesday, in Shravana, Chitra and the
Nakshtras called Dhruwa the going of anmmmals (cattle) out of cow
shed and their entering, bringing up in it, this is not auspicious.

Notes: It has occurred in the Pashu Raksha Muhurta’ that on
that day there should be the Nakshstra of that yoni which is the yoni

57 The Nakshtras

of that animal. Yoni is a technical word. What is the yoni of every
Nakshira, it has been told in the 25 and 26 Shlokas of chapter 6.
Here the meaning is that the Nakshtras of that yoni are to be taken
which is the yoni of that animal which is to be protected. For exam-
ple, for the protection of Ox and Cow the Nakshtras of ‘Gau’ (Cow)
yoni are to be taken, for the protection of horses the Nakshtras of
‘Ashwa’, for the protection of buffaloes, the Nakshtras of Mahisha
(Buffalo), and for the protection of elephants, the Nakshatras of ‘Gaja’
(elephant) yoni are to be taken.

THE MUHURTA WITH REGARD TO TAKING MEDICINE
AND STITCHING: At the time when the patient takes the First Dose
of a medicine there should be the Nakshtras called Laghu, Mridu and
Chara and the Nakshtras of Moola; there should be the presence of
the benefic planets like Venus, the Moon, Jupiter and Mercury in the
Ascendant of Dual Nature (3/6/9/12) and there should be no planet
in the 12/7/8 Houses from the Ascendant; and there should be the
day of Friday, Monday, Thursday, Wednesday and Sunday and any
Tithis leaving the tithis called Rikta and Amavasya. It is inauspicious
to take medicine in the Nakshira of one's own birth. Doing the work
of stitching is auspicious in the Nakshtras of Punarvasu, Dhanishtha,
Chitra, Anuradha, Ashwini and Pushya.

THE MUHURTA OF MAKING PURCHASE AND SALE: It
18 inauspicious to make a sale in the Nakshtras of making purchase
and to make a purchase in the Nakshtras of making sale. The
Nakshtras of Revati, Shatbhisha, Ashwini, Swati, Shravana and Chitra
are auspicious with regard to making purchase

ANDHAKSHA, MANDAKSHA, MADHYAKSHA AND
SWAKSHA NAMES OF THE NAKSHTRAS: Dhanishtha, Pushya,
en Nakshtras are called Andhaksha. Hasta, Uttarashadha, Anuradha,
Shatabhisha, Ashlesha, Ashwini and Mrigshira-these 7 Nakshtras are
named as Mandaksha. Ardra, Magha, Poorva Bhadrapada, Chitra,
Jyestha, Abhijit and Bharani, these 7 Nakshtras are called
Madhyaksha; Swati, Punarvasu, Shravana, Kritika, Uttara Bhadrapada,
Moola and Poorvaphalguni, these 7 Nakshtras are named as Swaksha.

Notes: Andhaksha means to be blind. In the same way
Mandaksha means one having small eyes, Madhyaksha means one
having medium sized eyes'and, Swaksha means one having beauti-
ful eyes. The easier method of knowing these Nakshtras Andhaksha
etc. is as follows: begin counting from the Nakshtra named Rohini
The four Nakshtras onwards {including Rohini) will be respectively
Andha, Manda, Madhya and Swaksha Nakshtras. Then the next four
Nakshtras will again have these four names. This order will go on
till the end of 28 Nakshtras.

THE EFFECTS OF THE NAKSHTRAS ANDHA ETC. : The

62 . Muhurts Chinte Mani
wealth lost (or stolen) in the Nakshtras named Andhaksha is gained
immediately; that lost (or stolen) in those called Mandaksha is gained
with efforts, that lost (or stolen) in those called Madhyaksha is heard
of in or after many days to be in a distant, remote place, but it (this
wealth) is not gained and; that lost (cr stolen) in those named
Swaksha or Sulochana is neither heard of nor gained.

Notes: The effects of these Nakshtras is to be applied with re-
gard to the person (younger or elderly} who has run away from home
in anger due to quarrel etc.

In other books on Astrology it is stated that the wealth lost or
stolen in the Nakshtras called Andhaksha moves in the Eastern di-
rection; that lost or stolen in those named Mandaksha moves in the
Southern direction; that lost or stolen in those named Madhyaksha
moves in the Western direction and; that lost or stolen in those called
Swaksha moves in the Northern direction. The following Shloka is
obtained in this connection

THE NAKSHTRAS WHICH SHOULD BE REJECTED IN
LENDING AND BORROWING AND AMASSING MONEY: The
money which is lent to some one as a loan, is kept in a chest or
buried under ground, is given to some one on interest or is lost in
the Nakshtras named Teekshana, Mishra, Dhruwa and Ugra and the
yogas of Bhadrakarana and Vyatipaata is not got back.

63 The Nakshtras

THE MUHURTAS WITH REGARD TO DIGGING WELLS
0 TANKS AND BEGINNING DANCING: In the Nakshtras of
Anuradha, Hasta, those called Dhruwa, and those of Dhanistha,
Shatbhisha, Magha, Poorvashadha, Revati Pushya and Mrigshira; if
there is Mercury or Jupiter in the Ascendant; if the Malefics are weak,
if Venus is situated in the 10th House from the Ascendant and the
Moon is of the watery signs (9/10/11/12), in such a Muhurta the dig-
ging of a well and a tank etc is auspicious. In the above said
Nakshtras excluding Poorvashadha and Magha and including Jyeshtha
Nakshtra, if there is Mercury in the Ascendant and the Moon is there
in Gemini or Virgo sign and there are benefic planets in the 4th
House, then the learning or the first beginning of Dancing is auspi-
cious.

Notes: In the above mentioned Shloka the situation of Mercu-
ry in the Ascendant, that of the Moon in the Gemini or the Virgo
sign and then that of benefic planets in the 4th House have been spo-
ken of as auspicious. Now, after the situation of these two benefic
planets (Mercury and the Moon) only two benefic planets remain.
Therefore, from the phrase in the Shloka ‘feg@ ”, the plural of
a benefic planet does not seem to be proper duc to the reason that
possibly there may be one or two benefic planets in the 4th House.
Therefore ‘féqa 3% this reading may be proper.

Although according to other learned astrologers the Nakshtras
auspicious for digging smal! well, well and tank are different, even
then the Acharya (the writer of the present book) has spoken of
Nakshtras of the same type for all of them because of the same kind
of all the three (they are all reservoir of water) things.

THE NAKSHTRAS OF VAAPI OR SMALL WELL: Swati,
Ashwini, Pushya, Hasta, Punarvasu, Rewati and Shatabhisha.

THE NAKSHTRAS OF DIGGING WELL: Hasta, Pushya,
Dhanistha, Shatabhisha. Anuradha, Magha and al! the three Uttaras.

THE NAKSHTRAS OF BUILDING TANK: Ashwini,
Mrigshira, Rewati, all the 3 Uttaras, Rohini Pushya, Shatbhisha, and
Poorvashadha.

(Mrigshira, Rewati, Chitra, Anuradha), on the days of Wednesday, Fri-
day, Sunday and Thursday, when the benefic planets are in the As-
cendant and the Sun or Mars are in the 10th or in the 110 House,
when there is friendship in the Lords of the Yoni and the Rashi of
the Master and the servant (The details of Yoni Maitri or friendship
and the friendship of the Planets is given in the Shlokas from 25 to
28 of the Chapter entitled ( ‌'  the Chapter regarding
marriage), that servants should serve the Masters.

Notes: The serial No. of the Nakshtra of the Master should not
be before that of the servant.

EY: The use of money (the exchange of money, depositing monty
in the Bank, taking LIC Policy and fending money ¢tc) is auspicious
in the Swati, Punarvasu, the Nakshtras called Mridu (Mngshira,
Rewati, Chitra and Anuradha) Vishakha, Pushya, Shravana,
Dhanishtha, Shatbhisha, and Ashwini Nakshtras; in the Ascendants
called chara (Movable) which are 1/4/7/10 and if there is no planet
in the 9th, 5th and 8th Houses from the Ascendant.

Money is not to be borrowed on Tuesday. On the day of the
Sankranti of the Sun in the Vndhi yoga. in the Hasta Nakshtra and
on Sunday-money borrowed at these times continues to exist in the
borrower's family for ever, that is, such debt is never completely paid
off and the burden of it is always there on his family: even his sons
and grandsons are never relieved from this debt. Money should not
be jent to any one on Wednesday.

Notes: The family burdened with debt should keep fast on Tues-
day. By doing so the family certainly gets relieved from the debt
Collection of monex is good on Wednesday

65 The Nakshtres

THE MUHURTA FOR PLOUGHING: Ploughing (First plough-
ing at the beginning of the year or the session) has been said to be
auspicious in the Nakshtras of Moola, Vishakha, Magha, in those
named as ‘Chara’ or Movable (Swati, Punarvasu, Shravana,
Dhanishtha and Shatbhisha), in all the three Uttaras and Rohini, in
those called Mridu or Delicate (Mrighshira, Rewati, Chitra and
Anuradha), in those called Kshipra (Hasta, Ashwini and Pushya), on
days other than Sundays and Saturdays, when the Malefics (the Sun,
Mars and Saturn) are weakened, when the Moon is in the Navamsha
of the watery signs (4/10/11/12); when the Planets of Venus and the
Moon have full strength and when Jupiter is posited in the Ascen-
dant. There is loss in Agriculturing if ploughing is performed in the
Ascendants of Leo, Aquarius, Cancer, Anes, Capricom and Libra, on
the Rikta Tithis (4/9/14) and on the Shashthi (6th) Tithi.

THE MUHURTA FOR SOWING SEEDS: The sowing of seeds
is auspicious on the tithis and days and in the Nakshtras which have
been said as auspicious for ploughing, that is, the sowing of seeds is
auspicious in the Nakshtras of Moola, Magha, Swati, Dhanishtha,
Uttaraphalguni, Uttarashadha, Uttara Bhadrapada, Rohini, Mrigshira,
Rewati, Chitra, Anuradha, Hasta, Ashwini and Pushya and on the days
of Monday. Wednesday Thursday and Friday,  Nakshtras from the

66 Muhurta Chinta Mani

Nakshtra in which Rahu is there are inauspicious for the sowing of
seeds, three Nakshtras after it are auspicious, then one Nakshtra is
inauspicious, then three Nakshtras are auspicious, then again one
Nakshtra is inauspicious; and then again 3 Nakshtras are auspicious,
then one Nakshtra is inauspicious, and then 3 Nakshtras are auspi-
cious and then four Nakshtras are inauspicious. Seeds are to be sown
in the auspicious Nakshtras only. This is called Rahu Chakra or Hala
Chakra or the cycle of Rahu or the cycle of plough share. The Munis
or thinkers have called inauspicious and auspicious the 3/8/9/8
Nakshtras from the Nakshtra in which the Sun is there in this Rahu
Chakra or ‘Hala Chakra’, that is, the three Nakshtras before the
Nakshtra in which the Sun is there are inauspicious for ploughing,
then 8 Nakshtras are auspicious, then again 9 Nakshtras are inauspi-
cious and 8 Nakshtras are auspicious,

Notes: In Shioka 28 the wniter has spoken of the auspicious
Nakshtras, tithis and days on which the first ploughing at the begin-
ning of the year or the session should be done. The sowing of seeds
is auspicious in all the Nakshtras except those of Shravana,
Shatbhisha, Punarvasu and Vishakha and the day of Tuesday. The
Rikta and the Shashthi Tithis are inauspicious and should be rejected
in reference to the first ploughing. Therefore these tithis should be
rejected in reference to the sowing of seeds also.

VAMAN (AIl the three are done in the treatment of diseases) AND
RELIGIOUS ANUSTHANS (Performance of religious deeds):

SHIRA CHHEDANA OR THE LETTING OUT OF BLOOD
FROM VEINS (FOR PURIFICATION OF BLOOD). The Nakshtas
of Chitra. Swati. Anuradha, Jyestha, Rohini, Mrigshira. Shatbhisha.
those called Laghy (Hasta. Ashwini and Pushva) and Shravana and
the days of Tuesday. Sunday and Thursday are auspicious for Shira

Chhedana or the letting out of blood from veins.

VIRECHANA AND VAMAN OR CATHARSIS AND VOM-
ITING (Taking medicinal anema or laxative and to take medicine for
yomitting for cleaning the bowels): For Virechana, the Nakshtras that
have been said to be auspicious for Shiro Chhedana and the other
days than those of Wednesday and Saturday are auspicious.

PERFORMANCE OF RELIGIOUS RITES OR DEEDS: the per-
formance of religious rites is proper and auspicious in the Nakshtras
called Mitra (friend)-Mrigshira, Rewati, Chitra, and Anuradha-those
called Kshipra-Hasta, Ashwini and Pushya-those called Chara or Mov-
able-Swati, Punarvasu, Shravana, Dhanishtha, Shatbhisa-those called
Dhrowa-Uttaraphalguni, Uttarashadha, Uttara Bhadrapada and Rohini-
on Sunday and on the days of Benefic Planets, that is, on Wednes-
days, Thursdays, Fridays and Mondays, if there is the Shadavarga of
Mercury or Jupiter, in the Ascendant or if there is Jupiter in the As-
cendant and Jupiter is strong also.

Notes: The Sanskrit interpreter of ‘Muhurta Chintmani’, Shri
Govind has regarded Anuradha as the Nakshtra called Mitra (friend)
because this Nakshtra has ‘Mitra’ as one of its names. If we accept
this statement, then only 13 Nakshtras will be accepted as proper in
which religious rites may be performed and then the Nakshtras of
Mnigshira, Rewati and Chitra will not be accepted.

The Natal Ascendant, Saptamamsha, Decanate, Navamsha,
Dwadashamsha and Trimshamsha have been called as Shadavarga.
When there is the sign of Mercury or of Jupiter in these shadavargas
it should be understood that there is the shadavarga of Mercury or
of Jupiter in the Ascendant. If there is the sign of these planets in
the Shadavarga it should be regarded as auspicious.

THE MUHURTA FOR REAPING CORN: Corn should be
feaped in the Nakshtras called Tikshna (Moola. Jveshtha. Ardra and

Ashlesha), Poorva Bhadrapada, Hasta, Kritika, Dhanishtha, Shravana,
Mnigshira, Swati, Magha, all the three Uttaras, Poorvashadha, Bharani,
Chitra and Pushya, on the days other than those of Saturdays and
Tuesdays and on the Tithis other than those of Rikta, and in the As-
cendants that are sthir or fixed (2/5/8/11)

THE MUHURTA FOR KANAMARDANA (THRESHING THE
CORN) AND DHANYA ROPANA (PLUCKING THE PLANTS OF
CORN FROM ONE PLACE AND PLANTING THEM AT ANOTH-
ER): Kanmardana or Threshing should be done in the Nakshtras of
Poorvaphalguni, Uttaraphalguni, Shravana, Magha, Jyeshtha, Rohini,
Moola, Anuradha and Rewati. Dhanya Ropana is auspicious in the
Nakshtras of Vishakha, Poorva Bhadrapada, Moola, Rohini, Shatbhisha
and Uttaraphalguni, and on other days than those of Saturday an
Tuesday.

THE MUHURTA FOR THE STORAGE OF THE GRAINS
AND THE INCREASING OF IT: The storage of the prains is aus-

(Vishakha and Kritika), those called Ugra or intense (All the three
Poorvas, Poorvaphalguni, Poorvashadha, and Poorva Bhadrapada.
Bharant and Magha) Ardra, Ashlesha and Jyeshtha, in all the Ascen-
dants other than those of Cancer, Aries, and Libra and on all the aus-
Picious days.

72 Muhurta Chinta Mani

the day of Thursday which is 5 when counted from Sunday is to be
added to 22 and the resultant figure comes to be 27. Then 27 is to
be divided by 4, the remainder is 3. Therefore the Agnivasa or the
abode of fire should be on the Earth. The doing of Havana on this
day will be auspicious and beneficial.

According to the Shloka i is added to the Sum of the Tithi
and the day and the resultant figure is to be divided by 4. Then if
the remainder comes to be 1, the abode of fire is supposed to be in
Heaven, if it comes to be 2, in the Hades or the underworld and if
it comes to be 3 or 0, on the Earth. In this way the abode of fire
remains only in 3 places, that is the abode of fire is there only in
Heaven, in the Hades or the underworld (Pataal) and on the Earth.
Therefore, the figure could be divided by 3 only and there was also
not ihe necessity of adding one (1) to the sum of the Tithi and the
day. Why is one added to the sum of the Tithi and the day and why
is it divided by 4 ? This question arises quite naturally. The answer
to this question is suitably given by our teachers of Astrology in the
following way
Proof:

73 The Nakshtras

that is, due to the fact that the top of the flame of fire being up-
ward rising, the abode of the Fire remains respectively in the Hades
or the Underworld, on the Earth, in the Sky (clouds) and in Heaven,
in which the inner part of the Earth and the Sky (clouds) remains in
one and the same world of the Earth because the Sky (clouds) has
also been regarded in the atmosphere of the Earth. Bhaskaracharya
has clearly said that 12 yojanas (96 miles) above the Earth there is
the atmosphere of the Earth; the clouds etc are in this atmosphere.
The real abode of the fire is in the order of Hades-Earth-Sky (clouds)
and Heaven. Therefore after dividing the Sum of the Tithi and the
day by 4 duc to the remainder as | the abode of the fire should be
in the Hades or the underworld, as 2, on the earth, as 3 in the sky
(clouds) and as 0, in Heaven. But due to Heavens being the best
abode, the Acharyas or the learned kept this order of the abode of
Fire-Heaven-Hades or the underworld-the Earth and the sky (clouds).
It was due to this fact that there arose the necessity of adding 1 to
the Sum of the Tithi and the Day. Therefore, due to the remainder
being 1 the abode of the fire was supposed to be in Heaven, it be-
ing 2, im the Hades or the underworld; as the Earth and the Sky
(clouds) are supposed as one, so due to the remainder being 3 and
0, the abode of fire was supposed to be on the Earth.

THE MUHURTA FOR EATING THE NEW GRAINS: The eat-
ing of the new grains is auspicious in the Nakshtras named as ‘Chara’
' or Movable (Swati, Poonarvasu. Shravana, Dhanishtha. Shatbhisha),
those called Kshipra (Hasta. Ashwini and Pushya), those called Mridu

or Delicate (Mrigshira, Rewati, Chitra and Anuradha), in an auspi-
cious Ascendant, in all the tithis, Months, Ascendants Ghatis and Days
except on the Nanda Tithis (Pratipada, Shashthi and Ekadashi 1/6/11
Tithis) in the Ghati called Vishghati, in the months of Chaitra and
Pausha and on the days of Saturday and Tuesday.

beginning and the ending of the Vishghatika should be found out by
proportion after finding the real Bhabhoga Ghatis of a Nakshtra, If
the value of Nakshtras remains 60 Ghatis, then the Vishghati will be-
gin in each Nakshtra after the following Ghatikas and it will remain
til 4 ghatis.
