Now suppose the Bhabhoga ghatis of the Nakshtra of Rewati
are 64 Then we find that in the table given above the Dhruwa or

75 The Nakshtras

the constant of this Nakshtra (Rewati) is 30. Therefore 30 is muiti-
plied by 64. It gives 1920. It is divided by 60 and the quotient comes
to be 32. En this way it is clear that if the Bhabhoga of Rewati is
64 Ghatis, the beginning of the Vishghati will begin after 32 Ghatis
Again, as in the case of the value of a Nakshtra being 60, the value
of Vishaghati is 4, therefore we shall have to find the value of the
Vishaghati of a Nakshtra when its value is 64. In order to do 50 4
is multiplied by the Bhabhoga 64 and the resultant figure is divided
by 60. The quotient comes 4 ghatis 16 palas which is the total vatue
of the Vishaghati of Rewati. In other words, as the Bhabhoga of the
Nakshtra of Rewati is 64, then the Vishaghati will remain for 4 Ghatis
16 Palas after the 32 Ghatis.

THE MUHURTA FOR MAKING A BOAT: There is the proper
or auspicious Muhurta for making a boat in all the other Nakshtras
except in the Nakshtras of Bharani, Kritika, Rohini, Vishakha
Jyeshtha, Ashlesha, Magha, Ardra, on the days of Friday, Thursday
and Sunday and in an auspicious Ascendant.

Notes: In ‘Deepika’ the Muhurta for using a new boat is given
in this way. The using of a new boat is proper and ‘auspicious on
the auspicious days (Monday, Wednesday, Thursday and Friday), in
the Nakshtras of Shravana, Dhanishtha. Mrigashira, Poorva Phalguni,
Anuradha, Ashwini and Hasta, on other auspicious Tithis than those
called ‘Rikta’ (4/9/14) and Amavasya and when there is the auspi-
cious position of the Moon in othcr Houses except in those of
4-8-12 from the Natal Ascendant of the owner of the boat.

THE MUHURTA FOR GAINING SUPER NATURAL POW-
ER: ‘Veer Ka Sadhana (the having under control of Betal which is
a powerful spimt and Abhichaar or doing Marana or killing, Mohana
or possessing fully. and Uchchatana or removing a spirit) and other
such Karmas or rites are successfully performed in the Nakshtras of

76 Muhurta Chinta Mani

Moola, Ardra, Bharani, Magha, and Mrigshira, when Mercury is there
in the First House in the Ascendant of Aquarius, when the Planet of
Venus is in the 4th House from the Ascendant and the 8th House is
without any planet.

THE MUHURTA FOR THE BATHING OF THE PATIENT
WHEN HE IS RELIEVED OF THE DISEASE: The bathing of a pa-
tient when he is relieved of the disease he has been suffering from
is auspicious in  the other Nakshtras than those of Rewati,
Punarvasu, the Nakshtras named ‘Dhruwa’ (all the 3 Uttaras, that is,
Uttaraphalguni, Uttarashadha, Uttara Bhadrapada, and Rohini) those
of Magha, Swati and Ashlesha, on the Rikta Tithis (4/9/14), in the
chara or Movable Ascendants (1/4/7/10), on other days than those of
Friday and Monday, in the event of the Moon being emaciated (she
is emaciated when she is in the 4/8/12 Houses) when the Malcfic
Planets are in the 11th House, or in Angles or in Trines.

THE MUHURTA FOR LEARNING A CRAFT: It is auspicious
and excellent to commence the learning of a craft in the Nakshtras
named as Mridu or Delicate (Mrigshira, Rewati, Chitra and
Anuradha), those named as Dhruwa (Uttara Phalguni, Uttara Shadha,
Uttara Bhadrapada and Rohini), those named as Kshipra (Hasta.
Ashwini and Pushya), those named as Chara or Movable (Swati.
Punarvasu, Shravana. Dhanishtha and Shatabhisha), when Mercury, or
Jupiter is posited in the Ascendant or im the 10th House from the
Ascendant and the Moon is in the Shadavarga of Mercury or Jupiter

The Nakshtras

THE MUHURTA FOR MAKING TREATY FOR FRIEND-
SHIP: It is good and auspicious to make mutual friendship in the
Nakshtras of Mrigshira, Pushya, Anuradha, Poorvaphalguni, on the 8th
or 12th Tithi or in the Taitil ‘Karana’, on an auspicious day and when
the Ascendant is aspected by Venus.

Notes: The ‘Taitil Karana’ never comes on the 8th Tithi. It (the
Taitil karana) comes in the latter half of the 12th Tithi of the Krishna
Paksha or the Dark Half of the Month. Therefore, making treaty for
friendship on the 8th or on the 120 Tithi or in the ‘Taiti] Karana’,
this interpretation is quite proper.

THE MUHURTA FOR DIVINE TEST: It is auspicious and
proper to give a test (the test given to know whether the persons ac-
cused of theft, sexually loose morals are really guilty of the sins) leav-
ing the Tithis of 8th and 14th Saturday and Tuesday, the Bhadra Tithi,
the Nakshtra of the birth, the month of the birth, when there is the
Sun or the Moon in the 8th House from the natal Ascendant and the
Nakshtras of the Nadi of the Person, in the Ascendants having dual
nature (3/6/9/12) or in the Movable Ascendants and in the same
Navamsha Lagnas, when the position of the Moon, Jupiter and Tara
iS auspicious and in the Nakshtras of Hasta. Punarvasu, Shravana,
* Jveshtha and Shatbhisha.

Notes: In the ancient times in order to know the truth or false-
hood, a test was given to a person who was doubted of doing a sin-
ful deed like theft or of seducing some woman. In the time in which
the above stated shloka was composed, the person doubted was asked
10 touch some heated metal. If the person concerned was guilty of

78 Muhurta Chinta Mani

doing that deed the metal burnt his hand or the part of the body
which touched the metal. The innocent did not get any harm from
the metal. Such tests are not given in the present times.

As regards the ‘Nadi Nakshtras’, Shrimad Govinda ji has said
in the Sanskrit commentary of this book (Muhurta Chinatmani’): “For
the ordinary human beings the First Nakshtra from their Janama or
Birth Nakshtra is called the ‘Janama Nakshtra; the 10th, ‘Karma
Nakshtra’; the 16th “Sanghatika Nakshtra, the 18th, ‘Samudaya
Nakshtra’: the 23rd, ‘Vinasha Nakshira’; and the 25th, ‘Manasa
Nakshtra’:”. For the kings, besides’the above 6 Nakshtras, the other
Nakshtras such as the Nakshtra of the caste, of the country (region)
and of the time of their Abhisheka or Coronation, are also consid-
ered. In this way, for them there are 9 ‘Nadi Nakshtras’.

The Nakshtra of the country (region) is taken as per the name
of that country. The Nakshtra prevailing at the time of
‘Rajyabhisheka’ or coronation is referred to as ‘Abhisheka’ Nakshtra.

The Nakshtras of the castes are as follows:

1. The Nakshtras of the Brahmins: Poorva Phalguni,
Poorvashadha, Poorva Bhadrapada and Knitika.

2. The Nakshtras of the Kshtriyas (Martial Races): Utara
Phalguni, Uttarashadha. Uttarabhadrapada, and Pushya.

3. The Nakshtras of the farmers: Rewati, Anuradha, Magha and
Rohini.

4. The Nakshtras of the Vaishyas: Punarvasu. Hasta. Abhijit and
Ashwini.

5. The Nakshtras of the criminal castes: Moola Ardra, Swati and
Shatabhisha

6. The Nakshtras of the Shudras: Mnigashira, Iveshtha, Chitra
and Dhanishtha

7. The Nakshtras of the Chandala Caste: (the caste the mem-
bers of which take toll for cremation) Ashlesha, Vishakha, Shravana
and Bharani

The above mentioned 7 were the castes in which the Indian
Community was divided in those davs when the book was written

79 The Nakshtras

THE FAVOURABLE OR SUITABLE LAGNA OR ‘ASCEN-
DANT FOR DOING ALL THE ORDINARY AUSPICIOUS DEEDS:
The beginning of all the auspicious deeds has been regarded as proper
and excellent if there is any sign in the Ascendant of the signs of
Upachaya Houses, that is, the 3rd, the 6th, the 10th and the 11th
House from the Natal Ascendant and from the sign in which there
is the Moon that is, the Janama Rashi of the person concerned, and
there should be no planet in the 8th and the 12th House (that is, the
8th and 12th Houses from the Ascendant are ‘Shuddha’® or without
any planet); if there is the conjunction or aspect of the Benefic Planets
on the Ascendant; and if the Moon is also there in the Upachaya signs
(3/6/10/11) from the Ascendant. ,

Notes: A favourable or an auspicious Ascendant is found out
for the Muhurta of doing every deed. This is referred to as ‘Lagna
Shuddhi’ or a favourable or suitable Ascendant. ‘Lagna Shuddhi’ is
done according to the nature of the Muhurta. But here the writer has
told us of a ‘Lagna Shuddhi’ or favourable Ascendant or Ascendant
which will do for all the deeds of ordinary nature. Varahamihira has
said in “Brihat Samhita’ that the ‘Lagna Shuddhi’ or the favourable
Ascendant is auspicious and proper for the beginning of all kinds of
ordinary deeds if there are Benefic Planets in the 12/1/4/7/10/8 Hous-
es; the Malefic Planets should remain in the 3/6/11 Houses; and in
an Angle or in a Trine there should be Jupiter and Venus.

THE EFFECTS OF THE BEGINNING OF A DISEASE AS ~

. PER THE DIFFERENCE OF NAKSHTRAS: The patient who con:
tracts or gets fever in the 7 Nakshtras of Swati, Jyeshtha,

80 Muhurta Chinta Mani

Poorvaphalguni, Poorvashadha, Poorvabhadrapada, Ardra and
Ashlesha, is subjected to death. If the fever is contracted in the
Nakshtras of Rewati and Anuradha, it (the fever) remains for a long
lime; if it (the fever) is contracted in those of Bharani, Shravana,
Shatabhisha and Chitra, for 11 days; if in those of Vishakha, Hasta,
Dhanishtha, for 15 days; if in the 3 Nakshtras of Moola, Kritika and
Ashwini, for 9 days; if in those of Uttarabhadrapada, Uttaraphalguni,
Pushya, Punarvasu and Rohini, for 7 days, and if in those of
Mrigashira, Uttarashadha, for one month. If a person is bitten by a
snake in the 7 Nakshtras of Bharani, Ashlesha, Moola, those named
as Mishra or Mixed (Kritika, Vishakha, Ardra and Magha) he (the
person) is subjected to death.

Notes: If on the day on which the person is bitten by snake the Moon
is strong according to ‘Gochara’, the person is not subjected to death.

THE .COMBINATION WHICH LEADS TO IMMEDIATE
DEATH AT THE BEGINNING OF THE DISEASE: If the disease
has begun in the Nakshtras of Ardra, Ashlesha, Jyeshtha, Shatabhisha,
Bharani, Poorvaphalguni, Poorvashadha, Poorva Bhadrapada,
Vishakha, Dhanishtha and Kritika, on the days of Sunday, Thesday
and Saturday and on the Tithis of 4/9/14/12/6, the diseased person
dies soon.

THE MUHURTA FOR PRET KRIYA AND THE DEEDS
WHICH SHOULD NOT BE DONE DURING PANCHAKA: It is
proper and effect-giving to do Pret Kriya in the Nakshtras called
Kshipra (Hasta, Ashwini and Pushya) and in those of Ashlesha,
Moola, Mrigashira, Shravana, Ardra and Swati.

As long as the Moon remains in ihe Ascendants of Aquarius
and Pisces, that is, from the 3rd quarters of the Nakshira of
Dhanishtha to that of Rewati (the 5 Nakshtras from Dhanishtha to
Rewati are referred to as “Panchaka’) the deeds like the cremation

8) The Nekshtras

of a dead body, the journey towards the southern direction, the wey
ing of a cot-or stringed bed and making thatches for a house should
not be done

Notes: Certain rites are done after the death of some , The
combined name of these is ‘Pret Kriya’ if it is necessary to do the
cremation of a dead body in the Panchakas, then the pacification of
the Panchakas should be done as per the rules prescribed in the
Shastras.

THE MUHURTA FOR THE COLLECTION OF WOOD ETC.
The 6 Nakshtras before the Nakshtras of the 5 ue ose of the
Moon are called ‘Adhaha Sthala’. If the collection of wood is made
in these Nakshtras then juicy food is prepared in the Kitchen (with
that wood). Then with the collection of wood made in the 7 and 8
Nakshtras which are called ‘Shirsha’ dead body is cremated. Then if
the collection of wood is made in the Nakshtras 9,10,11 and 12 which
are called ‘Madhya’, there is the fear of snakes, Then in the Nakshtras
13,14,15, and 16 which are called ‘Poorva’, there is the coming of
friends to the person's house. After this in the Nakshtras 17,18,19
and 20 which are referred to as ‘Dakshina’, there is the fear of dis-
ease. Then in the Nakshtras 21,22,23 and 24 which are called
‘Pashchima’ the work of making of ‘Kwatha’ (A particular Ayurvedic
Medicine) is done. And 19809, in the Nakshtras 25,26,27 and 28
which are called ‘Uttara’ there is the getting of happiness by the per-
son-making this collection.

82 Muhurta Chinta Mani

THE YOGAS OF TRIPUSHKARA AND DWIPUSHKARA: If
on the days of Saturday, Tuesday and Sunday there are the Nakshtras
of Vishakha, Uttaraphalguni, Poorvabhadrapada, Punarvasu, Kritika
and Uttarashadha, and if on these days there are the Bhadra Tithis
also (2/7/12 Tithis) then due to the conjunction of these three-days,
Nakshtras and Tithis, there occurs the ‘TRIPUSHKARA YOGA’. This
‘Tri Pushkara Yoga’ (the combination of 3) gives three fold effect
in the death, in the destruction and in the increase (the meaning is
that if there is the “Tripushkara yoga” and if there is death in some-
one’s house, then 3 persons wiil die in his house, if there is the de-
struction of something in someone’s house in this yoga, then there
will occur the destruction of 3 things, and if in this yoga there is
the profit of one thing to someone, then there will be to him the profit
of three things). And the conjunction of these 3, the days of Satur-
day, Tuesday, and Sunday; the Nakshtras of Dhanishtha, Chitra and
Mrigshira; and the Bhadra Tithis (2/7/12 Tithis}Causes the occurrence
of the DWIPUSHKARA YOGA. This yoga gives two fold effect.

THE TIME WHICH SHOULD BE REJECTED IN DOING
‘PUTTALA DAHA’ OR THE BURNING (CREMATION) OF A
DUMMY OF SOMEONE: The ‘Puttala Daha’ or the burning (cre-
mation) of someone’s Dummy is not to be done on the days of Fri-
day, Tuesday, and Saturday, on the Tithis of Amavasya, Chaturdashi,
Tryodoshi (30,14,13) and on those called Nanda Tithis (1/6/11); in
the Nakshtras called ‘Tikshana’ (Moola, Jyeshtha, Ardra and
Ashlesha), in those called ‘Ugra’ (Poorvaphalguni, Poorvashadha and
Poorvabhadrapada, Bharani and Magha) in those of Rewati and
Shatbhisha, in these 11 Nakshtras; in the Tripushkara yoga; in the

83 The Nakshtras

‘Adhikamasa’ and in the Kshaya Masa (an additional month and a
destroyed month} in the Dakshinayana (when the Sun remains in the
signs from Cancer to Sagittarius), in the yogas of Vyatipata and
*Parigha’; when the planets of Jupiter and Venus are combust; on the
Bhadra and Vaidhriti Karanas and in the ‘Shukla Paksha’ or the bright
half of the month.

Notes: The burning of 2 dead body or Cremation is of two
kinds. First, there is that of the real dead body and secondly, in the
absence of the real dead body, that of the body made of straws like
the straws of Kusha Kasha and Plas gra¢s. The second kind of cre-
mation is done in that condition when the dead body of the person
concerned is not available. For example, the person concerned has
been drowned in water, or he has died in some foreign country or
region and his relatives have not received his dead body. Then it is
his dummy which is burnt.

Muhurta is not consulted in the cremation of the rea! body. If
the information of the concerned person’s death is got within a year,
the ‘Puttala Daha’ Sanskara (rite) may be done in ‘Dakshinayana’
also.

THE AUSPICIOUS AND INAUSPICIOUS TIME FOR THE
PERSON WHO BURNS THE DUMMY: The person who performs
the ‘Pret Kriya’, should not dy such rites as the cremation rite etc in
the ‘Janama’ and Pratyari Taras (These have been referred to and de-
scribed in the ‘Gochara Prakarana’) and when the moon is there in
the Houses of 4/8/12 from his Janama Rashi or the sign in which
the Moon is situated.

The performing of ‘Pret Kriya Karma is medium effects-giving
(for the person doing it if it is performed in the 12 Nakshtras of
Anuradha, Poorvaphalguni, Punarvasu, in those called Dhruwa
(Uttaraphalguni, Utiarashadha, Uttarabhadrapada and Rohini) in those
of Vishakha, Mrigshira, Chitra and Dhanishtha.

a4 Muhurta Chinta Mani

The Pret Kriya is auspicious for the person performing it on
the days of Sunday, Monday and Thursday and in the Nakshtras of
Shravana, Hasta, Swati, Ashwini and Pushya. Consideration has been
done with regard to the auspiciousness of the cremation of Dummy
Tite etc. The performing of these is not to be done during the time
of ‘Ashaucha’ (The period of Pataka: After someonc’s death in the
family a fixed period is regarded as unholy for the members of that
family and in this period the performing of worship, recitation of
Mantras and other auspicious deeds are not done, their performing
is rejected).

Notes: The person performing Kriya should not do ‘Pret Karma’
in the Janama Nakshtra (the Nakshtra of his birth) and in the Pratyari
Nakshtras, that is, the 5/10/14/19th and 23rd Nakshtras from the
Janama Nakshtra; and in the event of the Moon being in the 4/8/12
Houses.

“Ashaucha’ refers to that period in which from the day of the
death of the dead certain rites related to the Pretkriya-of him, such
as Ekadashaha, Dashagatra, Sapindana and Brehmanabhoja etc are
performed. This period is referred to as the period of Pataka. It is
usually of 13 days. 11 is regarded as unholy.

THE CONSIDERATION OF ABHUKTA MOOLA: According
to Brahmarishi Narada the last four Ghatis of the Nakshtra of Jyeshtha
and the first four Ghatis of that of Moola, these 8 Ghatis fcrm the
‘Abhukta Moola’.

Brahmarishi Vashishtha has said that it is the last one Ghati of
the Nakshtra of Jyeshtha and the first two Ghatis of that of Moola
(the 3 Ghatis combined) which form the ‘Abhuktamoola’. But accord-
ing to Acharya Brahaspati the “Abhuktamoola’ is formed by the last
half Ghati of the Nakshtra of Jyeshtha and the first kalf Ghati of that
of Moala,

THE MEASURE OF AND THE EFFECTS OF 'THE
ABHUKTAMOOLA ACCORDING TO ANOTHER OPINION:  &
the opinion of some unknown Acharya (teacher of Astrology) that
‘Abhuktamoola’ is formed by the first cight ghatis of the Nakshira
of Moola and the last 5 ghatis (these 13 ghatis combined) of that of
Jyeshtha. ‘

The native born in the ‘Abhuktamoola’ should be given up or
left or the father should not see its face for eight years.

THE EFFECTS OF THE BIRTH IN THE NAKSHTRA OF
MOOLA AS PER DIFFERENCE OF QUARTER AND IN THAT OF
ASHLESHA: There is the destruction (death) of the native’s father
if the native is born in the first quarter of the Nakshtra of Moola,
that of the native’s mother, if in the 2nd quarter; there is types of
wealth, if in the 3rd quarter, and the birth is auspicious if it occurs
in the 4th quarter. And if Shanti Anushthana or Pacification of the
defective birth is performed then it (the birth) is auspicious in all the
quarters.

The effects are reversed if the birth occurs in the different quar-
ters of the Nakshtra of Ashlesha, that is, it (the birth) is auspicious
if it occurs in the First quarter, it causes the loss of wealth, if in the
2nd one; it causes the death of the native’s mother, if in the 3nt
Quarte: and; that of the native’s father, if in the 4th Quarter.

Notes: According to Brahmarishi Vashishtha if the First quar-
ter of the Nakshtra of Moola falls in the night it does not cause any
evil for the native’s father, and similarly if the 2nd quarter of this
Nakshtra falls in the day time, it does mot cause any evil to its mother:

86 Muhurta Chinta Mani

THE ABODE OF THE NAKSHTRA OF MOOLA: The
Nakshtra of Moola resides or lives in Heaven in the months of
Ashadha, Bhadrapada, Ashwin and Magha; on the earth in those of
Shravana, Kartika, Chaitra and Pausha; in Pataal or the Hades or the
Underworld in those of Phalguna, Margashirsha, Vaishakha and
Jyestha. The inauspicious effects occur in the place where the
Nakshtra of Moola resides or lives.

Notes: The 12 Hindi Months in serial order are - Chaitra,
Vaishakha, Jyestha, Ashadha, Shravana, Bhadrapada, Ashwin, Kartika,
Margashirsha, Pausha, Magha and Phalguna. The month of Chaitra
falls roughly in the month of March. The falling of the other months
may be calculated after Chaitra.

THE INAUSPICIOUS TIME FOR THE BIRTH OF A CHILD:
The birth of a child occurring in the ‘Gandanta’ (Tithi Gandanta,
Nakshtra Gandanta, and Lagna Gandanta) in the Nakshtra of Jyeshtha;
in the yogas of Shoola, Mahapaata, Parigha, Vyaghaata, and Ganda;
in the event of Tithi Kshaya; on a Sankranti, in Vyatipata and
Vaidhriti, on the Sinivali Amavasya (the Amavasya on which the
Moon is visible) and that of Kuhn (the Amavasya on which the Moon
is not visible), in the Vajra yoga, on the Chaturdashi or the 14th Tithi
of the Krishna Paksha or the dark half of the month; in Yamaghania
(described in 9th Shloka of the Ist Chapter of this book) in the
Dagdha Yoga (described in 1/8), in the Mrityu Yoga (described in
1/30 and 1/5) in the Karana of ‘Bhadra’; in the Janama Nakshtra or
the Nakshtra of the birth of a brother and sister, and in that of the
native’s father and mother, in any of all these. is evil effects giving
or inauspicious. Therefore, there should be done the Shanti or paci-
fication of ail these. All is auspicious by performing ‘Shanti’ or paci-
fication.

87 The Nakshtras

THE NUMBER OF THE TARAS (OR STARS) OF THE
NAKSHTRAS ASHWINI ETC.: There are 3 Taras in Ashwini, 3 in
Bharani, 6 in Kritika, 5 in Rohini, 3 in Mrigshira, 1 in Ardra, 4 in
Punarvasu, 3 in Pushya, 5 in Ashlesha, 5 in Magha, 2 in
Poorvaphalguni; 2 in Uttaraphalguni, 5 in Hasta, 1 in Chitra, 1 in
Swati, 4 in Vishakha, 4 in Anuradha, 3 in Jyeshtha, 11 in Moola, 2
in Poorvashadha, 2 in Uttarashadha, 3 in Abhijita, 3 in Shravana, 4
in Dhanishtha, 100 in Shatbhisha, 2 in Poorvabhadrapada, 2 in
Uttarabhadrapada and 32 in Rewati

Notes: The path in which in the sky the Sun always revolves
round the earth is called ‘Kranti’. The circular path referred to as
-‘Krantivratia’ has been divided into 27 equal parts. Here Shri Rama
Daivagya has given the number of the Taras which were seen
toon each par by ia Ackayas or Teacher of Asolo inf
observatories.

THE SHAPES OF THE NAKSHTRAS: The shape of Ashwini
is like the mouth of a horse, that of Bharani like ‘Bhaga’ or the Va-
gina of a woman, that of Kritika like a dagger, that of Rohini like a
bullock cart, that of Mrigashira like the mouth of a deer, that of Ardra
like a gem, that of Punarvasu tike a house, that of Pushya like an
arrow, that of Ashlesha like a wheel, that of Magha like a house,
that of Poorvaphalguni like a stage, that of Uttaraphalguni like a bed,
that of Hasta like a hand, that of Chitra like a Pearl, that of Swati
like a coral, that of Vishakha like ‘Torana’ (A rosary of leaves hung
outside a door), that of Anuradha like bali (the heap of rice), that of
Jveshtha like a Kundal (a ring or an ormament of gold or silver worn

88 Muhurta Chinta Mani

in the ears), that of Moola like the tail of a lion, that of Poorva
shadha like the teeth of an elephant, that of Uttarashadha like Machan
{a device to hunt a lion or to ward off birds from a field of maize),
that of Abhijita like a triangle, that of Shravan, like “Tricharana’ (like
the shape of Vaman, the shape which Lord Vishnu assumed to de-
ceive Bali), that of Dhanishtha like Mridanga (an Indian Musical in-
strument), that of Shatbhisha like a c:rcle, that of Poorvabhadrapada
like a stage or a Machan, that of Uttarabhadrapada like two joined
men, and that of Rewati like Mridanga.

Notes: The Taras of the Nakshtras are described in Shloka no.
58. Due to the shapes of those Taras the shapes of the Nakshiras are
exactly like those described in the above 2 shlokas.

THE MUHURTA FOR DIGGING AND BUILDING A TANK,
FOR PLANTING A GARDEN OR AN ORCHARD AND ESTAB-
LISHING AN IMAGE OF SOME GOD OR GODDESS: It is auspi-
cious to begin the digging and building of a tank, the planting of a
Garden or an orchard and the establishing of an image of some god
or goddess in the Uttarayana of the Sun which is there from January
14 to July 16 (except in the month of Chaitra), at the rising of Ve-
nus, in the Naksthras called Mridu or delicate (Mrigashira, Rewati,
Chitra and Anuradha), in those called Kshipra (Hasta, Ashwini, and
Pushya), those called Chara or Movable (Swati, Punarvasu, Shravana,
Dhanishtha, and Shatbhisha), in those called Dhruwa (Uttaraphalguni,
Uttarashadha, Uttarabhadrapada and Rohini)-in these 16 Nakshtras in
the Shukla Paksha or the bright half of the month in the Nakshtra
of that god the image of whom is to be established (described in the

89 The Nakshtras

first shloka of this very chapter), his Tithi (Tithis have been described
in the 3rd shloka of Chapter 1), his Muhurta (these have been told
in the Shlokas from 52 to 55 in the 6th Chapter); in all the other
Tithis and Days excepting the Rikta Tithis (4/9/14) and on the day
of Tuesday, at those times when the Moon and the Malefic Planets
are in the Houses of 3/6/11], when the Benefic Planets are there in
the other houses than those of 8 and 12. The establishing of the im-
age of the Sun-God should be done in the Ascendant of Leo; that of
Brahma in that of Aquarius; that of Shankar or Shiva in that of
Gemini. In the Ascendants of dual natured signs (Gemini, Virgo, Sag-
ittarius and Pisces) there should be the establishing of the images of
the goddesses, and in the ‘Chara’ or Movable Ascendants (Aries, Can-
cer, Libra and Capricorn) that of a god of lower level. Particularly,
the establishing of the images of all the gods is to be done in the
Ascendants of fixed signs (Taurus, Leo, Scorpio and Aquarius)

Tlie establishing of the 8 planets the Moon etc is to be done
in the Nakshtra of Pushya, of the gods Ganesha, Yaksha, Sarpa
(Snake) and Bhoota etc in that of Rewati, and of the gods of Jains
(A Sub Religion in India) in that of Shravana.

CHAPTER 3
THE DESCRIPTION OF SANKRANTIS

1-1 THE NAMES OF THE SANKRANTIS AND THEIR
EFFECTS: If there is the Sankranti of the Sun in the Nakshtras named
as Ugra—Poorvaphalguni, Poorvashadha, Poorvabhadrapada, Bharani
and Magha that Sankranti is given the name of Ghora; this Sankranti
is the giver of joy and happiness to the people of Shudra Varna or
caste; if the Sankranti falls in the conjunction of the Nakshtras called
‘Laghu’ (smalf)—Hasta, Ashwini, Pushya and Abhijit—and of Mon-
day, it is named as the Sankranti of Dhwankshi; it gives happiness
and joy to the people of the Vaishya Vama or caste; if it happens
to fall in the conjunction of those Nakshtras which are referred to as
‘Chara’ or Movable (Swati, Punarvasu, Shravana, Dhanishtha and
Shatbhisha) and of the day of Tuesday, its name is ‘Mahodara’; it is
the thieves who are made joyful and happy in this Sankranti; the
Sankranti which happens to fall in the conjunction of the Nakshiras
named as Maitra (Mrigashira, Rewati, Chitra and Anuradha) and the
day of Wednesday is called by the name of ‘Mandakini’, it gives joy
and happiness to the people of the Kshtriya Varna or caste: that which
happens to fall in the conjunction of the Nakshtras called ‘Sthira’ or
fixed (Uttaraphalguni, Uttarashadha, Uttarabhadrapada and Rohini) and

91 The Description of Sankrantis

of the day of Thursday is referred to as ‘Manda’ which is the giver
of joy and happiness to the vara or caste of Brahmins, that hap-
pening to fall in the conjunction of Nakshtras named as Mishra or
Mixed (Vishakha, Kritika) and of the day of Friday gets the name
of ‘Mishra’; it gives joy and happiness to the animals; that Which
falls in the conjunction of the Nakshtras named as ‘Tikshna’ or in-
tense (Moola, Jyeshtha, Ardra and Ashlesha) and of Saturday is given
the name of ‘Rakshashi’, it gives joy and happiness to the people of
“Chandala’ Caste.

Notes: The name of the time of entrance of the Planets from
one sign into the other sign is ‘Sankranti’ Kala. A Sankranti is called
the Sankranti of that sign in which a Planet enters. In this way there
are the Sankrantis of all the Planets. In the Sankrantis of the Sun,
the Moon, Mars Mercury, Jupiter, Venus and Saturn respectively
32,2,9,6,88,9 and 160 ghatikas which occur before and after the time
of the Sankranti are rejected in doing an auspicious deed. In this
chapter it is only the 12 Sankrantis of the Sun that have been spe-
cifically commented upon as the ‘Saur Masa’ occurs due to the
Sankranti of the Sun, according to which Varsha or year, Ritu or sea-
son, Ayana {the Uttarayana and the Dakshinayana of the Sun) and
the Parvas or the festivals of ‘Kumbha’ etc. are determined. There
are the descriptions in our Dharmashastras (Scriptures) of the main
period in which bathing, giving of alms in charity etc. should be done.
These descriptions are available only of the Sankrantis of the Sun and
not of those of the other Planets.

2-3. If a Sankranti occurs in the first 3rd part of a day it gives
pain to the Kshtriyas, if in the 2nd, to the Brahmins, if in the 3rd to,
the Vaishyas and if it occurs at the setting of the day it gives pain
to the Shudras. If a Sankranti occurs in the first prahara of night, it
destroys the Pishachas ctc.; if in the 2nd one, the Rakshasas: if in *
the 3rd one, the Natas (actors in circus); and if in the 4th one, the
guards of animals or the gopas or the cowherds. The Sankranti oc-
‘curring at the time of the rising of the Sun destroys the hypocrites.
The Sankranti of ‘Makara’ or Capricorn is called Uttarayana, and that
of ‘Karka’ or Cancer is referred to as Dakshinayana.

Notes: If the ‘Dinmaana’ is divided by 3, the ghatis and palas
obtained as quotient are called the ‘Tritiyamsha’ (or the 3rd part of
the ‘Dinmaana’. If the ‘Ratrimaana’: is divided by 4, the ghatis and
Palas obtained as quotient are called prahara or yaama.

Pishacha: is a kind of evil spirit. Rakshasa: is a demon. Nata:
A caste the people of which carn their livelihood by showing bodily
feats.

‘THE NAMES OF THE SANKRANTIS AS PER THE DIFFER-
ENCE OF SIGNS: The Sankranti of the signs of Sagittarius, Gemi-
ni, Virgo and Pisces is named as ‘Shadashiti Mukha’; that of the signs
of Libra and Aries is called ‘Vishuwa’; and the name of that of the
signs of Leo, Scorpio, Taurus and Aquarius is ‘Vishnupada’.

THE PUNYA KAALA OF A SANKRANTI: The 16 ghatis be-
fore and the 16 ghatis after the Sankranti of the Sun, that is 32 ghatis
is the Punya Kaala or the holy time. If a Sankranti begins before mid-
night then the latter half of the previous day (from 12 o'clock to the
setting of the Sun on the previous day), and if it begins after mid-
night, then the first half of the next day, (that is from the Sunrise to
12 o'clock on the next day) there is the Punya Kaala.

94 Muhurta Chinta Mani

THE CONSIDERATION OF THE SPECIAL PUNYA KAALA
' OF A SANKRANTI: If a Sankranti begins exactly at midnight, then
there is the Punya Kaala of it in both the days before and after it If
there is the Sankranti of the sign of Cancer before the rising of the
Sun, then it is the previous day which is the Punya Kaala; and if
there is that of the sign of Capricorn after the setting of the Sun (in
the night) then the next day is the Punya Kaala.

THE MEASURE OF THE SANDHYA AND THE SPECIAL
CHARACTERISTICS OF THE SANKRANTIS OF CANCER AND
CAPRICORN: 3 ghatis before the half rising of the disc of the Sun
is called the Sandhya (the time joining two times) of the morning;
and 3 ghatis after the half setting of the disc of the Sun is referred
to as the Sandhya of the evening time.

'f there is the Sankranti of Cancer in the Sandhya of the mom-
ing, then there is Punya Kaala in the whole day which follows this
Sandhya; and if there is the Sankranti of the Capricorn in the Sandhya
of the evening, then there is Punya Kaala in the day before it.

Notes: Lf there is the Sankranti of Cancer in the morning
Sandhya, then there remains Punya Kaala in the whole day from the
rising to the setting of the Sun and if there is the Sankranti of Cap-
ricorn in the 3 ghatis after the setting of the Sun (in the evening
Sandhya), then the whole previous day from the rising to the setting
of the Sun remains Punya Kaala.

THE SPECIAL PUNYA KAALA: In the Sankrantis of the signs
of Cancer, Leo, Scorpio, Taurus and Aquarius, the 16 ghatis before

95 The Description of Sankrantis

Sankranti are specially holy and givers of Punya; in those of the signs
of Libra and Aries, it is the 16 ghatis in the middle of the Sankranti
that is, the 8 ghatis before and the 8 ghatis after middle of the
Sankranti time, which are specially holy and givers of Punya: atid in
those of the signs of Gemini, Virgo, Sagittarius, Pisces and Caprtig
corn, it is the 16 ghatis after the Sankranti time which are specially
holy and givers of Punya.

SAAYANA SANKRANTI AND THE PUNYA KAALA IN IT:
If Ayanamsha is multiplied by 60 and the product is divided by the
movement of the longitude of the Sun the quotient which is obtained
in days, ghatis and Palas, so many days before the Sankranti of Ari-
es etc. there is the ‘Chala’ (saayana) Sankranti. In it (Saayana
Sankranti) also the giving of alms in charity and the doing of Tapa
etc. (Penance etc.) gives much Punya as is given by these in the

THE SAMA, BRIHAT AND JAGHANYA NAMES OF THE
NAKSHTRAS: The Nakshtras cailed Mridu or Delicate
(Mrigashira (1), Rewati (2), Chitra (3) and Anuradha (4)), those called
Kshipra (Hasta (5), Ashwini (6), Pushya (7) and Abhijit (8))
Dhanishtha (9), Shravana (10), Kritika (t1), Magha (12),
Poorvaphalguni (13), Poorvashadha (14), Poorvabhadrabada (15) and
Moola (16), these 16 Nakshtras are named as ‘Sama’ Nakshtras. The
Nakshtras called Dhruwa (Uttaraphalguni, Uttarashadha,
Uttarabhadrapada and Rohini), Vishakha and Punarvasa these 6
Nakshtras are called ‘Brihat’ Nakshtras and Ashlesha, Shatbhisha,
Ardra, Swati, Jyeshtha and Bharani, these 6 Nakshtras are referred
to as ‘Jaghanya’ Nakshtras.

Notes: By dividing the 360° of the zodiac (the circular path of
the signs) into 27 equa! parts, there is one part each of 13°-20 or
800 kalas. This is more or less equal to the 800 kalas of the movt-

96 Muhurta Chinta Mani

ment of the Moon the total value of each of the 15 Nakshtras which ;
are called ‘Sama’ is equal to the movement of the Moon in one day.

Therefore they are named as ‘Sama’; the total value of each of those

6 Nakshtras named as ‘Brihat’ is more than the movement of the

Moon, therefore they are named as Brihat; and the total value of each

of those 6 Nakshtras called ‘Jaghanya’ is less than the movement of

the Moon, therefore they are named as Jaghanya Nakshtras.

THE MUHURTAS AND THE EFFECT OF SANKARANTI
ACCORDING TO THE DIFFERENT NAMES OF THE
NAKSHTRAS: There are 15 Muhurtas in a Sankranti if it happens
to occur in the Nakshtras named as ‘Jaghanya’, 45 Muhustas if in
‘Brihat’, and 30 if in ‘Sama’. In the month of the Sankranti occur-
ring in the Nakshtras having 15 Muhurtas the rates of grains etc are .
costly, in that of the Sankranti occurring in the Nakshiras having 45
Muhurtas these are cheap, and in that of the Sankranti occurring in *
the Nakshtras having 30 Muhurtas these are neither costly nor cheap,
these are average.

The same effect prevails in the appearance or rising of the
Moon, that is, if the Moon appears or rises in a Jaghanya Nakshtra
on the 2nd Tithi of the Shukla Paksha or the bright half of the month,
the rates of grains etc are costly in that month, if in a ‘Brihat’
Nakshtra, these are cheap, and if in a ‘Sama’ Nakshtra these are av-
erage, neither cheap nor costly.

THE VIMSHOPAKA PHALA (EFFECT) OF THE YEAR DUE
TO THE SANKRANTI OF CANCER: There are 10 Vimshopakas of
the year if there is the Sankranti of the sign of Cancer on Sunday,
if on Monday, 20; if on Tuesday, 8; if on Wednesday, 12; if on
Thursday, 18; if on Friday, 18; and if on Saturday, 5, that is, in that
year, the measure of auspicious effects is equal to the said viswas.

97 The Descnption of Sankrantis
If a Sankranti happens to occur in the *Karanas’ of Taitila, Naga
and Chatuspada, then at that time the Sun moves in sleeping condi-
tion. In the 5 Karanas from the karana of ‘Gara’ (Gara, Vanija,
Bhadra, Bava, and Balava) the Sun moves in the sitting condition,
and in the karanas of ‘Kinstughana’, Kaulava and Shakuni, the Sun
moves in the standing condition. In these conditions in the tates of
grains etc. and in the falling of rains there occur the effects which
may be regarded respectively as inauspicious, medium and excellent.

(4) Ketaki (5) Bilva or Bela (6) Arka or Madaar or Akwana (7).
Doobha (8) Kamala or Lotus (9) Mallika or Bela (10) Rose (11).
Japa or Adhul flower-these are their flowers.

In this way, in the period in which a Sankranti prevails, there. 4
is the destruction or loss of those clothes, foods, conveyances, weap-
ons, Lepanas, castes and flowers which have been spoken of as be-

- longing to the various Sankrantis or there is 1055 to the people who
eam their livelihood through these clothes, foods, conveyances, weap-
ons, lepanas, castes and flowers.

And if a Sankranti is sitting it destroys those who are sitting,
if it is standing, it destroys those who are standing, and if it is sleep-
ing, it destroys those who are sleeping.

THE AUSPICIOUS OR INAUSPICIOUS EFFECTS TO HU-
MAN BEINGS FROM SANKRANTI ACCORDING TQ THE
NAKSHTRAS IN WHICH THEY ARE BORN: If the birth Nakshtra
falls in the 3 Nakshtras from the Nakshtra which is before the
Nakshtras in which a Sankranti occurs, then in that month there will
be going out (or travelling); then if it falls in the next 6 Nakshtras,
there will be happiness and comfort, then again if it falls in the next
3 Nakshtiras, there will be pain; then if it falls in the next 6 Nakshtras
there will be the obtainment of clothes, again, if it falls in the next
3 Nakshtras, there will be loss of wealth, and if it falls in the next 6
Nakshtras there wili be gains of wealth.

THE THINGS WHICH SHOULD BE DONE WHEN A PLAN-

ET IS STRONG: if the Sun is strong the king should be seen; if
the moon, there should be the reading and teaching of the shastras
- or ancient classics (religious books) if Jupiter, marriage, if Venus,
there should be travelling; and if Saturn, learning (from the guru)

should be taken.

100 Muhurta Chinta Mani

Lf Tara is strong the Moon is auspicious, if the Moon is strong
the Sun is auspicious; and if the Sun is strong, all the planets be-
come strong.

There is the Chandramaasa (ihe Lunar month) from the
Pratipada or the first Tithi of the Shukla Paksha or the bright half
of the month to the Amavasya (the 30th Tithi of a month). If there
is no Sankranti of the Sun in this Chandramaasa then there is the
‘Adhimaasa’ (additional month) and if there are two ‘Sankrantis of
the Sun in it, then there is the ‘Kshaya maasa’ (or lost month). In
the “Kshayamaasa’ the First Maasa (month) and the next maasa should
be taken according to the division of the Tithi. Thus, if the birth or
the death occurs in the First half of the Tithi, then the birth day of
the native and the annual Shraddha of the dead (the anniversary of
the dead) are to be celebrated or performed in the previous month;
and if the birth or the death occurs in the second half of the Tithi, -
then his birthday and his annual Shraddha are to be celebrated or per-
formed in the next month. .

THE AUSPICIOUS AND THE VEDHA PLACES (HOUSES)
FROM THE JANAMA RASHI OR THE BIRTH RASHI: If the Sua
in Gochara or in transit is there in the 6th place or house from the
Janama Rashi of the native (the sign in which the Moon is there at
the time of birth is referred to as the Janama Rashi of the native)
then he is auspicious for him, provided that there is no planet in the
_ 12th House, that is, if there is some pianet in the 12th House, thete
is ‘Vedha’ to the Sun and then the Sun posited in the 6th House be-
(gk

105 “Gochara Prakamam’ The Planets in Transit

5. DIFFERENCE OF OPINION WITH REGARD TO VEDHA:
The opinion of Narada and the other Acharyas (teachers of Astrolo-
gy) is that the Vedha of the planets is from the Janama Rashi (Thus,
if the Sun is in the 6th House from the Janama Rashi and if there is
some planet in the 12th House from it, then the Sun has Vedha from
that planet). But the opinion of Acharaya Kashyap is that Vedha is
from the sign of that very planet, that is when the Sun is in the 6th
House, there is Vedha if there is some planet in the 12th House from
the Sun, or when the Sun is in the 12th House, there is Vedha if
there is some planet in the 6th House from the Sun.

According to Acharya Kashyap, both these kinds of Vedha are
there in the region between the Himalayas and the Vindhya Moun-
tains (that is, in the Northern India). This Vedha is not defective in
other places.

THE EFFECTS OF AN ECLIPSE AS PER THE JANAMA
NAKSHTRA AND THE JANAMA RASHI: If an eclipse occurs in
the Janama (birth) Nakshtra there occurs the death of the native, if
il occurs in the Janama Rashi, the native gets bodily pain, in the 2nd,
sign loss; in the 3rd, obtainment of Lakshmi or wealth; in the 4th,
mental pain; in the Sth anxiety, in the 6th, happiness; in the 7th, pain
to the native’s wife, in the 8th, there occurs the death of the native;
in the 9th, there is the loss of respect; in the 10th, the native gets
happiness; in the 11th, gains and, in the 12th, destruction or loss of
wealth, These are the effects of an eclipse. In the eclipse which gives
inauspicious effects, the native should recite Mantras, give Cow, gold
as alms or in charity, and should do Japa (or the repeated recitation

106 Muhurta Chinta Mani

of a mantra) for pacification, or, it is the opinion of the other
Acharyas that the eclipse which gives inauspicious effects should not
be seen.

SPECIAL EFFECTS DUE TO THE STRENGTH OF THE
MOON: If the Moon is hemmed between Malefic planets or if she
is in conjunction with a Malefic Planet or if she is in the 7th place
from a Malefic Planet then inspite of being auspicious she gets or
becomes inauspicious; And if she is in the Navamsha of a Benefic
Planet or of her extreme friend and is aspected by Jupiter, then inspite
of being inauspicious she gets or becomes auspicious.

Notes: Here it is the Moon who should be regarded as the main
auspicious effects-giving planet. indeed, it is not only the Moon, but
every planet, becomes inauspicious if he is hemmed between Malefic
Planets; and becomes auspicious if he is in the Navamsha of a
Benefic Planet or of his extreme friend and is aspected by a Benefic
Planet

THE STRENGTH OF THE MOON AS PER THE DIFFER-
ENCE OF SHUKLA AND KRISHNA PAKSHA: If the Moon is aus-
picious in the beginning of the Shukla Paksha or the Bright half of
the month (in Pratipada etc.) and if she is inauspicious in the begin-
ning of the Krishna Paksha or the Dark haif of the month (in the
16th Tithi etc), then both the Pakshas are auspicious. But this strength
of the Moon should be considered only at the time of emergency or
of trouble, and not at any other time.

107 “Gochara Prakarnam’ The Planets in Transit

THE WEARING OF GEMS AND DIAMONDS FOR THE
PACIFICATION OF THE DEFECTS ARISING FROM A MALEFIC
OR INAUSPICIOUS PLANET IN GOCHARA: A ring made. of gold
is to be divided into 9 parts. In this ring the native should get stud-
ded a diamond in the eastern part for the pacification of Venus; Pearl
in the Agneya Kona (East South) for that of the Moon; Coral in the
Southern part for that of Mars; Gaumeda in the Nairiritya Kona (West
South) for that of Rahu; Neelam or Sapphire in the Western part for
that of Saturn; Vaidurya Mani or Cat’s eye in the Vayavya Kona
(West North) for that of Ketu; Pukharaj or Topaz in the Norther
part for that of Jupiter, Panna, or emerald in the Ishana Kona (East
North) for that of Mercury and; Manikya or Ruby in the middle part
for that of the Sun. This ring is to be worn by the native.

Notes: This ‘Yantra’ is referred to as ‘Nava Graha Yantra’ or
a yantra for the pacification of the nine planets.

of Jupiter, diamond for that of Venus, Sapphire for that of Saturn,
Gaumeda for that of Rahu, and Cat's eye for that of Ketu. Gold

THE SADHANA OR CALCULATION OF THE AVASTHAS
OF THE MOON: The number of the passed Nakshtra (counting from
Ashwini) is to be multiplied by 60. To this should be added the
passed ghatis of the present Nakshtra. Then it is to be multiplied by
4 and the product should be divided by 45. the quotient is again to
be divided by 12, The remainder is the avastha of the Moon in the
order of the 12 signs Aries etc.

Example: Suppose the passed Nakshtra is Hasta. Its number
from Ashwini is 13. If it is multiplied by 60, the product comes to
be 780. To 780 should be added the 10 Bhukta or passed ghatis of
the present Nakshtras of Chitra, the resultant figure comes to be 790
which is again multiplied by 4 and the product comes to be 3160. It
is divided by 45, and the quotient comes to be 70. If it is divided
by 12, the remainder comes to be 10. If the native’s Rashi or sign
is Aries. then the Avastha of the Moon is 10 which is ‘jwara’ (fe-
ver)-see the speculum of the Avasthas of the Moon.

THE 12 AVASTHAS OF THE MOON AND THEIR EF-
. FECTS: (1) Pravasa, 2. Nasha 3. Marana 4. Jaya 5. Hasya 6. Rati 7.
Kreeda 8. Supta 9. Bhukta 10. Jwara 11. Kampa 12. Sthirta. These
are the Avasthas of the Moon in the order of the signs of Aries etc.;
that is, the Moon’s Avastha in the case of the native having the Rashi
or sign of Aries is Pravasa etc. that of having the Rashi of Taurus
is Nasha etc. that of having the Rashi of Gemini is Marana etc,, that
of having the Rashi of Cancer is Jaya and so on. The effects of these:
Avastha are in accordance with their names.
