CHAPTER 5
THE SAMSAKARAS

AUSPICIOUS TIME FOR THE FIRST APPEARANCE OF
THE MENSES: The first appearance of women’s menses is auspi-
cious in the Shukla Paksha of the 7 months of Magha, Margashirsha,
Baisakha, Ashwina, Phalguna, Jyeshtha and Shravana; on the auspi-
cious days (Monday, Wednesday, Thursday and Friday); in an aus-
picious Ascendant and in the day time.

EXCELLENT, MEDIUM AND LOW NAKSHTRAS FOR THE
FIRST APPEARANCE OF WOMEN'S MENSES: The first appear-
ance of women’s menses is auspicious if it occurs in the 15 Nakshtras
of Shravana, Dhanishtha, Shatabhisha, the Nakshtras named as Mridu
or Delicate (Mrigashira, Rewati, Chitra, Anuradha) those named as
Kshipra (Hasta, Ashwini and Pushya), those named as Dhruwa
(Uttaraphalguni, Uttarabhadrapada, Uttarashadha and Rohini) and
Swati and on white (clean) clothes. In the 5 Nakshtras of Moola,
Punarvasu, Magha, Vishakha and Kritika, the first appearance of
women’s menses is medium. And it (the first appearance of wom-
en’s menses) is low or base in the other remaining 7 Nakshtras
“(Bharani, Ardra, Ashlesha, Poorvaphalguni Jyeshtha, Poorvashadha
and Poorvabhadrapada).

il4 Muhurta Chinta Mani

THE TIME WHICH SHOULD BE REJECTED IN THE FIRST
APPEARANCE OF WOMEN’S MENSES: The first appearance of
women’s menses is not auspicious on the Bhadra Tithis (described
in the 43rd Shloka of chapter 1); during sleep, on the day of a
Sankranti, on the Tithis of Amavasya (the 30th Tithi) and Rikta (4/
9/14), in the evening time, on the Tithis of Shashthi, Dwadashi and
Ashtami (6/12/8) in the Vaidhriti yoga, in the diseased condition, dur-
ing the period of the eclipses of the Sun and the Moon and in the
Yoga of Vyatipaata. The meaning is that if there is the first appear-
ance of women’s menses in these inauspicious times there come ob-
stacles in the happiness arising from the birth of issues. Therefore it
is auspicious if its pacification is done.

THE MUHURTA FOR THE BATHING OF WOMEN AFTER
MENSES: The woman having menses should be asked to take bath
after menses in the Nakshtras of Hasta, Swati, Ashwini, Mrigashira,
Anuradha, Dhanishtha, those named as Dhruwa (all the 3 Uttaras and
Rohini) and Jyestha, on the auspicious Tithis (2/3/5/7/11/12/13), on
the auspicious days (Monday, Wednesday, Thursday and Friday). The
woman who takes bath after menses in the Nakshtras of Mrigashira,
Rewati, Swati, Hasta, Ashwini and Rohini gets pregnant or conceives
soon.

Notes: The customs prevailing in a country or region are also
taken into consideration in the bathing of a woman after menses. At
some place a woman takes bath four days after the beginning of the
menstrual cycle. Here the days which are prescribed as rejected are
not considered for cleaning or bathing. Manw has said. “  
 " that is, the woman becomes worthy to be touched by her

115 The Samsakaras

husband after 4 days (of the beginning of menses). At some places
a woman having menses is allowed to take bath only when there is
an auspicious day and an auspicious Nakshtra. This is the most proper
time of bathing.

THE TIME WHICH IS TO BE REJECTED IN HAVING SEX-
UAL INTERCOURSE FOR CONCEPTION: The husband should
teave all the three gandantas (the Tithi, the Nakshtra, and the Lagna
Gandanta), the Vadha and Janama Tara (that is, 7/10/16/19/25th
Nakshtras from the birth or Janama Nakshtra), the Nakshtras of Moola
Bharani, Ashwini, Rewati and Magha, the time of the eclipse of the
Sun and the Moon, the ‘Yogas’ of Vyatipata and Vaidhriti, the days
of the shraddha of the Father and the Mother (the days of their death),
the day time, the first half of the Parigha Yoga, the Nakshtra troubled
by Utpaata (portent), the Lagna (Ascendant) of the 8th Rashi or sign
from the ‘Janama Rashi’ or sign of birth, the Lagna of a Malefic
Planet (1/5/8/10/11) for doing sexual intercourse with his wife after
the period of menses has expired, and she has bathed.

FURTHER THINGS WHICH SHOULD BE KNOWN ABOUT
THE MUHURTA OF CONCEPTION: The Karana of Bhadra (de-
scribed in 1/43), Shashthi (the 6th Tithi), the days of festivals, the
Rikta Tithis (4/9/14), the evening time, the days of Tuesday, Sunday
and Saturday and the 4 nights from the beginning of the menstrual
discharge-these should be left for sexual intercourse which is meant
for conception.

Sexual intercourse (for conception) is auspicious in. the
Nakshtras of Uttaraphalguni, Uttarashadha, Uttarabhadrapada,

THE MUHURTA FOR SIMANTA SAMSKARA: The perfprm-
ing or doing of the Samskara wnich is named as Simanta is. angpi-
cious on the days of Thursday, Sunday, and Tuesday, in the Nukehtras
of Mnigashira, Pushya, Moola, Shravana, Punarvasu and Hasta; on the.
other Tithis except those of the Rikta Tithis, the Amavasya, the 12
the 6th and the 8th Tithis, (that is on the 1/2/3/5/7/10/11/13 Tithis);
when Masapati or the lord of the Month of conception is endowed
with strength (since the month in which the deed of conception is
performed to the 9th Month, there is the Masapati or Lord of the
Month for every month); in the 6th or 8th month from the concep-
tion; when the Benefic Planets are in the Angles and Trines; when
the Malefic Planets are in the 3/6/11 Houses and when there is the
Ascendant and the Navamsha of a Male sign. Or the Simanta
Samskara is excellent in the Nakshtras named as Dhruwa (the 3
Uttaras and Rohini) and Rewati, on auspicious days and when there
is the Ascendant and the Navamsha of a male sign.

Notes: The word ‘Samskara’ or ‘Sanskara’ means the changing
of the shape of something, giving it a new shape or form. The rites
or deeds described in the ‘shrutis’ and ‘smritis’ (the religious books
which were heard and remembered) by which man is purified (of
sins) are spoken of as ‘Samskaras’. The name of the invisible deed
giving specific significance which is done by performing special
Vedic deeds, ordinary and exceptional in one’s own caste and place,
has been said to be ‘Samskara’. In this chapter the word ‘Samskara’
has been used for the invisible specific meaning, for the suitable or
favourable day, Nakshtra etc. The Indian Manisha (thinkers) have en-
gaged their attention in the search of absolute happiness or bliss and
they have found it in Moksha (or relief from the cycle of birth and
death). Therefore, the deeds by which man is cleaned of his sins and
becomes a rightful claimant of Nirwana or Moksha Pada are also
called ‘Samskaras’. The Sage ‘Charaka’ has said ' 
' that is, ‘Samskara’ is the name of removing the evil
qualities which are already there in man and bearing good qualities
in their place. From this point of view Samskara is the planning of
the new building of man. According to the Indian Munis (thinkers)
Samskaras are 48, but of these it is only the 16 that are specially
performed

The Names of the 16 Samskaras: i. Garbhadhan (conception)
2. Punsavana 3. Simant 4. Jaata Karma 5. Naama Karana (christen-

118 Muhurta Chinta Mani

ing or naming) 6. Nishkramana (going out of the house) 7. Anna
Prashana (the first eating of grains) 8. Vapan 9. Karnavedha 10.
Vratadesha 11. Vedarambha 12. Keshanta [3. Snaan 14. Vivaha or
marriage 15. Agni Paripraha 16. Tretagni Sangraha.

Sushruta (an authority on Indian Medicines) has written in
Chapter 6 entitled as ‘Shareer Sthaana’ or the various parts of the
‌ " that is, the head is divided into 5 Sandhis (joints);
if these are hurt or injured, if there is lunacy if there is fear and be-
cause of not moving man dies. Due to the Sandhis (joints) of the
skull of the head, it is called Simanta. There is the brain in the head
with which we think and meditate. SMANTO-NNAYANA {It is an-
other name of the Simanta Samskara) means the elevation or the up-
ward rising of the brain or the development of the brain. Therefore,
the meaning of the SIMANTO-NNAYANA Samsakara means that
Samsakara in which the mind of the mother is concentrated on the
development of the brain. “Punsavana Samskara’ is the Samskara of
the development of, the body, while ‘Simantonnayana Samskara that
of the mind. In these two Samskaras is included the bodily and the
mental development, everything relating to the foetus in the womb.

It is said that the ‘Simantonnayana Samskara should be per-
formed in the 4th or the 6th or the 8th month after conception. It is
clear from this is that the best time for the Simantonnayana Samskara
is when the cells of the brain of the foetus in the womb are build-
ing. It is written in the ‘Shareer Sthaana* Chapter of Sushruta:

that is, in the 5th month (after conception), it is the soul which be-
comes more conscious, in the 6th one, the intellect; in the 7th one,
the various organs and limbs become more pronounced, in the 8th
one, ‘oja’ remains unstable; the infant born in the 8th month does
not remain alive because of the lack of ‘oja’. From this statement, it
is clear that, as Sushruta opines, in the Sth month (after conception)
the soul of the infant in the womb becomes more conscious than it
was before this time. “It becomes more conscious than it was before”

119 The Samsakaras

means this that in the 4th month the Nervous system begins to take
shape or is built. It is because of this that the, time of
‘Simantonnayana Samskara’ has been chosen to be in the 4th month
In the chapter 1/14/1 of ‘Ashwalayana Grihya Sutra’, there is the say-
ing “  ‌ ” that is there should be the comple-
tion of the Simantonnayanam Samskara in the 4th month. Sushruta
has written: “9  that is, in the 6th month there begins the
development of the intellect of the infant in the womb. Therefore, it
is the 6th month also which has been prescribed for the doing of this
samskara.

In the 8th month (after conception) the woman is referred to
as Dohrada. ‘Dohrada’ means one who has 2 Hearts - one heart of
the woman’s own, and the other of the infant in her womb. In the
8th month after conception, the body, the brain and the heart-all these
three are fully prepared. Therefore, if the Simantonnayana Samskara
has not been done in the 4th month, it may be done in the 6th month,
and if it has not been done in the 6th one also it may be done in
the 8th one.

THE LORDS OF THE 10 MONTHS OF CONCEPTION AND
THE DETERMINION OF THE STRENGTH OF THE MOON: The
Lord of the first month of conception is Venus; of the 2nd, Mars,
of the 3rd, Juptier, of the 4th, the Sum; of the Sth, the Moon; of the
6th, Saturn; of the 7th, Mercury, of the 8th, the Ascendant Lord; of
the 9th, the Moon; and of the 10th, the Sun.

In the Samskaras of marriage and conception ctc., it is the
Chandra Bala or the strength of the Moon of the women which is to
be considered; and in the other Samskaras, of their husbands’.

120 Muhurta Chinta Mani

THE MUHURTA FOR THE PUNSAVANA SAMSKARA AND
VISHNU POOJA: After ‘Garbhadhaana’ or Conception the
‘Punsavana Samskara’ should be performed in the 3rd month on the
Tithis, days and in the Nakshtras which have been stated as auspi-
cious for the ‘Simanta Samskara’. And after this in the &th month,
in the Nakshtras of Shravana, Rohini and Pushya, in auspicious
Lagans or Ascendants or when there is a benefic planet in auspicious
Ascendants, and when the 8th House from the Natal Ascendant is
auspicious and is without any Malefic Planet, the worship of Lord
Vishau should be performed (for the protection of conception).

Notes: The foetus which is there in the womb of the mother
develops in two directions. First, there is the bodily development and
secondly, the mental development. For the physical or bodily devel-
opment there is the ‘Punsavana Samskara’. By this Samskara, the at-
tention of the mother is drawn towards the bodily development of
the foetus which is being nourished in her womb. Therefore, this
Samskara is performed after conception when it becomes certain that
the conception has been stabilised.

THE MUHURTA FOR THE JAATAKARMA AND THE
“NAAMA KARANA’ SAMSKARAS: It is auspicious to perform the
‘Jaata Karma’ and the ‘Naama Karana’ samskaras of the newly born
infant on the other Tithis except in the Parva or festival (14/8/30/15/
the Sankranti of the Sun) and the Rikta Tithis (4/9/14), on an auspi-
cious day, on the 11th or the 12th day from the birth, in the
Anuradha), in those named as Dhniwa (the 3 Uttaras and Rohini), in
those named as Kshipra (Hasta, Ashwini and Pushya) and in those
named as ‘Chara’ or Movable (Swati, Punarvasu, Shravana,
Dhanishtha and Shatabhisha, in these 16 Nakshtras.

Notes: The ‘Jaata Karma’ is a post natal ‘Samskara’. After the
birth of the infant there are certain necessary things to be done such
as cleaning the mouth, the nose of the infant, make it bathe etc. Af-
ter these things certain Samskaras are also performed, the aim of
which is to instil in the infant certain samskaras and habits though

121 The Sarnsakaras

the infant itself is not conscious of them and learns them unknow-
ingly. These Samskaras are as follows:

1, To write ‘Om’ on the infant’s tongue with a thin 100 of gold:
This is done by the father and the mother in order to instil in the
infant without its knowledge certain Samskaras. When the father
writes ‘Om’ on the infant's tongue, he takes this responsibility upon
himself that he will make his progeny the traveller travelling on the
path of Adhyatma or spirituality and will not leave him free in ma-

2. To say ‘Vedosi’ in the ear of the infant. The meaning of
‘Vedosi’ is “you are a creature having knowledge of the Absolute,
you are not ignorant”. After the Jaata Karma Samskara, the 5th is
the ‘Naamakarana’ Samskara or the Christening or naming ceremo-
ny. The ‘Naamakarana is generally very auspicious if if is done in
the forenoon, medium in auspiciousness if it is done at noon. It should
not be done in the afternoon and in the night.

The name of the infant should be in one of the 5 ways.

1. The name may be one relating to the ‘Kuldevata’ (the fami-
ly god, or the god who is worshipped most in the family).

2. There has been the tradition of naming the infant on the ba-
sis of the names of the months which begin with Margashirsha and
are these in order 1. Krishna 2. Ananta 3. Achyuta 4. Chakradhara
5. Vaikuntha 6. Janardana 7. Upendra , Yagyapurusha 9. Vasudeva
10. Hari 11. Yogisha 12. Pundari Kanksha (These are the names of
the months beginning with Margashirsha).

3. The name may be on the charans or quarters of the
Nakshtras. This is called “Rashinaama’

4. The actual name (that which is spoken easily) is excellent if
it is of two, four or six letters.

5. The Sth is the pet name. It should be sober, sweet and deli-
cate and it is excellent if it is according to the parent’s wish.

122 Muhurta Chinta Mani

THE MUHURTA FOR THE BATHING OF THE PRASUTI
WOMAN (the woman who has been delivered of an infant): The
bathing of the Prasuti woman has been spoken of as excellent in the
10 Nakshtras, of Rewati, those named as Dhruwa (the 3 Uttaras and
Rohini) Mrigashira, Hasta, Swati, Ashwini and Anuradha and on the
days of Sunday, Tuesday and Thursday. the ‘Sutika Snaana’ or the
bathing of the Prasuti woman is not auspicious in the 10 Nakshtras,
of Ardra, Punarvasu, Pushya, Shravana, Magha, Bharani, those named’
as Mishra or Mixed (Vishakha and Kritika) Moola and Chitra, on the
days of Wednesday and Saturday and on the Tithis of Astami (8th),
Shashthi (6th), Dwadashi (12th) and Rikta Tithis (4/9/14). The Sutika
Snaana is medium in auspiciousness in the remaining 7 Nakshtras of
Ashlesha, Poorvaphalguni Jyestha, Poorvashadha, Dhanishtha,
Poorvabhadrapada and on the days of Monday and Friday.

THE EFFECTS OF THE TEETHING OF A CHILD: If the
child gets teeth in the Ist month after its birth, that child itself gets
destroyed; if in the 2nd Month, it destroys the younger brother, if in
the 3rd month, the sister, if in the 4th month, the mother, if in the
5th month, the elder brother, if in the 6th month, it gets extreme hap-
piness; if in the 7th month, it gets happiness and comforts from the
father, if in the 8th month, the child gets strong; if in the 9th ruonth,
it becomes wealthy, and if in the 10th month, it remains happy in
life. If the child is born with teeth it brings destruction to its own
self, to the father and the mother or if after the birth it gets the up-
per teeth in the prohibited period, it brings destruction to the father
etc.

123 The Samsakares

14. THE MUHURTA FOR THE CRADLING OF THE CHILD.
(Swinging of the infant in the cradle for the first time). The 5
Nakshatras from the Nakshatra of the Sun (the Nakshatra in which
the Sun is there) at the time when the child is swung for the first
time in the cradle are health-giving for the child. After this the 5
Nakshatras are death-bringing. After this in the 5 Nakshatras the child
becomes weak and lean. After this in the 5 Nakshatras, the child gets
diseased and lastly in the last 7 Nakshatras it gets happiness.

THE MUHURTA FOR THE DOLAROHAN (Swinging the
child in a cradle) AND TAKING THE CHILD OUT OF THE
HOUSE: The Dolarohana of the child is auspicious in any of the
days, 32, 12, 16, 18 and 10 from the day of his birth, on auspicious
days (Monday, Wednesday, Thursday and Friday), in the Nakshtras
named as Mridu or Delicate (Mrigashira, Rewati, Chitra and
Anuradha), in those named as Laghu (Hasta, Ashwini and Pushya)
and those named as Dhriwa (the 3 Utearas and Rohini) - in these
11 Nakshtras.

It is auspicious to bring the child out of the house for the first
time in the 4th Month from the Month of the Birth and on the Tithis
and days which have been spoken of as auspicious for travelling. Or
going of the child out of house is auspicious on the 12th day from
the day of birth.

Notes: The Tithis, days and Nakshtras are to be considered even
on the 12th day also from the day of the birth.

124 Muhurta Chinta Mani

THE MUHURTA FOR THE JALA POOJANA OF THE
PRASUTI WOMAN: The Prasuti woman should not do ‘Jala Poojana
(worship of water) in the event of Venus and Jupiter being combust,
and in the month of Chaitra, in a Mala Maasa (additional month) and
in the month of Pausha although one month might have been com-
pletéd since the time of delivery (A woman performs Jala Poojana
or worship of water one month after delivery). The Prasuti woman
should perform the ceremony of Jala Poojana on the days of Wednes-
day, Monday and Thursday, on other Tithis than those of Rikta (4/
9/14) and in the Nakshtras of Shravana, Pushya, Punarvasu,
Mrigashira, Hasta, Moola and Anuradha.

THE MUHURTA FOR ANNA PRASHANA (The eating of
grains for the first time): The Anna-Prashana of male children is aus-
picious in the even months 6th etc, (6/8/10/12) and that of the fe-
male children is auspicious in the odd months Sth etc (5/7/9/11} in
the 16 Nakshtras, in those Nakshtras that are named as Mridu or Del-
icate (Mrigashira, Rewati, Chitra and Anuradha), in those named as
Laghu or small (Hasta, Ashwini and Pushya); those named as Chara
or Movable (Swati, Punarvasu, Shravana, Dhanishtha and Shatabhisha)
and those named as Sthira or Stable (the 3 Uttaras and Rohini). In
the Anna Prashana, the Tithis named as Rikta (4/9/14) and those
named as Nanda (1/6/11), the Tithis of Astami (8th), of Amavasya
(30th) and the Dwadashi (12th), the days of Saturday, Tuesday and
Sunday, the Lagnas or Ascendants of the Navamsha of the 8th sign
from the Janama Rashi and Janama Lagna and the Ascendants of Pi-
sces, Aries and Scorpio are to be left out (that is, Anna Prashana
samskara should not be done in these Nakshtras, etc.)

126 Muhorte Chinta Mani

windy discases; and if Venus is there the child, male or female, en-
joys various kinds of foods and pleasures.

THE MUHURTA FOR THE SEATING OF THE CHILD ON
THE GROUND : Making it (the child} wear ‘Kati Sutra’ the child
should be seated on the ground after worshipping in the 5th Month
the Earth and the God Varaha, when Mars is strong, on the other
Tithis than those named as Riktas (4/9/14), in a Chara or Movable
Lagna or Ascendant, on auspicious days and in the Nakshtras named
as Sthira or Dhruwa (the 3 Uttaras and Rohini) and in those of
Mrigashira Jyeshtha, Anuradha, and in those named as Laghu or small
(Hasta, Ashwini and Pushya).

THE DETERMINATIONN OF THE MEANS OF LIVING OF
THE CHILD: Seating the child on the ground put before it many
things such as clothes, weapon, book, pen, gold and silver. The child
will earn his bread or livelihood by that thing which it takes of its
own will or desire.

127 The Samsakeras

THE MUHURTA FOR EATING TAAMBOOL OR BETEL
LEAF: It is better to make the child eat betel leaf at the age of 2.1/
2 months or at the time of Anna Prashana, on other days than those
of Tuesday and Saturday, in the Nakshtras named as Dhnuwa (the 3
Uttaras and Rohini) in those named as Mridu or Delicate (Mrigashira,
Rewati, Chitra and Anuradha) in those named as Laghu or small
(Hasta Ashwini and Pushya), in those of Shravana, Moola, Punarvasu,
Jyeshtha, Swati and Dhanishtha, in these 17 Nakshtras, in the Ascen-
dants of Gemini, Capricorn, Virgo, Aquarius, Tauris and Pisces, in
the event of the Benefic Planets being in the Angles and the Trines
and of the Malefic Planets being in 3/6/11 Houses.

THE MUHURTA FOR KARNA VEDHA OR MAKING HOLE
IN THE EARS FOR WEARNING JEWELS: ‘The Karna Vedha
Samskara’ is excellent if it is done leaving, the two months of Chaitra
and Pausha, the sleeping time of Lord Vishnu (From Ashadha Shukla
Ekadashi or the 11th Tithi of the bright half of the Month of Ashadha
to the Kartika Shukla Ekadashi), the month of the birth, the Tithis
named as Rikta (4/9/14), even years (2/4 etc) and the Janama Tara
(1/10/11 Nakshtra from the Janama or birth Nakshtra, ail these-and
in the 6/7/8/ month from birth or on the 12/16 day from the day of
birth, on the days of Wednesday, Thursday, Friday and Monday, in
the odd (3/5 etc} years, in the Nakshtras of Shravana, Dhanishtha,
Punarvasu, those named as Mridu or Delicate (Mrigashira, Rewati and
Anuradha and those named as Laghu or small (Hasta, Ashwini and
Pushya).

Notes: The ‘Kama Vedha Samskara’ of children should be per-
formed on the 12th or the 16th day from the day of birth after con-
sulting favourable Tithi-Day-and Nakshtra. If it is not possible to do
it on these days then it should be done in the 6th or the 7th Maath.
If again it is not possible to do it in these months also, then it should.
be done in the 3rd or the Sth year.

128 Muhurta Chinta Mani

The Chaitra etc. lunar months in which the child is born is
called the ‘Janama Maasa’ or the month of birth. According to some
Acharyas the one month extending from the day of birth to the 30th
day is regarded as the Janama Maasa.

‘LAGNA SHUDDHI’ OR A FAVOURABLE ASCENDANT IN
*KARNA VEDHA’: ‘Karna Vedha Samskara’ is auspicious when the
8th House from the Natal Ascendant is without any Planet in it,
Benefic Planets are in the Angles, Trines and in the 3/11 Houses,
when there is an Ascendant of the signs of Venus or Jupiter (2/7/9/
12) when the Malefic Planets are in the 3/6/13 Houses from the Na- ,
tal Ascendant and Jupiter is in the Ascendant.

THE TIME WHICH SHOULD BE REJECTED IN DOING
AUSPICIOUS DEEDS: It is not auspicious and excellent to estab-
lish the image of a god (or goddess) or to build a tank, to celebrate
a marriage, to do ‘Agni Adhaana’ (to use fire in auspicious deeds),
to do entry in a newly built house, to do ‘Mundana’ or the first cut-
ting of a child’s hair, to do coronation and to wear “Yagyopaveeta
or Janeu’ (sacred thread worn by the Hindus) - all these things - in
the Dakshinayana of the Sun, in the childhood, old age and the Com-
bust condition of Jupiter and Venus and at the time of the rising of
Ketu: Some Acharyas opine that till one Paksha (15 days) or half a
Paksha (7 days) after the rising of Ketu, the above stated auspicious
deeds are not auspicious And according to some other Acharya since

129 The Samsakaras

the rising of Ketu and as long as it remains visible, auspicious deeds
should not be done.

Notes: The rising of Ketu cannot be known through mathemat-
ical calculations. Ketus are of three kinds:

1) Divya (born in the sky)

2) Antariksha (born in a different place from the place of ‘the
' Planets and the Nakshtras)

3) Bhauma (born on the earth)

The rising and setting of Ketus cannot be known through Math-
ematical calculations because of their being in the nature of sudden
happening. The number of Ketus is not fixed. Acharya Varahamihira
has given much information about Ketus in his book entitled “Brihat |
Samhita

THE CHILDHOOD AND OLD AGE OF JUPITER AND VE-
NUS : After the rising of Venus in the east there is her childhood
for 3 days and after her rising in the West there is her childhood
for 10 days. And 15 days before her setting in the East and 5 days
before her setting in the West there is her old age.

Jupiter has his childhood for 15 days after his rising in the east
and in the west both, and he has his old age for 15 days before his
setting in the east and in the west both.

Notes: As long as the difference between the Sun and Jupiter
remains below 11° (eleven degrees), so long because of his hiding
in the rays of the Sun he (Jupiter) is called Combust. And as long
as the difference between the Sun and Venus remains below 9° (nine
degrees} so long because of the aforesaid reason about Juptier she
(Venus) is also called combust. Before their being combust there is
the old age of Jupiter and Venus because in these days they are de-
void of their brilliance. And after their rising there is increase in their
brilliance gradually. Therefore because of their being devoid of bril-
liance for some day's after rising there is their childhood.

130 Muhurta Chinta Mani

28. DIFFERENCE OF OPINION WITH REGARD TO THE
CHILDHOOD AND THE OLD AGE (OF JUPITER AND VENUS):
Some Acharyas say that there are 10 days for each of the childhood
and of the old age respectively after and before the rising and set-
ling of Jupiter and Venus. And some have said that this time is only
of 7 days. And some Acharyas have said that for the Muhurta of
some extremely important work the time of these stages is only 3
days.

The Moon remains a child for half day after rising and she re-
mains old for 3 days before setting.

Notes: The aforesaid differences seem to have come into being
because of keeping the necessity of the Muhurta in mind. 1 should
be accpeted that different Customs prevail in the different regions of
the country with regard to the childhood and old age of Jupiter and
Venus. Gargacharya has referred to this fact in the shloka given be-
low:

fates  NH

that is, before and afier or on both the sides the childhood and old
age of Jupiter and Venus should be regarded as 10 days in the plac-
es near the Vindhyachala Mountains, 7 days in Ujjain, 6 days in Ben-
gal, 5 days in Huna region and 3 days in the other provinces.

132 Muhurta Chinta Mani

THE DETERMINATION OF MINDANA ON THE MOTH-
ER’S BEING PREGNANT: If the conception of the mother of the
child whose Mundana Samskara is to be done is of more than 4
months duration or time, if is not proper to do Mundana and such
Mundana should be probhibited, and if the child is of more than 5
years of age then Mundana is auspicious even in the event of the
mother’s being pregnant ‍

EXCEPTION OF MALEFIC TARA IN DOING MUNDANA:
If at the time of Mundana the Moon is in a Trine, in her own exal-
tation sign or in the Varga of her friendly planets or in her own di-
visional chart, then inspite of the Tara’s being malefic (the 1/3/5/7th
Tara) the Mundana is auspicious. If the Moon in transit being auspi-
cious is in the sign of a Benefic Planet, then even the Malefic Tara
becomes auspicious in such things as Mundana, travelling etc.

THE TIME WHICH IS TO BE REJECTED IN THE
MUNDANA OF THE ELDER SON: If the child’s mother is having
menses or is Prasutika (one who is recently delivered of a child) then
the Samskaras like ‘Mundana’ etc should not be performed; and the
*Mundana’ of the eldest son should not be done in the month of
Jyestha (roughly falling in May/June) According to some Acharya
(Acharya Bharadwaja) the Mundana etc should not be done in the
month of Margashirsha (roughly falling in Noverneber/December) also.

133 The Samsakaras

- THE MUHURTA FOR KSHORA-KARMA (making the barber
cut the beard moustache and the hair of the head). It is auspicious
to clean the teeth, to get ‘Kshora Karma’ done and to get the nails
cut on the tithis, days and the Nakshtras which have been stated as
proper and auspicious for the Muhurta of Mundana. But ‘Kshora-
Karma’ etc. should be left and should not be done on the days of
Saturday, Tuesday and Sunday, on the 9th day from the previous
Kshora, in the evening time, on the Tithis named’ as Rikta (4/9/14)
on the Tithis of Parva and in the night. One who wants his well be-
ing and his benefit should not clean his teeth, should not do Kshora
Karma and should not cut his nails without ‘aasana’ {or mat) for sit-
ting, during war on the battlefield, in some (other) village, on the
day of travelling, after having bath, after oiling and after having taken
meal.

Notes: The doing of ‘Kshora Karma’ on the auspicious days has
been said to be longevity increasing and the doing of it on inauspi-
cious days is harmful for longevity.

On the 9th day from the previous ‘Kshora Karma’ another
‘Kshora Karma’ should not be done. Thus if Kshora Karma was done
on Wednesday, another such Karma should not be done on the $th
day from Wednesday, that is on Thursday. While doing Kshora
Karma there should be the use of rug or mat etc.

If there is necessity of it, Brahmins may do Kshora Karma on
Sunday, Kshtriyas on Tuesday and Vaishyas and shudras on Satur-
day.

The consideration of time has not been said with regard to the
Raaj-Karmacharis (Persons in the king’s or government service).

SPECIAL TIME FOR KSHORA KARMA: With the permission
of a Brahmin or of the king to take part in a yajna, at the time of

134 . Muburta Chinta Mani

the death of father and mother and on the occasion of relief of a
prisoner from the prison, Kshora-Karma may be done on those days
which have been rejected for such Karma. The man whose wife is
pregnant should not do the carrying of a dead body to the crema-
tion-place, the travelling to a place of pilgrimage or 0 a foreign coun-
try, bathing in the sea and Kshora Karma.

. fed ar A waged

Of the afore-said Nakshatras used for shaving purposes, the king
should shave only after five days of his previous shave. If on the
fifth day those Nakshtras are not found available then one should
shave in the muhurta of shaving Nakshtra, as it is considered auspi-
cious.

If a person repeatedly does Kshora-Karma within.a year, 6 times
in the Nakshtras of Kritika, 3 times in that of Anuradha, 8 times in
that of Rohini, 4 times in that of Magha and 4 times in that of
Uttaraphalguni, he is subjected to death, that is, Kshora Karma should
not be done in these Nakshtras.

Notes: Muhurtas of Nekshtras are said in Chapter No. 6 and
sloka No. 52-53.

THE MUHURTA FOR THE BEGINNING OF LEARNING
LETTERS BY CHILDREN: In the  year from the birth after mak-
ing the child worship Lord Ganesha, Lord Vishnu, goddess Saraswati,
and goddess Laxmi, it (the child) should be taught the writing of the

135 The Samsakaras

letters or it should be given the knowledge of the letters of the al-
phabet on the Tithis of 11,12,10,2,6,5, and 3, in the Nakshtras named
as Laghu or smal! (Hasta, Ashwini and Pushya), in those of Shravana
Swati, Rewati, Punarvasu, Ardra, Chitra and Anuradha, in the auspi-
cious or benefic Lagnas or Ascendants (2/3/6/9/12) except the Lagnas
or Ascendants of the Chara Rashis (movable signs) and on auspicious
days.

Notes: According to Markandeya, the Shashthi (6th) Tithi should
be rejected for the beginning of learning the letters Samskara. In the
Lagna or Ascendant which prevails at the time of the beginning of
the learning of letters there should be no planet in the 8th House from
the Ascendant.

THE MUHURTA FOR THE BEGINNING OF GETTING ED-
UCATION: In the context of the beginning of getting education by
children the auspicious Nakshtras are Mrigashira, Ardra, Punarvasu,
Hasta, Chitra, Swati, Shravana, Dhanishtha, Shatabhisha, Ashwini,
Moola, Poorvaphalguni, Poorvashadha, Poorvabhadrapada, Pushya and
Ashlesha, the auspicious days are Sunday, Thursday, Wednesday aud
Friday and the auspicious Tithis are 6/5/3/11/12/10/2; and the Lord
of the Ascendant running al the time of the beginning of Getting Edu-
cation should be a benefic planet and there should be benefic ptan-
ets in the Angles and Trines from the Ascendant.

Notes: Some Acharyas have said that for beginning to learn ar-
chery the Nakshtras named as Dhruwa, those of Rewati and Anuradha
are the auspicious ones.

136 . Muhurta Chinta Mani

THE TIME OF UPANAYANA SAMSKARA (the ceremony of
investiture with the sacred thread or Janeu): The Upanayana Samskara
of the Brahmins has been decided to be done in the 8th or the 5th
year from the time of conception or of birth, that of the Kshtriyas
in the 6th or the Lith year and that of the Vaishyas in the 8th or
the 12th year; (This is an ideal time for the Samskara of Upanayana).
If this Samskara is done when twice of the prescribed time has
passed, then this Samskara has been regarded as of medium level.

Notes: The word Upanayana is a compound word made up of
‘upa’ which means near and ‘Nayana’ which means taking. Thus it
means taking near the Gum or the Acharya for getting education or
Studying the Vedas.

The idea of Upanayana is of great significance. It refers to the
fact that one birth of the child took place at the home of its father
and mother and another birth will take place at the home of the Gur.
After the ‘Upanayana the child will be referred to as ‘Dwija’ or the
twice-born, that is a person who is born twice. ‘Upanayana’ is called
Vrata-bandhana also.

The main rite in the Upanayana Samskara is the investiture with
the Janeu or the sacred thread. Therefore it is called Yagyopaveeta
or Vratabandha Samaskara also. The Samskaras of Upanayana and
of Vedarambha are very near to each other so near that these can be
hardly differentiated. The Vedarambha Samaskara (getting the edu-
cation of the Vedas) begins just with the Upanayana Samaskara even
today both these Samskaras are performed at the same time in the
Gurukulas (Gurukula means the home of the teacher. In the ancient
times, the Shishya or the pupil went to the home of the Guru or
teacher to get education. He resided there as long as his edcuation
was completed) The word Upanayana itself means that the child is
now worthy of and dedicated to get the knowledge of the Vedas.

137 The Samsakaras

THE MUHURTA FOR THE UPANAYANA SAMSKARA: It
is auspicious to perform the Samskara of Upanayana in the Nakshtras
named as Kshipra (Hasta, Ashwini and Pushya), those named as
Dhniwa (the 3 uttaras and Rohini), that of Ashlesha, those called
Chara or movable (Swati, Punarvasu, Shravana, Dhanishtha and
Shatabhisha), that of Moola, those named as Mridu or Delicate
(Mrigashira, Rewati, Chitra and Anuradha), those of Poorvaphalguni,
Poorvashadha, Poorvabhadrapada and Ardra, on the 5 days of Sun-
day, Wednesday, Thursday, Friday and Monday, on the Tithis of 2/
3/5/11/12/10 and in the Shukla Paksha or the bright half of the month.
The first 3rd part (that is upto the Sth Tithi of the Krishna Paksha)
of the Krishna Paksha is also acceptable. The Upanayana samskara
is not to be done in the 3rd part of the day or in the afternoon which
is called ‘Apranha’ in Hindi.

Notes: The first third part of the Dinamaana is called
‘Poorvanha; the Znd part, ‘Madhyanha’, and the 3rd part, ‘Apranha’.
It is the rites done to the gods which are properly done in the
‘Poorvanha’ of the day; those done to men in the ‘Madhyanha’, and
those done to the manes (Shraddha etc.) in the ‘Apranha’. The main
period of performing Upanayana is the ‘Poorvanha’ of the day. The
Madhyanha and the Apranha should be altogether rejected for this
purpose. If there is the lack of an auspicious Ascendant in the
Poorvanha, then Upanayana may be performed in the Madhyanha as
well. Upanayana is extremely auspicious and proper in the month of
Chaitra and at the time when the Sun is gone in the sign of Pisces.

According to Brahmarishi Vashishtha, Kashyapa and Narada the
16 Nakshtras of Hasta, Chitra, Swati, Shravana, Dhanishtha,
Shatabhisha, Rohini, Mrigashira, all the three Uttaras, Anuradha,
Rewati, Ashwini, Punarvasu and Pushya are auspicious for
Upanayana. These are stated to be auspicious by the Rishis (sages)
that is, are ‘Arsha’. The Upanayana has been said to be inauspicious
for the Brahmins if it is done in the Nakshtra of Punarvasu also. -

139 The Samsakaras

cendant of that planet who is the Lord of the Varna and the Veda
of the child, and the strength of the planet described in the chapter
of ‘Gochara Prakaranam’ is extremely auspicious in the Upanayana
samskara. The storng position of the planets who are the Lords of
the Vedas, of the Sun, the Moon and Jupiter is the best for the
Samskara of Upanayana or ‘Yagyopaveeta’. If at the time of the
Upanayana samaskara Jupiter and Venus (Lords of both the Varnas
and the Vedas) are in their enemy’s sign, in their deep debilitation
sign and are defeated in the planetary war, then the concerned child
is devoid of the Vedas and the shastras.

Notes: If the ‘upaneet’ child (the child whose Upanayana’
samaskara is to be done) is of the Rigaveda branch, the day of Thurs-
day, 9/12 Lagnas or Ascendants and the strength of Juptier, if he is
of the yayuraveda branch, then the day of Friday, 2/7 Lagnas or As-
cendants and the strength of Venus, if he is of the Saama Veda
branch, then the day of Tuesday, 1/8 Lagnas or Ascendants and if
he is of the Atharvaveda branch, then the day of Wednesday, 3/6
Lagnas or Ascendant and the strenght of Mercury -these are praise
worthy or auspicious days, Lagnas or Ascendants etc.

EXCEPION OF THE MONTH OF THE BIRTH ETC. IN THE
UPANAYANA SAMSKARA: If the ‘Upanayana Samskara’ of the
eldest child who is born in the Brahmin Varna is done in the
Nakshtra, the month, the Lagna or the Ascendant and on the Tithi
of his birth, then the child is endowed with much education and learn-
ing. If the child is of the Varna of Kshtriya or of Vaishya and he is
bom after the first conception, that is, he is born of the 2nd concep-
tion and if his “Upanayana Samskara’ is performed in the above-stated
conditions, then he also is endowed with much education and learmn-
ing.
THE AUSPICIOUSNESS OR THE INAUSPICIOUSNESS OF

140 Muhurta Chinta Mani

JUPITER: [f Jupiter is in the 5/9/11/2/7 places or Houses from the
Janama Rashi (sign of birth) of the boy or the girl, then he is excel-
lent; his (Jupiter's) being in the 10/6/3/1 places or Houses he (Jupi-
ter) should be pacified or worshipped; it is only then that he is ac-
ceptable or favourable and if he is in 4/8/12 places or Houses, then
he is not acceptable or favourable in the Samaskaras of marriage,
‘Upanayana’ etc.

THE EXCEPTION OF THE [INAUSPICIOUS JUPITER: If Ju-
piter is in his sign of exaltation, own sign or friend's sign, no mat-
ter he is in any sign, but he must be in his own Navamsha or in his
own Vargottama Navamsha or exalted Navamsha (thus the Jupiter in
the sign of Gemini should be of the sign of Gemini in the Navamsha
also), then he is excellent (or auspicious) in the 4/8/12 Places or
Houses from the sign of birth (the Janama Rashi) atso.

If Jupiter is in his debilitation sign or in his enemy’s sign (10/
3/6/2/7) then he is inauspicious even in an auspicious House.

THE TIME WHICH SHOULD BE REJECTED IN THE
UPANAYANA SAMSKARA: The Samskara of Upanayana is not to
be performed in the Krishna Paksha or the dark half of the month
on the Tithis from Shashthi (the 6th) to Amavasya, on the day of
Pradosha (described in the 55th Shloka of this very chapter, on the
days of Anadhyaya (on the Tithis which are rejected described in the
54th Shloka of this very chapter), on Saturday, in the ‘Apranha Kaala’
{in the afternoon) on the day on which there is thunder “and light-
ning in the clouds in the morning and on the Galagrati Tithis (the
13/14/15/30/1/4/7/8/9 Tithis).

143 The Samsakaras

THE CHARACTERISTICS OF ‘PRADOSHA’: If there occurs
the beginning of the 13th Tithi in the 12th Tithi before midnight, of
the 7th Tithi in the 6th Tithi befure 1% Prahara in the night, and of
the 4th Tithi in the 3rd Tithi before 1 Prahara in the night, then these
3 Tithis are referred to as the Pradosha Tithis. In this way the 12th,
the 6th and the 3rd Tithis are named as Pradosha Tithis.

Notes: The meaning of ‘the Pradosha Night’ is ‘the mouth of
the Night’. In this way Pradosha occurs on every day as after the
setting of the Sun the beginning of the Night, due to its being the
mouth of the night, will be named as Pradosha. This Pradosha is
‘Naityika’, that is, it occurs daily and is the daily occurring Pradosha.
the Prodosha which has been referred to in the present Shloka is
named as Naimittika Pradosha. The studying and teaching of the
Vedas and of the Vedangas (parts of the Vedas) have been prohibit-
ed in these Pradoshas.

After the ‘Upanayana Samskara’, another samskara which is
called ‘Brahmodana’, is performed on the same day in the evening.
In the Brahmodana Samskara a specia) ‘paaka’ or dish is prepared
with sacred food grains. this ‘Paaka’ or dish is named as Brahmodana
Paaka’. This Smaskara is performed among the Brahmins. The child
is caused to cat ‘the sacred Paaka’. If after the ‘Upanyana Samskara’
and before the ‘Brahmodana Paaka’ there occurs any kind of natural
disturbance (the thunder of the clouds, storm or tempest), then the
Brahmodana Samskara should be performed after doing the pacifica-
tion rite.

THE FIXED NAKSHTRA IN THE UPANAYANA

144 Muhurta Chinte Mani

SAMSKARA ACCORDING TO EACH VEDA: The Upanayana
Samskara of the Rigavedis is auspicious in the Nakshtras of
Mrigashira, Ardra, Ashlesha, Hasta, Chitra, Swati, Moola and all the
3 Poorvas; that of the Yajurvedis in those of Rewati, Hasta, Anuradha,
Mnrigashira, Punarvasu, Pushya, Rohini and all the 3 Uttaras; that of
the Saamavedis in those of Ashwini, Dhanishtha, Pushya, Hasta, all
the 3 Uttaras, Ardra, and Shravana; and that of the Atharvavedis in
those of Mrigashira, Rewati, those named as Laghu or small (Hasta,
Ashwini and Pushya), Anuradha, Dhanishtha and Punarvasu.

THE PACIFICATION RITE FOR THE AUSPICIOUS DEEDS
IF THE CHILD'S MOTHER IS HAVING MENSES: If after the
completion of the ‘Nandi Shraddha’ (At the beginning of every aus-
Picious deed there is done a principal part of that karma or deed,
Nandi, that is the Shraddha indicating well being), as ill luck would
have it, the child’s mother has menses, and there is no proper Lagna
or Ascendant after this present one, then the Samskaras of Mundana,
Upanayana and marriage etc should be performed on the same day
according to the customs after doing the pacification rite, otherwise
it is not auspicious (that is, the Samskaras of Mundana etc, are not
auspicious without doing the pacification rite.)

THE CHHURIKA BANDHANA MUHURTA FOR THE
KSHATRIYAS: After the Upanayana Samskara and before the mar-
riage Smaskara the ‘Chhurika Bandhana Samskara’ (the tying of a
dager on the back) is auspicious in the months which are auspicious
for ‘the Upanayana Samskara’ on the other days etc except in the
month of Chaitra, in the combust condition of Mars and on the day
of Tuesday.

145 The Samsakaras

THE MUHURTA FOR THE KESHANTA KARMA AND
SAMAVARTANA SAMSKARA: (Getting the hair cut and the com-
pletion of Brahmacharya): In the Muhurta which has been told as aus-
picious for Mundana (Stated in the 29th Shloka of this very chapter)
Samskara, the doing of ‘the Keshanta Karma’ is auspicious. This
Smaskara is performed in the 16th year. And the Muhurta which has
been told as auspicious for the Upanayana Samskara is also auspi-
cious for the ‘Samavartana Karma’ or the completion of
Brahmacharya.

Notes: The ‘Keshanta Samskara’ of a Brahmin should be done
in the 16th year, of a Kshtriya in the 22nd year and of a Vaishya in
the 24th year.

Afier completing the study of the Vedas and the Vedangas at
the house of the Guru or teacher there used to be for the
Brahamacharis ‘Samavartana Samskara’ before entering into or tak-
ing the Grihastha Ashrama. In the modern times the getting of de-
gree after graduation is also a kind of Samarvatana Samskara or con-
‘vocation.

Our Rishis divided the whole life of man, taking it as consis-
ting of 100 years, into 4 Ashramas-the Brahamacharyashrama, the
Grahasthashrama, the Vanaprasthashrama and the Sanyasashrama-each
of which spread over 25 years of man’s life. There are Samskaras at
the different stages of life in each of these Ashramas. The
Brahamacharyashrama is the first of these Ashramas. In this Ashrama
one completed his education. He resided in Gurukula or the Home
of the Guru (teacher).

—

CHAPTER 6
MARRIAGE SANSKARAS

THE IMPORTANCE OF ASCENDANT IN MARRIAGE: A
wife having good conduct and charming manners is the giver of
Dharma, Artha and Kama (religion, wealth and gratification of sens-
es). The nature, conduct and good qualities of a woman become ex-
cellent if her marriage is performed in an auspicious Ascendant. It
is because of this that a good Ascendant is considered (or chosen)
for marriage as son, good conduct and wealth are bestowed by a good
Ascendant of marriage.

THE RULE FOR ASKING AN ASTROLOGER ABOUT AN
ASCENDANT FOR MARRIAGE AND A COMBINATION OF

148 Marriage Sanskaras

THE YOGA OR COMBINATION OF BEING LICENTIOUS
AND MRITAVATSA (THE WOMAN WHO GIVES BIRTH TO
CHILDREN BUT THE CHILDREN DIE SOON AFTER BIRTH) If
there is some Malefic Planet in the 5th House in the Horary Chart
and if he being aspected by his enemy planet is situated in a Malefic
Sign, then there is the certainty of the girl’s becoming promiscous
or Mritavatsa (that is, either she will be a woman of loose character
or her children will not survive).

THE YOGA OR COMBINATION OF THE CANCELLATION
OF MARRIAGE AS PER THE HORARY CHART: If the question
about a girl’s marriage is asked in the Krishna Paksha or in the Dark

.. Half of the Month and if at that time the Moon being in an even

sign is situated in the 6th or the 8th House and is aspected by a
Malefic Planet, then, as per such Horary Chart, there is the Cancel-

- lation of marriage, that is, the marriage does not take place even

though all the conditions and the date of the marriage have been de-
cided.

THE PACIFICATION RITE FOR REMOVING THE DEFECT
OF THE YOGA OR COMBINATION OF WIDOWHOOD. Having
made it certain from the Natal Ascendant or the Horary Chart of the
girl that there is the presence of the ‘Baala Vidhwa Yoga’ (the girl's
becoming widow soon after her marriage), the girl should be asked

149 Mariage Sanskaras

to keep Pippala Vrata and Savitri Vrata {these fasts are explained in
the notes) and then she should be married to a match having a long
span of longevity in his Natal Ascendant: or, first she should be mar-
ried according to the religious rites of her religion to the image of
Lord Vishnu, to a tree of Pippala and to a Pitcher in an auspicious
Muhurta (LAGNA OR ASCENDANT) in a lonely place, and then
she is to be married to a match having the Yoga or combination of
long span of longevity in his Natal Ascendant. In such a condition
the girl does not have the defect of re-marriage and if the above stat-
ed rites are performed her Yoga or combination of widowhood is can-
celled.

Notes: The Savitri Vrata (The fast named as Savitri) is kept on
the Amavasya (the 30th Tithi of the Month) of the Month of Jyeshtha.
This fast, if it is fully observed, is kept on three days, on the 13th,
the 14th and the 30th Tithis of the Krishna Paksha of the Month of
Jyeshtha. If the girl is unable to keep fast on three days continuously,
then in such a condition fast may be kept only on the Amavasya or
the 30th Tithi of Jyeshtha. Wo keep this fast for removing the
defect of the Yoga or combination of Widowhood in their Natal As-
cendants.

The Pippala Vrata (The fast named as Pippata): This Vrata or
fast is kept for one month beginning on the 3rd Tithi of the Krishna
Paksha or the Dark Half of the Month of either of the Month of
Chaitra or of Ashwina. This fast is kept for the cancellation of the
Yoga or Combination of Widowhood in their Natal Ascendants.

an data |

THE CONSIDERATION OF THE ISSUES OF THE GIRL AS
PER THE HORARY CHART: The pandits or the astrologers should
predict that the girl (about whom thcy have been asked) will have
the kind of issue (girl or boy) with whom some other (married) wom-
an happens to come there of her own accord and without being in-
vited or asked on the occasion of the Harary Chart

150 Muhurta Chinta Mani

Notes: If at the time when the Horary Chart of a girl is being
prepared some married woman happens to come to the Astrologer
with her issue (son or daughter) of her own accord without being
asked to come there, then according to the Science of Prediction the
first issue of the concerned girl will be of the kind which is there in
the lap of the woman coming there, that is, the girl will have a
daughter if the woman has girl in her lap and she will have a son if
she has boy, this should be the prediction for her.

9. THE AUSPICIOUS AND INAUSPICIOUS OMENS AT
THE TIME WHEN HORARY CHART IS PREPARED: If at the time
when the Horary Chart is prepared there is heard the sound of Conch,
Mridanga, Veena etc then it is an auspicious omen and everything
will go well, but if there is heard the cry of a crow, an ass, a dog
or a jackal, then it is an inauspicious omen and some evil will hap-

pen.

THE MUHURTA FOR CHOOSING BRIDE: In the Nakshtras
of Uttarashadha, Swati, Shravana, Poorvaphalguni, Poorvashadha,
Poorvabhadrapada, Anuradha, Dhanishtha, Kritika or in the Nakshtras
of marriage (described in the 55th Shtoka of this very Chapter) first
the girl (who is to be chosen as bride) should be pleased with the
gifis of clothes, jewels, fruits and flowers and then having done this
she should be chosen as bride.

Notes: Having been satisfied with the other matters relating to
the marriage of the boy and the girl, the relatives of the boy give
word to the relatives of the girl that the girl is suitable to them and
she has all the desirable qualities in her and there is no need to search
other match for her now and with this the relatives of the boy choose
the girl as the bride after putting clothes, jewels, fruits and flowers
in her lap.

It is said at another place that the ] is to be chosen as bride

151 Marriage Sanskaras

with hands full of sugar cane, rice, flower, supari, betel and turmer-
ic and with untired peaceful heart. The bride is to be chosen on all
the rest auspicious days except Tuesday, on all the tithis except those
Tithis which are named as Rikta, in an auspicious Ascendant. At the
time of choosing all the five parts of the Panchanga should be aus-
picious and the Moon should also be strong and favourable.

THE MUHURTA FOR THE CHOOSING OF THE BRIDE-
GROOM OR FOR ENGAGEMENT: The Brahmin or the real broth-
er of the bride should choose the bridegroom or should do the en-
gagement on an auspicious day along with the sound of a song and
musical instrument and with clothes, Yagyopaveeta or Janeu (sacred
thread worm by the pious or religious Hindus) etc in the Nakshtras
named as Dhniwa (the 3 Uttaras and Rohini) in those of Kritika,
Poorvaphalguni, Poorva Shadha and Poorvabhadrapada.

THE TIME FOR MARRIAGE AND ‘GRAHA SHUDDHI’
(SUITABILITY OF THE PLANETS). The arranging of marriage is
auspicious at the time when Jupiter is ‘Shuddha’ or suitable six years
after the birth of the daughter in even years 8/10 and so on; when
the Sun is ‘Shuddha’ or suitable in the case of the boy in odd years,
9/11 and so on and when the Moon is ‘Shuddha’ or suitable in the
case of both the boy and the girl.

Notes: The position of Jupiter in a benefic House from the
‘Janama Rashi’ {the sign in which the Moon is there at the time of
birth) of the girl is referred to as ‘Guru Shuddhi’ (or the favourable
position of Jupiter); and the position of the Sun in a benefic house
from the ‘Janama Rashi’ of the boy is referred to as ‘Ravi Shuddhi’
(or the favourable position of the Sun) and the position of the Moon
in a benefic house from the ‘Janama Rashis’ of both the boy and
the girl is referred to as ‘Chandra Shuddhi’

152 Muhurta Chinta Mani

PROPER TIME FOR MARRIAGE: Celebrating marriage is aus-
picious when the Sun is in the signs of Gemini, Aquarius, Capricorn,
Scorpio, Taurus and Aries. If the Sun is in the sign of Gemini, then
in the month of Ashadha it is ouly from the Pratipada (the first Tithi
of the month) to the 10th Tithi in the Shukla Paksha or the Bright
Half of the month that the celebrating of marriage is auspicious. If
the Sun is in the sign of Scorpio marriage may be celebrated in
Kartika also, if he is in the sign of Capricom, in Pausha also and if
he is in the sign of Aries, in Chaitra also.

Notes: It is the “Saur Maasa’ or Solar month alone which 18
regarded as proper in the auspicious deeds like doing marriage etc.
Marriage is not to be celebrated in the months of ‘Kartika’, ‘Pausha’
and ‘Chaitra’. But if the Sun enters the sign of Scorpio in the
Kartika’ “Chandra Maasa’ (Lunar Month), then it (Kartika’), be-
comes *‘Margashirsha’ according to the ‘Saur Maasa’ or Solar Month.
Similarly, if the Sun enters the sign of Capricorn in the month of
“Pausa’, then it (‘Pausa’) becomes ‘Magha' and if he enters the sign
of Anies in the month of Chaitra, then it (Chaitra) becomes Baisakha.
Therefore, the celebration of marriage becomes auspicious in these
conditions even in the months of Kartika, Pausha and Chaitra.

153 Marriage Sanskaras

THE CONSIDERATION OF THE TIME OF MARRIAGE: If
the bridegroom and the bride are the first born of their respective
parents, then it is not proper to celebrate their marriage in their
Janama Maasa, Janama Nakshtra and Janama Tithi (Month, Nakshtra
and Tithi respectively of birth). If both are born of the second con-
ception then the celebration of their marriage in the ‘Janama Maasa’
etc becomes the giver of son (to the newly married couple), such is
the saying of the Pandits or the learned astrologers.

CELEBRATING AND NOT CELEBRATING MARRIAGE IN
THE MONTH OF ‘SYESTHA’: The marriage of the eldest daughter
and of the eldest son is not to be celebrated in the month of Jyeshtha.
If the daughter is the eldest one she may be married in Jyeshtha to
a bridegroom who is not the eldest son of his parents or the eldest
son may be married in Jyeshtha also to 3 bride who is not the el-
dest daughter of her parents.

It is the opinion of some Acharya (Bharadwaja) that the cele-
bration of marriage is auspicious in the month of Jyeshtha also leav-
ing that Jyeshtha month in which the Sun is in the Nakshtra of Kritika
and joining in wedlock (celebrating the marriage) of the bride and
the bridegroom. of the First Conception is also not auspicious.

Notes: Here it is the issue which is born of the first concep-
tion who is to be regarded as the eldest progeny. If the first concep-
tion is any how destroyed, then the issue born of the second con-
ception is not regarded as the eldest progeny according to the shastras,
although practically he/she is regarded as the eldest progeny.

154 Muhurts Chinta Mani

THE RULES REGARDING THE TIME OF MARRIAGE ETC.
OF THE PROGENY BORN IN THE SAME FAMILY AND OF THE
SON AND THE DAUGHTER BORN OF THE SAME PARENTS:
In one’s own family the marriage of a daughter should not be cel-
ebrated till the expiry of 6 months after the marriage of a son and
the Mundana (first hair cutting) of a child is not to be done till the
expiry of 6 months after the marriage of a boy or a girl. Two real
brothers should not be joined in wedlock with two real sisters. The
marriage of two real brothers or sisters should not be celebrated till
6 months and ‘Shraddha’ of some one in the family is not to be done
in an auspicious deed.

Notes; In the family of father, grandfather and great-grandfa-
ther, the celebration of the marriage of a daughter till 6 Saur Maasa
(Solar Months) after the marriage of a son, has not been regarded as
auspicious, Here marriage is only an example, other ‘Samskaras’ like
the ‘Upanayana Samskara’ should also not be done till 6 months (So-
lar Months) after the expiry of the doing of any other samskara of
the progeny of the same family

The Samskaras of the issues born of the same mother should
not be performed at the same time

The Samskaras like ‘Upanayana’ etc. of two children bom of
different mothers may, be done by doing them in different “Mandaps’
{places where the Samskaras are done. These places are variously
beautified) or getting them done by different Acharyas (Acharya here
means the Pandit who does the Samskara).

CONSIDERATION OF SOME ONE’S DEATH IN THE FAM-
ILY OF THE BRIDEGROOM OR THE BRIDE: If there occurs the
death of some one in the three generations of either of the bride-
groom or of the bride after the ‘Vaagadaana’ (choosing the bride-
groom by offering him money, sweets and fruits), then the marriage
should be celebrated after one month. And some Acharyas say that
marriage may be celebrated within a month also (after the death) af-
ter the expiry of the period of ‘Ashaucha’ (13 days after the death

of some one in the family have been regarded as inauspicious for
doing any auspicious deed. These are called the days of Ashaucha)
after doing pacification rite.

THE PROBHIBITION REGARDING THE DOING OF
MUNDANA ETC AFTER MARRIAGE: The Samskaras of
“Upanayana’ and ‘Mundana’ are not auspicious til 6 months after the
marriage of some   the family of 3 generations. And Mundana
(in the family) is not auspicious till 6 months after ‘Upanayana’.

A daughter is not to be sent to her husband's house till 6
months after the coming-in of a son's wife. But if the year (Samvata)
is changed then auspicious deeds may be done even within 6 months.
Thus if the marriage of a son takes place in the month of Magha,
then in the month of Vaisakha (within 6 months) the Samskaras like
“Mundana’, ‘Upanayana’ etc may be done, as the Samvata (year) is
changed (According to the Hindu System Samvata or year is changed
in the Month of Chaitra which falls roughly in March or April).

THE EFFECTS ARISING BECAUSE OF THE BIRTH OF
THE BOY OR THE GIRL IN THE NAKSHTRAS OF ASHLESHA,
MOOLA, JYESHTHA AND VISHAKHA: The daughter and the son
born in the Nakshtra of Ashlesha destroy (cause the death of) their
mother-in-law; those born in that of Moola, their father-in-law, and
the daughter born in the Nakshtra of Jyestha destroys the elder brother
of her husband; and that born in the Nakshtra of Vishakha destroys
the younger brother of her husband. The meaning is that they are
inauspicious to the relatives stated above.

156 Muhurta Chinta Mani

THE REMOVAL GF THE DEFECT ARISING DUE TO THE
BIRTH IN THE NAKSHTRA OF ASHLESHA ETC.: The daughter
born in the first three Charanas or quarters of Vishakha is the giver
of happiness to the younger brother of her husband. The daughter
(or the son also) born in the 4th Charana or quarter of Moola
Nakshtra is auspicious for her father-in-law and that born in the first
quarter of the Ashlesha Nakshtra is so for her mother-in-law.

Notes: Here there is no removal of the Nakshtra of Jyeshtha.
It is stated in the 19th Shioka that a daughter born in the Nakshtra
of Vishakha brings destruction or death to the younger brother of her
husband. But in the present Shloka the writer has spoken of the re-
moval of this defect that if the daughter is born in the first three
Charanas or quarters (3/4 part) of the Nakshtra of Vishakha, she is
the giver of happiness to the younger brother of her husband.

In the same way the boy or girl born in the 4th quarter of the
Nakshtra of Moola are auspicious for their father-in-law. It is only
the first 3 quarters of this Nakshtra that have been regarded as the
destroyer of the father-in-law. Again, a daughter and a son born in
the Nakshtra of Ashlesha have been spoken of.as the destroyers of
their mother-in-law, but the first Charana or quarter is auspicious.

\

THE EIGHT KOOTAS IN THE MELAPAKA OF MAR-
RIAGE: 1. Vama, 2. Vashya, 3. Tara, 4. Yoni 5. Grahamaitri, 6.
Ganamaitri, 7. Bhakoota, and 8. Nadi, these are the eight kinds of
Kootas each of which has one more guna or point than the former
one; that is, the guna or point of Varma is 1; that of Vashya, 2; that
of Tara, 3; that of Yoni, 4; that of Grahamaitri, 5; that of Ganamaitri,
6; that of Bhakoota, 7 and that of Nadi, 8

Notes: According to the rule of the Arithmetic of ‘Saika
Padaghana Padardhama’, the number of all the gunas or points is
(8+ 18/2 = 36

Guna or Point here refers to the measure which may be ex-

pressed in points and which covers all the aspects of the married life
of the bridegroom and the bride. The points are called Gunas. If the

157 Marriage Sanskaras

number of the gunas or points of ‘Melapaka’, (Melapaka is a techni-
cal term. It means the total Gunas or points obtained by both the
would be bridegroom and bride on the basis of 8 Kootas) of the
bridegroom and the bride is 18 or more than 18, then the Nakshtra
Melapaka of both is suitable for marriage. The greater the number
of gunas than 18, the better is the Melapaka of the marriage of both.
It is said in ‘}yotimibandha’ that if the number of gunas of Kootas
is 16, it is not suitable; if it is from 17 to 20, it is medium, if it is
from 21 to 31, it is excellent, and if it is from 31 to 36, it is most
excellent. But if there is the ‘Dusta Bhakoota’ or the Bhakoota is evil,
then it is inauspicious upto 20; it is medium from 21 to 25; it is ex-
cellent from 26 to 30, and it is most excellent from 30 to 36.

The Astrologers do the comparison of the Janamangas (horo-
scopes) of the bride and the bridegroom before their marriage in many
different ways. The system stated above is referred to as Nakshtra
Melapaka System. In this system there are the above stated 8 parts.
These parts are referred to as Kootas. The ‘Janama Nakshtra’ or the
Nakshtra at the time of birth alone is the main basis of this system.

THE CONSIDERATION REGARDING THE KOOTA NAMED
“VARNA”: The signs of Pisces, Scorpio and Cancer are Brahmins:
Aries, Leo and Sagittarius are Kshtriyas, Taurus, Virgo and Capri:
corm are Vaishyas; and Gemini, Aquarius and Libra are Shudras
{These-Brahmin, Kshtriya, Vaishya and Shudra-are the 4 Vamas or
Castes), The girl belonging to higher Varna than the Vama of the
boy is not auspicious, such has been said by the Pandits or the
learned astrologers.

Notes: If the Varna of the bridegroom is higher than that of
the bride or if it is different, then there is the obtainment of onc
‘guna’, otherwise there is zero obtainment of guna; that is the Koota
.of Varna has been given 1 (one) Guna.

THE CONSIDERATION OF TARA KOOTA: Counting is to
be done from the Nakshtra of the girl to that of the boy, and then it
(Counting) is to be done from the Nakshtra of the boy to that of the
girl. If by dividing each of them by 9, the remainder comes to be 3/
5/7, then the Tara is inauspicious. And if the remainder is other than
these (that is, if it is 1/2/4/6/8/0) then the Tara is auspicious.

Notes: 3 Maximum points have been allotted to Tara Koota. If
the Tara Koota of both the bride and the bridegroom is excellent,
there will be 3 points. If it is auspicious of one and inauspicious of
the other, there will be 14 marks; and if it is inauspicious of both
and the Tara is also inauspicious (it is of 3/5/7), then the Tara Guna
(The Tara point) will be zero. Therefore it is quite clear that the gunas
are determined according to the auspicious and inauspicious Taras of
both the bride and the bridegroom. The names of the Taras have been
given in the 12th Shioka of chapter 4.

163 Marriage Sanskaras

YONI KOOTA: The yoni of the Nakshtras of Ashwini and
Shatabhisha is ‘Ashwa’ (Horse), that of Swati and Hasta, Mahisha
(She buffalo}; that of Dhanishtha and Poorvabhadrapada, Singha
(Lion); that of Bharani and Rewatt, Haathi (Elephant); that of Pushya
and Kritika, Medha (Mesha-Aja-Bakra or Ram in English); that of
Shravana and Poorvashadha, Vanara (Monkey), that of Uttarashadha
and Abhijita, Nakula (Mangoose). that of Mrigashira and Rohini,
Sarpa (Serpent); that of Jyeshtha and Anuradha, Harina (deer), that
of Moola and Ardra, Swana (dog), that of Punarvasu and Ashlesha,
Marjara (Bidala or Tom cat in English). that of Magha and
Poorvaphalguni, Mushaka (Rat), thai of Vishakha and Chitra, Vyaghra
(Leopard); and that of Uttaraphalguni and Uttarabhadrapada, Gau
(Cow).

There happens to be mutual Mahabair (Extreme enmity)
between-two yonis of the Nakshtras stated in one quarter of the
shloka, therefore these should be regarded as worthy of being left out
while equating the gunas or marks.

Notes: One yoni has been described in one quarter of the
shloka. In this way there are 7 x 2= 14 yonis in the shloka, Here it
has been said that there is the condition of Mahabair (or extreme en-
mity) in the 2 yonis of each quarter which is not acceptable. Thus
there is the Mahabair between the Ashwa (Horse) yoni of the

164 Muhurta Chinta Mani

Ashwini-Shatabhisha Nakshtra in the first half of the first quarter and
the Mahisha (she buffalo} yoni of the Swati-Hasta Nakshtra in the
2nd Half of the first quarter. This Mahabair (extreme enmity) is quite
natural and it should be so. In the same way there is Mahabair (ex-
ireme enmity) between Singh-Haathi (Lion and elephant), Medha-
Vanara (Ram and Monkey), Neola-Sarpa (Mangoose and serpent),
Harina-Swana (deer and dog), Marjara-Mushaka (Cat and Mouse) and
Vyaghra-Gau (Leopard and cow). Therefore the marriage of the bride
and the bridegroom having the above stated opposite yonis should
not take place. 4 points have been allotted to the ‘Koota’ of ‘Yoni’.
The Similanty and adjustibility of mental habits and attitudes is
known on the basis of the yonis. There is great importance of the
yoni koota in reference to sexual joy, mutual attraction and love. Here
the number of Nakshtras has been taken to be 28, that is, the Abhijita
Nakshira has also been given a place in the yoni koota. The number
of gunas in the Mahabair (extreme enmity) is zero; in the Bair (en-
mity), 1 (one); in the udaseenta or neutrality, 2; in the Mitrata (friend-
ship), 3; and in the Ati-mitrata (extreme friendship), 4.

Marriage may take place if the yoni kootas of the boy and the
girl are mutual enemy and the vashya kootas are suited or are friend-
ly.

If the Lord of the Rashi or sign of the bride and the bnde-
groom is the same or if there is friendship between them then there
will be 5 gunas if one is friend and the other is neutral, then 4 Gunas,
if both are neutral, 3 Gunas; if one is friend and the other is enemy,
2 Gunas, if one is neutral and the other is enemy, zero Guna.

GANA-KOOTA (The Koota of Ganas): The 9 (nine) Nakshtras
namely Magha, Ashlesha, Dhanishtha, Jyeshtha, Moola
Shatabhisha, Kritika, Chitra and Vishakha are Rakshasa (Demons)
Ganas; the 9 other Nakshtras namely Poorvaphaiguni, Poorvashadha,
Poorvabhadrapada, Uttaraphalguni, Uttarashadha, Uttarabhadrapada,
Rohini, Bharani and Ardra are Manushya (Man) Ganas, and the 9
still other Nakshtras namely Anuradha, Punarvasu, Mrigashira,
Shravana, Rewati, Swati, Hasta, Ashwini and Pushya are Deva (gods)

Ganas.

THE EFFECTS OF THE KOOTA OF GANAS: If the Gana of

the bride and the bridegroom is the same, then there will be exces-
sive love between wife and husband; love will be of average inten-
sity uf the gana of one is Devata (god) and of the other is Manushya
(Man), there will be death if the Gana of one is Rakshasa (demon)
and of the other is Manushya (Man), and there will be quarrel be-
tween them if the Gana of one is Devata (god) and of the other is
Rakshasa (demon).

Notes: The nature of the native is shown on the basis of Gana
Koota. The Ganas of Devata (gods) Manushya (men) and Rakshasa
(demons) represent respectively the qualities which may be spoken
of as divine human and demonic. If a native has Rakshas as his gana

169 Marriage Sanskaras ;

he/she should be wedded to the native having the same (that is,
Rakshasa) gana. This koota has been allotted 6 Points.

THE CONSIDERATION OF BHAKOOTA: If the Janama Rashi
{the sign in which the Moon is situated at the time of birth) of the
Bridegroom and of the bride are mutually in the 6th and the 8th
House, it (this yoga or combination) is the giver of death; if it is in
the 9th and in the 5th House, it will cause the loss of progeny, if it
is in the 2nd and in the 12th House, it will cause poverty, and there
will be happiness if they are in other places.

Notes: According to ‘Jyoti Prakasha’ if the Janama Rashi of the
bride is in the Sth House from that of the Bridegroom, it will be in-
auspicious, but it will be auspicious if it is in the 9th House. And if
the Janama Rashi of the bride is in the 2nd House from the Rashi
of the Bridegroom it will be inauspicious, but it will be auspicious
if it is in the 12th House.

If the Janama Rashis of the bridegroom and the bride are in
the 7th House and in the 4th and the 10th House, from each other,
it has been regarded as auspicious, but the two pairs of Rashis or
signs-Capricorn and Cancer, Leo and Aquarius-are the 7th from each
other but they are enemy to each other. And the Janama Rashis of
the bridegroom and of the bride will be in the 4th and the 10th House

170 Muhurts Chinta Mani

in this way-Libra from Capricorn, Leo from Taurus, Cancer from Ar-
ies, Pisces from Gemini, Virgo from Sagittarius, Scorpio from Aquar-
ius. But there is enmity between the Lords of these pairs of Rashis
or signs. Therefore, they are also regarded as inauspicious.

Therefore it is the 3-6, 4-7, 5-8, 9-12, 10-1 and 11-2, 4th and
10th situations which are auspicious and it is the Rashis or signs
which are in the 7th House from each other (4-10 and 5-11) that are
inauspicious.

If the Rashis of the Bridegroom and of the bride are in the 6/
8 House from cach other and if the Lords of the Rashis are friends
to each other, OR if the Lord of both the Rashis is only one planet,
then this situation comes in the limit of friendship. These situations
are formed in the following Rashis or signs: 1-8, 2-7, 4-9, 5-12, 6-
11 and 10-3, Doing of marriage is auspicious in these situations. The
relation of the concept of ‘Bhakoota’ is there with regard to economic
condition and growth of the family. 7 Points have been allotted to
Bhakoota.

172 Muhurta Chinta Mani

relating to Nadis is absent, then marriage is auspicious or if the Lords
of the Janama Rashis have friendship with each other and the Nadi
is also favourable, then also marriage is auspicious. If the Janama
Rashis of the bridegroom and the bride are in the 6th and the 8th
House from each other (if the Rashis of the bndegroom and the bride
are in the 6th and in the 8th House from each other, it is called the
‘Shadashtaka Dosha’ or the defect of being the 6th and the &th) and
if there is the enmity of the Lords of the Rashis of both with each
other but if there is the friendship of the Navamsha Lords of the
Janama Rashis of both, and if both are endowed with strength also
and are without Nadi Dosha (defect of the Nadis), the marriage is
auspicious.

If the Tara and the Nadi of the bridegroom and the bride are
favourable, even then marriage is auspicious.

If in the ‘Dusta’ or evi] Bhakoota there is no friendship between
the Lords of the Janama Rashis of the bridegroom and the bride, but

if the Rashi of the Bride is the ‘Vashya’ of the Rashi of the Bride-
groom and the Nadi is favourable, even then marriage is auspicious.

Notes: If there is the ‘Dusta’ or evil ‘Bhakoota’, 5 kinds of re-
moval have been stated here in this shloka.

I. The Lord of the Janama Rashis of the bridegroom and the
bride is the same planet.

2. There is friendship between the Lords of the Rashis of the
bridegroom and the bride.

3. If both the above stated conditions are not there, then the
Navamsha Lords of the Rashis of both the bridegroom and the bride
are endowed with strength and are friends to each other.

4. The Tara of the bridegroom and the bride is excellent.

5. The Vashya Koota is favourable, that is the Rashi of the
bride is the Vashya of the Rashi of the bridegroom.

In ail the above stated 5 kinds of removal the favourableness
of the Nadi has been said to be necessary.

173 Marriage Sanskaras

DUSTA BHAKOOTA NASHAKA YOGA (The combination
which destroys the evil Bhakoota): If there is the fnendship of the
Lords of the Janama Rashis of the bridegroom and the bride or if
there is friendship between the Lords of the Navamshas of the Janama
Rashis, then there does not occur the Dosha or defect of Dusta Gana
(Manushya, Rakshasa and Devata).

‘Shubha’ or auspicious ‘Bhakoota’ (the 3rd, the 110, the 4th,
the 10th the Bhakoota etc.) is the destroyer of the Doshas (defects)
relating to the enmity of the Planets (the enmity of the Lords of the
Rashis of the bridegroom and the bride).

Graha Maitri (the friendship of the Planetary Lords of the
Janama Rashis of the bridegroom and the bride) destroys the Dosha
(defect) of Dusta Bhakoota (evil Bhakoota).

NADI KOOTA (The Koota of Nadi): The Nakshtras of
Jyeshtha, Moola, Ardra, Punarvasu, Uttaraphalguni, Hasta,
Shatabhisha, Poorvabhadrapada and Ashwini are counted among the
Nadi named as ‘Adi’; those of Pushya, Mrigashira, Chitra, Anuradha,
Bharani, Dhanishtha, Poorvashadha, Poorvaphalguni and
Uttarabhadrapada among the Nadi named as ‘Madhya’; and those of
Swati, Vishakha, Kritika, Rohini, Ashlesha, Magha, Uttarashadha,
Shravana and Rewati among the Nadi named as ‘Antya’. If the
Nakshtras of the bridegroom and the bride are in the same Nadi, the
marniage of such persons is inauspicious; if the Nakshtras of the bride-
groom and the bride are in the ‘Madhya’ Nadi they are certainly giv-
ers of death.

174 Muhurta Chinta Mani

Notes: Nadi Koota is of the utmost importance. These Nadis
are related to the health, longevity and the happiness of issues (sons
and daughters) of the couple who are joined in wedlock. It is thought
that if the Nadi of the bridegroom and of the bride is the same, the
married life of such couple does not pass happily. The issues of such
couple are unhealthy and somewhat mentally undeveloped. There is
also the possibility that some mental defect will be developed.

If the Nadi of the bridegroom and the bride is ‘Adi’, then mon-
ey crisis come in the family life of the couple and there is adverse
effect on their health. If the Nadi of both is ‘Madhya’ then there will
certainly be the death of one of them.

8 Points have been allotted to the ‘Koota’ of ‘Nadi’. If there
is the ‘Nadi Dosha’, then marriage should not take place. It should
be altogether rejected.

THE FIRST MIDDLE (SECOND) AND LAST PARTS OF
THE NAKSHTRAS: 6 Nakshtras beginning with Rewati (Rewati,
Ashwini, Bharani, Kritika, Rohini and Mrigashira) are the Nakshtras
of the First Part; 12 Nakshtras beginning with Ardra (Ardra,
Punarvasu, Pushya, Ashlesha, Magha, Poorvaphalguni. Uttaraphalguni,
Hasta, Chitra, Swati, Vishakha and Anuradha) are of the Middle (sec-
