ond) Part, and 9 Nakshtras beginning with Jyeshtha (Jyeshtha, Moola,
Poorvashadha, Uttarashadha, Shravana, Dhanishtha, Shatabhisha,
Poorvabhadrapada and Uttarabhadrapada) are of the Last part.

If the Nakshtras of both the bridegroom and the bride happens
to be in the First Part, then the husband is dear to the wife; if in the
Middle Part then both have great love for each other, and if in the
Last Part then the husband has great love for the wife.

VARGA KOOTA (THE KOOTA OF VARGAS): The Lord of
the  (Aa) Varga (31        at ait- these are
vowels in the Hindi Language) is Garuda (a bird, the conveyance of
Lord Vishnu) that of  (Ka) Varga ( @ 7 W &) is Manjara or
Tom Cat;; that of @ (Cha) Varga (3  W  3) is Singha or
Lion; that of < (Ta) Varga (<@  S @ ) is Swana or Dog; that
of i (ta) Varga (    7) is Sarpa or Snake, that of 4 (Pa)
Varga (9 % 4 A A) is Mushaka or Mouse; that of 4 (ya) varga
(4   ) is Mriga or deer; that of  (sha) Varga (  )
is Medha or Ram; and the Varga which is the 5th from a Varga is
the enemy to the former and vice versa.

Notes: The Lord of the ‘Varga’ of that person the first letter
of whose name is in the  (Aa) Varga is ‘Garuda’. The same should
be understood in reference to all the other Vargas. The Fifth Varga
from each Varga is the Varga of its enemy. thus the  (Ta) Varga
from the 3 (Aa) Varga is the Sth Varga. The Lords of them are re-
spectively Garuda and Sarpa who are enemies to each other. There
is enmity also in the same way between Marjara and Mushaka, Singha
and Mriga, and Swana and Mcdha.

Martiage should not be arranged of the persons having Vargas
whose Lords are enemies to each other. If the bridegroom and the
bride are of the same Varga or if there is friendship between the
Lords of the Vargas of the bridegroom and the bride, then marriage
is auspicious. This consideration should be there also when some
friendship is established. The employee and the employer and the two

176 Muhurta Chinta Mani
partners in a trade should also have the same consideration of Vargas.

If the ‘Janama Kundali’ (ihe Horoscope or the birth chart) is
not available, then Melapaka or equating of Gunas or Points is made
on the basis of the first letter of the popular names of the bridegroom
and the bride. This ‘Melapaka‘ is based on the letters.

THE REMOVAL OF NADI DOSHA OR THE DEFECT OF
NADL: If the Janama Rashis of the bridegroom and the bride are the
game and the Nakshtras of them are different or if the Nakshtras are
the same and the Rashis are different, then there is no defect of the
‘Nadi’ and of the ‘Gana’. And if the Nakshtras are the same but the
quarters of the Nakshtras are different, it is also regarded auspicious.
that is, it does not have the ‘Nadi Dosha’ or the Defect of Nadi.

Notes: The writer means to say that the Rashis and the
Nakshtras of the bridegroom and of the bride should not be the same.
if their Nakshtras are the same then it is necessary that their Rashis
should be different; and if their Rashis are the same then their
Nakshtras should be different. If the Nakshtras are the same then the
quarters cf the Bridegroom and of the bride should be different. Thus
if there is the 3rd quarter of the Nakshtras of Shatabhisha and
Poorvabhadrapada, then the Rashi in both the cases is the same but
the Nakshtras are different. Therefore here the Dosha or Defect of
the Rakshasa-Manushya Gana, and the Dosha of the same Nadi will
not be present.

In the 3rd and the 4th quarter of the Poorvabhadrapada, the
Nakshtra is the same but the Rashis are different. Therefore here also
there will be no NADI DOSHA. If there is the same Rashi and dif-
ferent Nakshtras of the bridegroom and of the bride, it is the Nakshtra
of the bridegroom which should be the first (in the serial order). If
the Nakshtras of the male partner is the second to that of the female
it iS not auspicious. This has been said by shri Gargacharya in the
following shloka

178 Muhurta Chinta Mani

Notes: There are 30° (thirty degrees) in each Rashi or sign. The
9th part of a sign is called Navamsha. Therefore the 9th part of 30°
is 30 + 9 = 3°-20'. There are 9 Navamshas in the signs of Aries,
Leo and Sagittarius beginning from Aries and going down to Sagit-
tarius; 9 Navamshas in the signs of Taurus, Virgo and Capricorn be-
ginning from Capricorn and going down to Virgo; 9 Navamshas in
the signs of Gemini, Libra and Aquarius beginning from Libra and
going down to Gemini; and 9 Navamshas in the signs of Cancer,
Scorpio and Pisces beginning from Cancer and going down 10 Pi-
sces.

The Navamsha begins with a movable sign in the Movable
Signs, in the fixed signs from the 9th sign from it and in the Dual
signs from the 5th sign from it.

Like the Planetary Lords of the signs there are the planetary
Lords of the Navamshas. The Lord of the sign of Aries is Mars and
the Lord of the first Navamsha of Aries is also Mars. Where the Lord
of the Rashi or sign and the Lord of the Navamsha is the same
Planet, that Rashi or sign is called Vargotiama sign.

THE SPECULUM OF NAVAMSHA

THE RULE REGARDING HORA: There is the Hora of the
Moon te the first 15° in the even signs and that of the Sun to the
30° after that. And contrary to it in the odd signs there is the Hora
of the Sun to the first 15° and that of Moon to the 30° after that.

Notes: In the above Shloka it has been said that the half part
of a sign is called Hora and therefore there are two Horas in each
sign of 15° each.

TRIMSHAMSHA AND DRESHKANA OR DECANATE: In
the even signs the first 5° are the Trimshamshas of Venus, after 7°
of Mercury, 8° of Jupiter, 5° of Mars. In the odd signs there is the
reverse of it, that is, the first 5° are the Trimshamshas of Mars, after
that 5° of Saturn, 5° of Jupiter, 7° of Mercury and 5° of Venus.

The Dreshkana or Decanate is of the same Rashi or sign upto
10°, of the Sth Rashi or sign from that upto 20°, and of the 9th Rashi
or sign from that from 20° to 30°.

18) Marriage Sanskaras

DWADSHAMSHAS AND THE EFFECTS OF
SHADAVARGAS: The Dwadshamshas are in the order of the same
Rashi or sign in which the Dwadshamsha is to be known, that is,
there are 12 Dwadshamshas in any Rashi or sign beginning from that
very Rashi or sign.

in this way 6 Vargas (Divisions) - Lagna or Ascendant, Hora,
Dreshkana or Decanate, Navamsha, Dwadshamsha and Trimshamsha
- have been spoken of. The Vargas (Divisions) of the Benefic Plan-
ets give auspicious effects and those of the Malefic Planets give in-
auspicious effects.

Notes: The 12th part of a Rashi or sign is called Dwadshamsha.
Therefore 30 + 12 = 2°-30° is the Dwadshamsha of a Rashi or sign.
In the sign of Aries the first Dwadshamsha is of Aries, the 2nd of
Taurus, and so on. The counting of Dwadshamshas is done in the
other Rashis or signs in the same way beginning from that very sign.

186 Muhurta Chinta Man:

HOUSE FROM THE JANAMA LAGNA AND JANAMA RASHI IN
THE LAGNA OR ASCENDANT AT MARRIAGE: If the 8th Rashi
or sign from the Natal Ascendant and Janama Rashi is there in the
Ascendant at Marriage, then such mamiage is not auspicious. But
when the Pianetary lord of the Natal Ascendant or of the Janama
Rashi is the Lord of the Ascendant at marriage also or if there is
friendship between the two, then this Dosha or Defect does not ap-
ply.

Notes: Suppose the Janama Lagna (the Natal Ascendant) or the
Janama Rashi is Aries and the Ascendant at marriage is Scorpio. Here
the Lord of the Lagna (Ascendant) and of the 8th House is the same
(This is called ‘Ekadhipatya’ or the Lordship of one and the same).
Now this Dosha or Defect will not apply here Again, suppose the
Janama Lagna or Janama Rashi is Leo and the Ascendant at mar-
riage is Pisces. Here also this Dosha or Defect will not apply due to
there being friendship between the Sun (the Lord of the Leo sign)
and Jupiter (the Lord of the Ascendant at Mariage).

THE REMOVAL OF THE DOSHA OR DEFECT OF THE
RASHI OR SIGN OF THE 8TH HOUSE FROM THE JANAMA
LAGNA OR THE JANAMA RASHI BEING THE ASCENDANT
AT MARRIAGE: If the 8th sign from the Janama Lagna (Natal As-
cendant) or the Janama Rashi is Pisces, Taurus, Cancer, Scorpio, Cap-
ricorn, or Virgo and the Ascendant at marriage is one of these signs.
then this Dosha or Defect does not apply, because there being the
friendship of the Lord of the Janama Lagna or of the Janama Rashi
with the Lords of these Ascendants, such Ascendant is the giver of
son, longevity, house and happiness.

Notes: Another Acharya has said that if the 8th Lord is in an
Angle and he is aspected by some Benefic Planet or the 8th Lord is
in his own Navamsha or in the Navamsha of some Benefic Planet,
in his highest exaltation, in his own sign or in a friend's sign then
the Dosha or the Defect of the 8th sign as Lagna docs not apply.

187 Marriage Sanskaras

THE CONSIDERATION WITH REGARD TO THE 8TH AND
THE 12TH HOUSE AT THE TIME OF BIRTH IN THE ASCEN-
DANT AT MARRIAGE: If the Navamsha Rashi or sign of the 8th
Howse or the Lord of the sign of the 8th House from the Janama
Lagna (Natal Ascendant) happens to be there in the Ascendant at
marriage, then the celebration of marriage in such Ascendant is not
auspicious or if the Navamsha of the 12th sign or the sign of the
12th House or the Lord of the 12th House happens to be there in
the Lagna or Ascendant at marnage, then this yoga or combination
proves to be a quarre!-causing one for the bridegroom and the bride.

THE VISHA GHATIS OF THE NAKSHTRAS, THE GHATI
WHICH SHOULD BE REJECTED WHILE DOING SOME AUSPI-
CIOUS DEEDS: The 4 *Ghatis’ after the 30 Ghatis of the Nakshtras

188 Muhurta Chinta Mani

of Rewati, Punarvasu, Kritika and Magha: after the 40 Ghatis of
Rohini, after the 32 Ghatis of Ashlesha, after the 50 Ghatis of
Ashwini, after the 18 Ghatis of Uttaraphalguni and Shatabhisha, af-
ter the 20 Ghatis of the Nakshtras of Poorvaphalguni, Chitra,
Uttarashadha and Pushya; after the 14 Ghatis of those of Vishakha,
Swati, Mrigashira and Jyeshtha, after the 21 Ghatis of those of Ardra,
and Hasta, after the 16 Ghatis of Poorvashadha, after the 24 Ghatis
of the Nakshtras of Uttarabhadrapada, Poorvashadha and Bharani, after
the 10 Ghatis of those of Anuradha, Dhanishtha and Shravana, after
the 56 Ghatis of the Moola Nakshira, the 4 Ghatis in each case as
per the ‘Madhyama Maana’ {Average value which is 60 ghatis) are
Visha Ghatis. These are to be rejected (as inauspicious) while doing
some auspicious deed. And the Dhruwa or the constant of the
Nakshtras should be multiplied by the Bhabhoga (the full Maana or
value of the Nakshtra) and the resultant is to be divided by 60. the
quotient thus arrived at is the spashta (Real) Dhruwa or constant. And
the 4 visha Ghatis is also multiplied by the Bhabhoga and the re-
sultant is to be divided by 60. The Quotient thus arrived at is the
spashta (Peal) Visha Ghati.

Notes: The Maana or value of the Nakshtras is roughly sup-
posed to be 60 ghatis and according to that Madhyama Maana or
Average value the Dhruwa or constant of Visha ghati has been sup-
posed to be 4 ghatis. But the Maana or Value (period) or Bhabhoga
is either less or more than 60 ghatis. In such condition the real Maana
or value of the Dhruwa or constant of the Nakshtras and of the Visha
ghatis should be found out. For example, suppose the Bhabhoga of
the Kritika Nakshtra is 64 Ghatis. Then, here the Dhniwa of the
Kritika Nakshtra as given in the Shloka (30) is to be multiplied by
64 and the product is to be divided by 60.

30 * 64
= 32 Ghatis

The Quotient 32 Ghatis is the real Dhruwa or constant of the
Kritika Nakshtra. In the same way the Visha Ghati of the Kritika
Nakshtra in the shloka which is 4 is to be multiplied by 64 and the
product is to be divided by 60 to arrive al the real Visha Ghatis:

4x 64
= 4 Ghatis 16 Palas

190 Muhurta Chinta Mani

The Ghatis which are written against the Tithis and Days are
those after which the 4 Ghatis are Visha Ghatis.

The Removal of the Dosha or Defect of the Visha Ghatis has
also been pointed out by some Acharyas. If the Moon is in the 4/7/
5/9/10 Houses or if the Ascendant Lord is in the 1/4/7/10 Houses
and is aspected by Benefic Planets, the Dosha or Defect of Visha
Ghatis does not apply or if the Moon or the Ascendant Lord being
in a Benefic Sign or in his own shadavarga is aspected by his friendly
planet, then also the Dosha or defect of Visha Ghatis does not ap-
ply.

THE NAMES OF THE 15 MUHURTAS OF DAY: Girisha (1),
Bhujaga (2), Surya (3), Pitri (4), Vasu (5), Jala (6), Vishwa Deva(7),
Abhijit (8), Vidhata (9), Indra (10), Indragni (11), Niririti (12), Udaka
Nath (13), Aryama (14) and Bhaga (15)—these 15 are the Muhurtas
in a day.

Notes: The names of the Muhurtas are after the Lords of the
Nakshtras. The measure of every Muhurta in a day is equal to the ;
15th part of the Dinamaana.

THE 15 MUHURTAS OF NIGHT: Shiva (1), Ajapada (2),
Ahirbudhanya (3), Poosha (4), Ashwini Kumar (5), Yama (6), Agni
(7), Brahma (8), Chandra (9), Aditi (10), Brahaspati (11), Vishnu (12),
Surya (13), Twashtra (14) and Maruta (15}—these 15 are the
Muhurtas of Night.

Notes: A Muhurta is equal to the 15th part of the Ratrimaana.
If the Nakshtra in which some auspicious deed is advised to be done

191 Marriage Sanskaras

is not there, then the deed may be done in the Muhurta of the Lord
of that Nakshtra.

THE MUHURTAS IN WHICH AN AUSPICIOUS DEED
SHOULD NOT BE DONE: Aryama (Uttaraphalguni) on Sunday,
Brahma and Raksha (Rohini-Moola) on Monday Vahni and Pitni
(Kritika and Magha) on Tuesday, Abhijita on Wednesday, Toya and
Raksha (Poorvashadha-Moola) on Thursday, Brahma and Pitri (Rohini-
Magha) on Friday and Isha and Sarpa (Ardra and Ashlesha) on Sat-
urday-these Muhurtas should be rejected while doing some auspicious
deed {that is, an auspicious deed should not be done in these
Muhurtas).

THE NAKSHTRAS OF THE SAMSKARA OF MARRIAGE
AND THE VALUE OF THE ABHIJITA NAKSHTRA: The celebra-
tion of the Samaskara of marriage is auspicious in the ‘Vedha rahita’
(without Vedha) Nakshtras of Mrigashira, Hasta, Moola, Anuradha,
Magha, Rohini, Rewati, Uttaraphalguni, Uttarashadha,
Uttarabhadrapada, Swati, on the other Tithis except the Rikta Tithis
and the Amavasya Tithi and on auspicious days (Monday, Wednes-
day, Thursday and Friday).

The 4th Quarter of the Uttarashadha Nakshtra and the first 15th
part of the Shravana Nakshtra added together is the value (period)
of the Abhijita Nakshtra, that is, Abhijita is not a separate Nakshtra.

192 Muhurta Chinta Mani

THE PANCH SHALAKA VEDHA IN MARRIAGE: The pres-
ence of a Planet in any one of the pairs of the Nakshtras of Rohini
Abhijita, Bharani-Anuradha, Uttarashadha-Mrigashira, Shravana
Magha, Hasta-Uttarabhadrapada, Swati-Shatabhisha, Moola-Punarvasu,
Uttaraphalguni-Rewati causes the presence of ‘Vedha’, that is, if there
is a planet in any one of a pair then the other member of the pair is
called ‘Vidhdha’ (that is, the Nakshtra which has got ‘Vedha’). And
the presence of a Planet in these pairs of Nakshtras causes mutual
Vedha in the 4th-ist Quarters and the 3rd-2nd quarters, that is, there
is the Vedha of the 4th Quarter with the lst quarter and of the 3rd
Quarter with the 2nd quarter.

Notes: Pancha shiaka and Saptashlaka cycles are used in order
to have the knowledge of Vedha. Pancha Shlaka is used in order to
know of auspicious Muhurta for the coming of bride, giving of alms
and marriage and Sapta-Shlaka is used to know the time for doing
other deeds.

The Nakshtra Vedha is insignificant in the presence of the fol-
lowing yogas or combinations.

1. The Lagna or Ascendant at the Marnage time is occupied
by some benefic planet.

2. If the Ascendant Lord is in the | 1th House and is in con-
junction with and aspected by Benefic Planets.

3, The marriage is to be celebrated in the ‘Kaala Hora’ of a
Benefic Planet.

4. The Moon is endowed with strength.

SAPTA SHALAKA CHAKRA: In the ‘Sapta Shalaka Chakra’
there is mutual Vedha in these pairs of Nakshtras. Jyeshtha-Pushya,
Shatabhisha-Swati, Poorvashadha-Ardra, Rewati-Uttaraphalguni,
Dhanishtha-Vishakha, Uttarashadha-Mrigashira, Ashwini-
Poorvaphalguni, Ashiesha-Anuradha, Hasta-Uttarabhadrapada, Rohini-
Abhijita, Moola-Punarvasu, Chitra-Poorvabhadrapada, Bharani-Magha
and Kritika-Shravana.

Notes: In the ‘Sapta Shalaka Chakra’ also if the Vedha is with
a Benefic Planet, then only that quarter of the Nakshtra which has
got Vedha should be rejected, but if the Vedha is with a Malefic
Planet, then the full time of that Nakshtra should be rejected.

Generally 1 is the ‘Pancha Shalaka Chakra’ which is consulted
in reference to marriage, but ‘Deepika Kara’ (writers of books on
Astrology) Bhoja and Varaha Mihira are of the view that the Sapta
Shalaka Chakra should also be consulted. Bhoja writes:

The meaning is that the woman who is married in a Nakshtra
which has got Vedha in the ‘“Sapta Shalaka Chakra’ gets widowed
very soon.

Therefore, as far as possible, consideration should be done ac-
cording to both the Chakras, but higher recognition is to be given to
the ‘Pancha Shalaka Chakra’.

THE NAKSHTRA DOSHA DUE TO MALEFIC PLANETS
AND ITS REMOVAL: The Nakshtras which is subjected to “Vedha’
by the Malefic Planets, the Nakshtra which has just been traversed
by Malefic Planets, that is, the Nakshtra through which a Malefic
Planet has just passed and the Nakshtra in the period of which all
the three kinds of portents have occurred, if it (the Nakshtra) is tra-
versed by the Moon, it becomes auspicious for doing auspicious
deeds.

198 Muhurta Chinta Mani

THE DOSHA OR DEFECT NAMED KRANTI SAMYA OR
MAHA PAATA: If there is the Sun in one sign and the Moon in
the other sign of the pairs of signs of Leo-Aries, Taurus-Capricorn,
Libra-Aquarius, Virgo-Pisces, Cancer-Scorpio and Sagittanius-Gemini,
there occurs the Dosha or Defect named as Kranti Samya (Maha
Paata). It is not regarded as auspicious for doing auspicious deeds.

Notes: When there is the Sun in Leo and the Moon in Aries
or when the Sun is there in Aries and the Moon in Leo etc. there
occurs the ‘Kranti Samya Dosha’ or Defect. It is not auspicious for
doing auspicious deeds.

When the tota! of the Saayana Sun and Moon becomes 6 signs
(180°), there are different ‘Ayans’ and one ‘Gola’ (Sphere) and the
‘Bhujamshas’ (longitude) of both being equal, therefore, their Kranti
(declination) is also equal or same. It is this which is referred to as
the Mahadosha (great defect) of “Vyattpaat’

When the ‘Kranti’ (declination) of the Sun and the Moon is
equal or same, due to the conjunction of the same rays of both a
strange type of infected atmosphere is created which has been regard-
ed as a symbol of inauspiciousness for doing auspicious deeds. And,
therefore, auspicious deeds should not be done in ‘Vyatipaata’.

KHARJOOR OR EKARGALA DOSHA OR DEFECT: On the
day on which there is the inauspicious yoga (combination) of
Vyaghaata, Ganda, Vyatipaata, Shoola, Vishakambha, Vaidhriti. Vaira.

199 Marriage Sanskaras

Parigha or Atiganda, and the Moon is situated in the odd numbered
Nakshtra from the Nakshira of the Sun (the Nakshtra in which the
Sun is there) there occurs the Dosha or Defect named ‘Kharjoor’
which is not acceptable for celebrating marriage Samskar There is
the counting in it of all Nakshtras including the Abhijita Nakshtra.

Notes: If there is the situation when the Moon is in the same
number of Nakshtras in which is the Sun and therefore the day has
evil causing yogas (combinations) like Vyaghaata etc. the ‘Kharjoor
Dosha’ or Defect does not occur. The “Kharjoor Dosha’ or Defect
has another name also which is ‘Ekargala Dosha’ or Defect. Accord-
ing to the view of Narada the Abhijita Nakshtra is not counted in it.
See ‘Naradiya Samhita’ Chapter 7 Shiokas 5-7.

UPAGRAHA DOSHA (THE DOSHA OR DEFECT ARISING
BECAUSE OF SUB PLANETS): If the Moon is situated in the
Nakshtras numbered 5/8/10/14/7/19/15/18/21/22/23/24/25 from the
Nakshtra in which the Sun is there, then there is the Dosha or De-
fect referred to as ‘Upagraha Dosha’ or Defect. The ‘Upagraha Dosha’
or Defect is not regarded as auspicious in the region around Delhi
and in the Places called “Vahlika’ (West to Kashmir).

THE REMOVAL OF THE DOSHAS OF PAATA,
UPAGRAHA AND LATTA AND THE DOSHA OF
ARDHAYAMMA: The inauspicious quarter of a Nakshtra is only that
one in which there is the situation of the Graha or planet which has
caused the Dosha or Defect of Paata, Upagraha and Latta; that is,
only that Quarter of a Nakshtra is to be rejected in which, in the
Paata and upapraha Doshas, the Sun is situated; And in the Latta
Dosha or Defect, there is the Dosha only in that Quarter of a Nakshtra
in which the Planet which has caused the Dosha or Defect is situ-
ated.
The number of the day. Sunday etc, is multiplied by 3 and the

200 Muhurta Chints Mani

Product is divided by 8 and 1 is added to the remainder, and the
number thus arrived at is *Ardhayaama’

Notes: The 8th part of Dinamaana is cailed Ardha yaama. One
Ardhayaama is inauspicious every day. Marriage should not be cele-
brated in the inauspicious ‘Ardhayaama’. Suppose we have to know
of Ardhayaama on Wednesday. Then the number of Wednesday
(counting from Sunday) is 4 which is multiplied by 3 which gives
12. The remainder after dividing 12 by 8 comes to be 4. 1 (one) is
added to 4 which gives 5. Therefore, the 5th will be the inauspicious
Ardhayaama.

There are 8 ‘Ardhayaamas’ in each both day and Night. ‘Ardha
Yaama’ is regarded extremely defective or inauspicious in the regions
of Mithila, Bengal, Nepal etc.

THE ‘KULIKA MUHURTA’: On days Sunday etc. respective-
ly the 14th, 12th, 10th, 8th, 6th, 4th and the 2nd Muhutra is named
as ‘Kulika’ Muhurta. In Night it is the 13th, 11th, 9th, 7th, 5th, 3rd
and the lst Muhurta (subtracting 1 from cach of the above Muhurtas)
which are named ‘Kulika Muhurtas’ On Saturday, the last 151
Muhurta is 50 regarded as inauspicious.

THE SPECULUM SHOWING “ARDHA YAAMA’ AND ‘KULIKA’

201 Marriage Sanskaras

DAGDHA TITHI: If the Sun is situated in the signs of Sagit-
tarlus and Pisces then the 2nd Tithi; if in Taurus and Aquarius, the
4th Tithi; if in Cancer and Aries, the 6th Tithi; if in Virgo and Gem-
ini, the 8th Tithi; if in Leo and Scorpio; the 10th Tithi; and if in
Capricom and Libra, the 12th Tithi is named as the Dagdha Tithi’.

Notes: The Dagdha Tithis also are to be rejected in doing the
Samskara of marriage. But it is only in Madhya Pradesh region that
these Tithis are regarded as defective or inauspicious. The sage
Kashyapa refers to this fact when he writes:

7]
that is, the Dagdha Tithis, are rejected as inauspicious in the Madhya
Pradesh.

JAMITRA DOSHA: If in the 7th Sign from the Ascendant at
marriage or from the Moon there is situated some Planet, it causes
the Dosha or Defect named Jamitra’. The marriage Samskar is not
auspicious in it, that is, the Ascendant and the Moon of the 7th sign
from any planet is not auspicious.

The Jamitra Dosha or Defect is also caused by the Planet who
is situated in the 55th Navamsha from the Ascendant or the Moon.
This brief Jamitra Dosha or Defect is also specifically inauspicious
for marriage Samskar.

Notes: Suppose the Ascendant is in the 2nd Navamsha of the
sign of Gemini. Then there are the 8 Navamshas of Gemini and af-

ter this there will be 9 Navamshas of each of the Cancer, Leo, Vite

go, Libra, Scorpio signs and 2 Navamshas of the Sagittarius sign and

thus the Navamsha of Sagittanus will be the 55th which is extreme

ly inauspicious for marriage
oad

202 Muhurta Chinta Mani

If a Planet is situated in the 7th House from any Ascendant or
from the Moon, there is formed the rough Jamitra Dosha or Defect.
But that whole sign should not be regarded as inauspicious for the
marriage Samskar and should not be rejected. The more proper thing
will be to accept only the brief ‘Jamitra Dosha’. According to it the
Planets of the 7th Sign from the Ascendant and the Moon having
the same number of degrees as are those of the Ascendant or the
Moon will be those which will be rejected as inauspicious.

The Jamitra Dosha or Defect is removed if the Planet situated —
in the 7th sign from the Ascendant or the Moon at the time of mar-
riage gets the aspect of Benefic Planets.

This Dosha does not occur also if the Moon is in her highest
exaltation, in her own sign, in a friend’s sign or in the shadavarga
of Benefic planets. Vatsyana has said.

that is, the woman who is married at a time when there is Mercury
in the 7th sign from the Moon always remains dear to her husband.

According to Varahamihira, the Jamitra Dosha should be -
sidered only in the regions on the bank of the river Jamuna.

It has occurred in ‘Sheeghrabodha’

that is, the Jamitra Dosha caused by the Moon, Mercury, Jupiter and
Venus is the giver of auspicious effects and that caused by the Sun,
Mars, Satum and Rahu is the giver of inauspicious effects.

According to ‘Muhurta Martanda’

that is, if there is any planet in the 7th sign from the Ascendant or
the Moon, then that planet should be subtracted from the Longitude
of the Ascendant or the Moon, and the sign etc. which are obtained
thus should be converted into minutes and seconds. Then it is divid-
ed by 200, and if the Quotient comes to be 54. then there is no
Jamitra Dosha or Defect.

THE EFFECTS OF ‘DASHA YOGA’: If the remainder is 0
(zero) there will be fear of wind, if it is 1, fear of clouds, if 4, fear
of fire; if 6, fear of the king; if 10, fear of thief, if 11, death, if 15,
disease, if 18, fear of Vajra (a weapon); if 19, quarrel and; if 20,
fear of the destruction of wealth.

If after adding the numbers of the Nakshtras of the Sun and
the Moon the figure happens to be an ‘even’ one, then it should be
halved and 14 should be added to the figure thus arrived at. If after
adding the figure happens to be an odd one then it should be halved
after adding 1 (one) to it. After this 14 curved lines are to be drawn.
Beginning with that Nakshtra which is obtained in this way write the
names of all the Nakshtras including the Abhijita Nakshtra (that is
all the 28 Nakshtras) at the ends of these lines. The planet which is
there in a Nakshtra should also be written with that Nakshtra. If the
planet and the Moon fall in the same line, then it should be thought
that the Moon is subjected to ‘Vedha’ with that planet. This Vedha
is inauspicious.

Notes: Suppose on some day the Sun is in the Jyeshtha and
the Moon is in the Hasta Nakshtra. the serial nos. of these Nakshtras
are 18 and 13. Added together the total obtained is 31. If 31 is di-
vided by 27 the remainder comes to be 4. Therefore, it is proved
that there will be the fear of fire. The Hasta Nakshira for marriage
on that day should be regarded as inauspicious.

Here the total of the Serial Nos. of both the Nakshtras is 31
which is an odd figure, therefore 1 (one) is added to it and when it
(31 + £ = 32) is halved, the figure obtained is 16; and when 14 is add-
ed to 16 the figure obtained is 30, if 30 is divided by 28 (as after
adding 14 to 16 there comes the figure 30 which is more than the
Number of the Nakshtras otherwise it was nol to be divided by 28)
the remainder comes to be 2, that is, there comes the Bharani
Nakshtra. Now, drawing 14 curved lines the first Nakshtra to be writ-
ten will be Bharani. At the same-time, the planet is also written along

206 Muhurta Chinta Mani

of fire); if 4, then Raaj Baana (the Baana which causes the fear of
the king); if 6, then Chora Baana (the Baana which causes the fear
of thief); and if 1, then Mrityu Baana (the Baana which causes death).
This Dosha is popular in the region of Maharashtra, that is, it is in-
auspicious in the Maharashtra, state or region of India.

Notes: As stated in the brackets against each Baana, if there is
the ‘Roga Baana’ the possibility of bodily pain and disease to the
husband and wife always remains there; if there is the ‘Agni Baana’
there remains the fear of fire; if Raaj Baana, there remains the fear
of getting punishment from the king; if Chora Baana, there is the fear
of , loss of things etc.; and if ‘Mrityy Baana’, there remains the
fear of loss of life or death. The meaning is that the effects obtained
are those that are indicated by the names of the Baanas.

THE BAANA DOSHAS POPULAR IN NORTHERN INDIA:
The traversed degrees of the Sun on the related Tithi of the month
in which the Dosha of Baana is to be considered are to be estab-
lished in 5 places and to each of them is to be added respectively
6,3,1,8 and 4 and then each is to be divided by 9. If the remainder
is 5 in the First place then there will be 082 Baana’; if it is 5 in
the 2nd place, ‘Agni Baana’; if in the 3rd place, ‘Raaj Baana’; if in
the 4th place, ‘Chora Baana’, and if in the 5th place, ‘Mrityu Baana’.
And if the remainder is 5 after dividing the remainder of the total
of all the places by 9, then there will be ‘Sashalya Baana’.

Notes: The effects of these ‘Baanas’ are those that are indicat-
ed by their names and have been stated in the previous Shioka. Here
only Sashalya Baana has been added. The effect of Sashalya Baana
is the treatment by surgery, that is, the affected person will under
go surgical treatment.

Example:

Suppose the traversed degrees of the Sun are 17 Then these are
placed in 5 Places

The remainder when eack of these is divided by 9 is
respecitvely 5,2,0,7, and 3. Here it is in the first place that the re-
mainder is 5. Therefore, as long as the traversed degrees of the Sun
remain 17 on that day there will remain the ‘Baana' named ‘Roga
(indicative of disease)

If all the remainders are added together and the total is divided
by 9, the remainder comes to be 8 : 5+2+0+7+4+3+ 9= Remain-
der 8. If the remainder had been 5 then on that day there would have
been the Baana named ‘Sashalya’. Therefore, on that day there will
be only the presence of the ‘Roga Baana’.

THE REMOVAL OF BAANA IN THREE WAYS: According
to the difference of time, the Chora Baana and Roga Baana are to
be rejected it the night, the Agni Baana and Rajbaana in the day
and the Mrityu Baana in the morming and the evening Sandhyas.

According to the difference in days the Raj Baana is to be re-
jected on Saturday, Mrityn Baana on Wednesday, Agni Baana and
Chora Baana on Tuesday, and Roga Baana on Sunday.

According to the difference of the auspicious deeds to be done,
the ‘Roga Baana’ should be rejected in doing ‘Yagyopavecta
Samskar’, Agnibaana in the covering (by thatches etc.) of the house,
Raj Baana at the time when one starts to do a King’s service (or
Government Service), the Chora Baana in travelling; and the Mrityu
Baana in Marriage Samskar.

208 Muhurta Chinta Mani

THE ASPECTS OF THE PLANETS: The planets have their as-
pect with 1 (one) ¢charana on the 3/10 Houses from the House in
which they are situated, with 2 Charanas on the 9/5 Houses; with 3
Charanas on the 4/8 Houses; and with 4 Charanas (full aspect) on
the 7th House. Another speciality about the the aspects of the Plan-
ets is that Saturn has his (extra) aspect on the 3/10 Houses; Jupiter
on the 9/5 Houses, and Mars on the 4/8 Houses and all the planets
have their full aspect on the 7th House from the House in which they

THE CONSIDERATION OF NAVAMSHAS IN THE ASCEN-
DANT AT THE MARRIAGE TIME: If the Lord of the Navamsha
of the Ascendant at the time of marriage aspects the Navamsha sign
or the Ascendant or he is posited in either of them, then it is very
auspicious for the bridegroom.

And if the Lord of the 7th sign from the Navamsha which is.
there in the Ascendant aspects the 7th Navamsha, that is, his own
Navamsha or he is posited in his own Navamsha, or the Lord of the
7th Navamsha from the Navamsha sipn of the Ascendant aspects the
7th House from the Ascendant, then it is auspicious for the bride. If
the above is reversed, then it is inauspicious for both.

Notes: Suppose the Ascendant at the marriage time is Libra
which happens to be there in the Navamsha of Sagittarius. Then if
the Lord of Sagittarius Jupiter is situated in Gemini, then he aspects
the sign of Sagittarius, and this Jupiter aspects the sign of Libra as
well. Therefore this situation will be auspicious for the bridegroom
or if Jupiter is situated in Sagittarius or Libra, even then it will be
auspicious for the bridegroom. .

If Mercury, the Lord of the 7th sign (Gemini) from the
Navamsha sign (Sagittarius) aspects the sign of Gemini or of Sagit-

209 Marriage Sanskaras

tarius or if he is situtaed in the sign of Sagittarius, then it is auspi-
cious for the bride. If that very Mercury being situated in any House
in the Ascendant aspects the 7th House, even then it is auspicious
for the bride.

THE AUSPICIOUS OR INAUSPICIOUS SITUATION DUE
TO THE ASPECT OF THE ASCENDANT LORD AND THE
NAVAMSHA LORD: If the Lord of the Navamsha of the Ascendant
at the marriage time aspects the Navamsha, and the Ascendant Lord
aspects the Ascendant, then it is specially auspicious for the bride-
groom. Or if the Navamsha Lord aspects the Ascendant and the As-
cendant Lord aspects the Navamsha, even then the situation is indic-
ative of auspiciousness.

And if the Lord of the 7th sign from the Navamsha aspects the
7th sign from the Navamsha and the Lord of the 7th House from
the Ascendant aspects the 7th House from the Ascendant, then it is
auspicious for the bride. Or if the Lord of the 7th House from the
Navamsha aspects the 7th sign from the Ascendant, and the Lord of
the 7th sign from the Ascendant aspects the 7th sign from the
Navamsha, then it is very auspicious for the bride.

Notes: Here is an example to illustrate the statement made in
above Shloka, Suppose the Ascendant at the marriage time is Libra,
and the Navamsha sign is Sagittarius. Now if the Ascendant Lord
Venus aspects the Ascendant and in the Navamsha Chart Jupiter as-
pects the sign of Sagittarius, then it is an auspicious situation for the
bridegroom.

Or if the Navamsha Lord Jupiter is situated in the Horoscope
in a House from where he may aspect the Ascendant and the As-
cendant Lord Venus is situated in the Navamsha Chart in a House
from where he may aspect the sign of Sagittarius, even then it is an
auspicious situation for the bridegroom.

And in the Navamsha Chart Mercury, the Lord of the Gemini
sign, is situated in a House from where he may aspect the sign of
Gemini, and in the Horoscope Mars, the Lord of the 7th House is

210 Muhurta Chinta Mani

situated in a House from where he may aspect the sign of Aries in
the 7th House, then it will be auspicious for the bride.

Or in the Navamsha Chart Mercury who is the Lord of the 7th
House here is situated in the Horoscope in such a House from where
he may aspect the sign of Aries in the 7th House; And in the Horo-
scope, Mars who is the Lord of the 7th House here is situated in a
Navamsha Chart in a House from where he may aspect the sign of
Gemini in the 7th House, even then the situation will be auspicious
for the bride.

If the friend of the planet who is the Lord of the Navamsha
which is there in the Ascendant is a Benefic one and if he aspects
the Navamsha sign or the Ascendant, then it is said in the Shastras
{Books on Astrology) that the bridegroom who is going to be joined
in wedlock will have excellent effects.

And if the friend of the Lord of the 7th Navamsha is a Benefic
planet and if he aspects the 7th Navamsha or if he aspects the 7th
sign from the Ascendant, then it causes good effects for the bride.

THE SURYA SANKRANTI] DOSHA OR DEFECT: The 3
days, the days before and after and the day in the Middle in the
Vishuwa Sankranti (the Sankranti of the sign of Aries and of Libra)
and in the Ayana Sankranti (the Sankranti of Capricorn and of Can-
cer) are not acceptable in doing auspicious deeds. [In the other
Sankrantis (than the above ones) it is the 16 Ghatis before and the
16 Ghatis after the Sankranti which should be rejected by the Leammed
Astrologers as inauspicious.

211 Marriage Sanskaras

THE SANKRANTI GHATIS OF EVERY PLANET: 33 Ghatis
in the Sankranti of the Sun, 2 Ghatis in that of the Moon, 9 Ghatis
in that of Mars, 6 Ghatis in that of Mercury, 8&8 Ghalis in that of
Jupiter, 9 ghatis in that of Venus and 160 Ghatis in that of Saturn
should be rejected (as inauspicious for doing an auspicious deed). Of
these the Sankranti of the Sun is more inauspicious than those of the
other planets.

Notes: The movement of a planet from one sign to another is
referred to as the Sankranti or ingress of that Planet. When a Planet
moves from one sign into another, the Ghatis before and the Ghatis
after this movement both together have been referred to as the
Sankranti Ghatis of that planet.

The time in which the disc of a planet crosses the joining point
of two signs is called as the Sankranti Ghatis of that Planet. These
Sankranti Ghatis are different, are greater or less in number accord-
ing to the size of the Disc of a planet and the average speed of that
planet. Here the number of the Ghatis has been calculated according
to the Average Speed of the Planets.

THE ASCENDANTS NAMED AS PANGU (LAME), ANDHA
(BLIND) AND BADHIRA (DEAF): The Ascendants of Libra and
Scorpio become Badhira (Deaf) in the day time and those of Capri-
com and Sagittarius in the night time. The Ascendants of Leo, Aries
and Taurus become Andha (Blind) in the day time and those of
Virgo, Gemini and Cancer in the night time. The Ascendant of Aquar
ius beocmes Pangu (Lame) in the day time and that of Pisces in the
night time

Notes: The meanings and significance of the various Ascendants
are given in brackets. Thus Pangu means Lame, Andha means blind
and Badhira means deaf.

212 Muhurte Chinta Mani

THE ASCENDANTS NAMED AS PANGU, ANDHA AND
BADHIRA ACCORDING TO ANOTHER VIEW: The Ascendants of
Sagittarius, Libra and Scorpio are named as deaf in the afternoon;
those of Gemini, Cancer and Virgo are named as Andha (blind) in
night; those of Leo, Taurus and Aries are Andha (blind) in day; and
those of Capricorn, Aquarius and Pisces are Pangu (lame) in both the
the Sandhyas (of the morning and of the evening).

THE EFFECTS OF THE ASCENDANTS NAMED AS
BADHIRA ETC: If a marriage takes place in the Badhira Ascendants,
it causes poverty; if a marriage is done in day in the Andha Ascen-
dant the bride is widowed; there occurs the death of the issues in
case marriage is done in Night in the Ascendant named as ‘Andha’
and if a marriage takes place in the Ascendant named as Pangu there
occurs the destruction of wealth. But if there is the aspect of Jupiter
on the Ascendant or on the Ascendant Lord there occurs no Dosha
or Defect.

Notes: The effects are obtained in that very period in which an
Ascendant is called Andha, Pangu and Badhira. Thus, the Virgo As-
cendant has been called Andha in night, so its effect will be obtained

in night only

THE EXCELLENT NAVAMSHAS OF THE MARRIAGE OF
A DAUGHTER: If a marriage is done in the Navamsha of the Sag-
ittarius, Libra, Virgo, Gemini or Pisces sign, then the girl married thus
certainly remains faithful to her husband.

|

213 Marriage Sanskaras

A daughter should not be marvied in the last Navamsha, but if
the Navamsha of a sign is Vargottama and marriage is done in it,
then it is excellent. But if the Moon is situated in the Libra or Cap- .
ricorn sign, then marriage should not be done in the movable
Navamsha of a Movable Ascendant, that is, even Vargottama is not -
auspicious in this situation.

Notes: The 9th Navamsha of every sign is not acceptable in
marriage. But if that Navamsha is that of the sign itself then it is .
auspicious. For example, in the signs of Gemini, Virgo, Sagittarius,
and Pisces the 9th Navamsha is their own, therefore in the case of
any of these signs even the 9th Navamsha is auspicious.

If the Moon is placed in the sign of Libra or Capricom, then
there the Vargotiama of a Movable sign is also inauspicious.

THE CONSIDERATION: OF MARRIAGE IN A PARTICULAR
ASCENDANT: Saturn in the 12th House from the Ascendant at mar-
riage, Mars in the 10th House, Venus in the 3rd House, the Moon
and a Malefic planet in the Ascendant are not auspicious, And the
Aascendant Lord, Venus and the Moon, these in the 6th House, and
the Moon, the Ascendant Lord, Benefic Planets and Mars in the 8th
House and all the planets in the 7th House are not excellent or aus-
picious

Notes: According to some Acharyas (or Astrologers) Venus or
Mercury or Jupiter in the 7th House from the Moon endow the girl
with good fortune and son.

215 Marriage Sanskaras

ited in Angles (1/4/10) and Trines (9/5) then al] the Doshas, of the
year, Ayana, Ritu, Tithi, Month, Nakshtra, Paksha, Dagdha Tithi,
Andha Kona, Ascendants of Badhira etc. are destroyed, and the Dosha
of the Ascendant and the Navamsha with a Malefic Planet or with
the Moon is also destroyed.

Notes: Here only the 1/4/10 Angles will be taken and the 7th
House will not be taken although it is also an Angle.

OTHER COMBINATIONS WHICH REMOVE THE DOSHAS
OR DEFECTS: If in the Ascendant at marriage Jupiter is in an An-
gle (1/4/10) or in a Trine (5/9) or the Sun is in the 11th House, or
the Ascendant is in Vargottama or the Moon is in Vargottama
Navamsha, then all the Doshas are destroyed. And if the Moon is in
the 110 House, then the Durmuhurta and the ‘PAAPA NAVAMSHA’
Doshas are destroyed.

Notes: Here it has been indicated that the Doshas (Defects) are
removed if even one of the above stated planets is in the said House.

THE POWER OF THE PLANETS IN REMOVING DOSHAS
(DEFECTS): If Mercury is in a Trine or in one of the Angles ex-
cept the 7th House (that is, in the 1/4/10 Houses) he removes one
hundred Doshas; if Venus is in this Situation she removes 200
Doshas; if Jupiter is in this situation he destroys one lac Doshas; And
if the Ascendant Lord or the Navamsha Lord of the Ascendant is in
the Lith House or in the 1/4/10 Houses from the Ascendant, then he
destroys all the Doshas in the same manner as fire burns cotton.

217 Marriage Sanskaras

THE MUHURTA OF THE MARRIAGE OF THE HYBRID
RACES: If the marriage of the Hybrid Races (Yawanas, Mohammed-
ans, Rajakas, or washermen etc.) takes place on Saturday, Tuesday
and Sunday in the Krishna Paksha or the dark half of the month and
in those Nakshtras which have been rejected as inauspicious for do-
ing Marriage Samskar, then it (the marriage) gives sons, longevity,
profit of wealth and increases love.

SPECIAL CONSIDERATION IN THE GANDHARVA MAR-
RIAGE ETC.: In the Gandharva Marriage etc. the 4 Nakshtras from
the Nakshtra of the Sun to that of the Moon are inauspicious, 2
(ahead of it) are auspicious, 3 are inauspicious, 1 is auspicious, 1 is
1 is inauspicious and 3 are auspicious

Notes: Suppose the Sun is situated in the Nakshtra of Rohini
and the Moon in that of Punarvasu, then from Rohini to Punarvasu
there are 4 Nakshtras (including both the Ist and the last)- Rohini,
Mrigashira, Ardra and Punarvasu. In these 4 Nakshtras, Gandharva
Marriage is inauspicious; in the 2 Nakshtra ahead of it (Pushya and
Ahslesha) such marriage is auspicious; then in the 3 Nakshtra ahead
of Ashlesha (Magha, Poorvaphalguni and Uttaraphalguni) it is inaus-
picious. Auspiciousness and inauspiciousness of such Marriage is to
be considered in this order.

Manu has spoken of  kinds of Marriages 1. Prajapatya, 2.
Brahma, 3. Daiva, 4. Arsha (the time of marriage in these marriages
is considered according to the shastras) 5 Gandharva 6. Asura
7. Rakshasa and 8. Paishacha (The time of these marriages-from 5
to 8-is not mentioned in the shastras)

218 Muhurta Chinte Mani

THE AUSPICIOUS TIME OF THE OTHER DEEDS RELAT-
ED TO THE MARRIAGE SAMSKAR: Before marriage the strength
of the Moon of the bride and the bridegroom is to be seer or even
in the absence of it in the Nakshtras described in marriage, there
should be done for marriage preparing of pulses by getting them
ground, separating husk from paddy (for preparing rice), grinding the
wheat etc. decoration of the sacred Pitcher (called Mangal Kalasha
in Sanskrit), decoration of the house and its parlour, making of Vedi
and Mandap (Pavilian) etc. (Vedi is made for doing Havana or yajna
for burning the Sacred fire and pouring butter, spices, myrrh etc. into
it, Mandap ts the pavilian under which Vedi is made). But the above
stated deeds should not be started on the 3/6/9th day before marriage.

MEASURE OF THE VEDI AND THE MUHURTA FOR RE-
MOVING IT: In the left part of the marriage Mandap (Pavilian)a
Vedi of one hand of height and of 4 hands on each of the four-sides
should be prepared. The Marriage Mandap (Pavilian) should be
remvoed on the other even days than the 6th day (that is on the 2/4/
8/10 etc. days) and on the odd (5/7 etc.) days.

THE TIME OF ANOINTING (OILING) THE BRIDE ETC.
BEFORE MARRIAGE: The number of days in which the would be
bride born in the signs of Aries etc. and the child whose Upanayana
Samskar may be done are to be bathed and anointed (oiled) etc. be-
fore these Samskars has been- fixed. Thus the would be bride born
in the sign of Aries is to be anointed etc. on 7 days before marriage:
she who is born in that of Taurus, 10 days; in Gemini, 5 days: in
Cancer, 10 days, in Leo, 5 days: in Virgo, 7 days: in Libra. 7 davs

219 Marriage Sanskaras

in Scorpio, 5 days, in Sagittarius, 5 days, in Capricorn, 5 days, in
Aquarius, 5 days; and in Pisces, 7 days.

THE PLACE WHERE THE POLE OF THE MANDAP
(PAVILIAN) IS TO BE FIXED: If at the time of marriage the Sun
is in the signs of Virgo, Leo and Libra then the pole is to be fixed
in the Ishaana Kone (between the East and North direction), if he is
in Scorpio, Sagittarius, and Capricorn, in the Vayavya Kone (between
the North and West direction), if he is in Aquarius, Pisces and Ari-
es, in the Nairiritya Kone (between the South and West direction);
and if he is in Taurus, Gemini and Cancer, in the Agni Kone (be-
tween the South and East direction).

THE TIME OF GODHULI LAGNA (GODHULI OR
EVENING ASCENDANT): In the Godhuli Lagna no special Dosha
of Nakshtra, Tithi, Karana, Lagna, day, Navamsha, Muhurta, yoga,
the Situation of planets in the 8th House and Jamitra etc. is to be
considered. The Munis or the sages have praised the Godhuli Lagna
and have recommended it as auspicious for doing all deeds (religious).

KINDS OF THE GODHULI LAGNA: If in the season of
Hemanta (Margashirsha, Pausha, Magha and Phalguna) the Sun ap-

221 Marriage Sanskaras

104. TO KNOW THE LONGITUDE OF THE SUN AT A
GIVEN TIME: The ghatis and the Palas which have passed since the
time when the Sankranti of the Sun started to occur to the Ishta Kaala
of the Ascendant at Marriage or to the other desired Ishta Kaala, are
to be multiplied by the above stated motion (speed) of the Sun. The
product is to be divided by 60. The quotient in degrees etc. is to be
added to the number of signs which have been traversed by the Sun
and it-will-pive-theongitude- ofhe-Sun-at-a-civen time (Ishta Kaala).

TO KNOW THE DEGREES ETC OF THE DESIRED LAGNA:
The Navamsha number before the auspicious Navamsha in the aus-
picious Ascendant for marriage etc. which is desired to be known
should be multiplied by 10 and the product is to be divided by 3,
and the quotient thus obtained are the traversed degrees etc. of the
Ascendant for knowing the shadavarga.

Notes: There are 30 degrees and 9 Navamshas in a sign. There-
fore, in order to know the desired Navamsha in an Ascendant there
is the formula

30 « desired Navamsha 10 x Navamsha
9 3

Suppose in the Gemini Ascendant found out for marriage we
have to know the Navamsha of the Pisces sign. The first Navamsha
in the Gemini sign is of Libra, and the Navamsha of Aquarius from
the Navamsha of the desired sign of Pisces is the Sth one. Therefore
by multiplying 5 with 10 and then by dividing it by 3 we get 16°-
40". Therefore the Navamsha of Pisces will begin when the 16°-40'
of the Lagna of Gemini will have passed.

222 Muhurta Chinta Mani

THE KNOWLEDGE OF ISHTA KAALA FROM THE LON-
GITUDE OF THE ASCENDANT AND OF THE SUN: There will
be separately the to be traversed Palas of the Sun and the traversed
Palas of ihe Ascendant if the to be traversed degrees of the Sayana
Sun and the traversed degrees of the Sayana Ascendant are multi-
plied separately by their ‘Udayamaana’ or Palas at the time of rising
and each of them then is divided by 30. To the Sum of both (the
obtained to be traversed palas of the Sayana Sun and the traversed
palas of the Ascendant) are to be added the ‘Udaya Palas’ of the signs
between the Sun and the Ascendant and this should be divided by
60, and the figure thus obtained is the Ishta Kaala or the desired time
from the time of the Sunrise.

Notes: Here is an illustration. Suppose the Ascendant at mar-
riage time is 2~16°-40'-00". If Ayanamsha is added to it then we
get 2-16"- 40'- 0°+ 21 - 22’ --3" = 3 - 8- 2' - 3". If the
‘Udayamaana’ of Cancer, 342 Palas are multiplied by its traversed
degrees 8°- 2'-3" it gives 2747-41-6. If it is divided by 30 then are
obtained the traversed Palas of the Ascendant 91-35. By adding the
Ayanamsha 21°- 22' - 23" to the Longitude of the Sun 10 - 6°- 33' -
34" we get the Sayana Sun 10 - 27°- 55' — 57", By multiplying the
to be traversed degrees of the Sayana Sun (30°)— (27°— 55' — $7") = 2
4'3" with the Udaya Maana of Aquarius and by dividing the figure
thus obtained by 30 we get the to be traversed palas of the Sun 17/
26. If to the Sum of these 91/35 + 17/26 = 109/} are added the
“Udayamaanas’ of the signs between the Sayana Sun and the Ascen-
dant of Pisces, Aries, Taurus and Gemini (221 + 221 + 253 + 304) we
get 1108 palas. (If these Palas are divided by 60 then the Quotient
thus obtained is the Ishta Kaala 18/28; that is, the Navamsha of Pi-
sces in the Ascendant of Gemini will begin at 18/28 Ghatis and Palas
from the Sunrise.

223 Marnage Sanskaras

SPECIAL STATEMENT ON KNOWING THE ISHTA
KAALA: If the Sayana Ascendant and the Sayana Sun are in the
saine sign, then the Sun is subtracted from the Ascendant and the
remaining degrees etc. are multiplied by the Udayamaana of that sign
and the product is divided by 30, then the quotient thus obtained is
itself Ishta Kaala.

If the Sayana Ascendant is less than the Sayana Sun, then the
Ishta Kaala obtained in this way will be of before the Sunrise. If the
Ascendant is 10 be known in the night then 6 signs are added to the
Sun, the Ascendant etc. are to be known by the method stated above.

224 Muhurta Chinta Mani

THE DESCRITPION OF THE ABOVE STATED DOSHAS
WHICH ARE TO BE AVOIDED WHILE DOING THE MARRIAGE
SAMSKAR: Utpaata, Mahapaata, Dagdha Tithi, the evil combinations
like Vyatipaata etc. the combustion of the Moon, Jupiter, and Venus
Tithi Kshaya, Tithi Vridhdhi, Gandanta, Bhadra, the day of Sankranti,
the combustion of the Ascendant Lord and the Navamsha Lord, the
Dosha arising due to the situation of the Ascendant Lord, the
Navamsha Lord and the Moon in the 6/8 Houses, the ‘Shadavarga’
or divisional chart of the Malefic Planet, the Ascendant and the
Navamsha occupied by the Moon and the Malefic Planet, the
Chandrayudha Dosha, the Kharjoor Dosha, Dasha Yoga, Jamitra
Dosha, Latta Dosha, Vedha (Sapta Shalaka and Panchashalaka), Baana
Dosha, Paapa Kartari yoga, the Dusta or Evil Yoga (combination)
arising due to Tithi, Nakshtra, and Days, the Doshas or Defects of
Ardha Yaama, Kulika etc., the Nakshtra with a Malefic Planet, the
Naksthra traversed by a Malefic, the Nakshtra having an eclipse, the
Nakshtra in which a Malefic Planet is about to enter, the Nakshtra
in which there has been a portent, the Nakshtra in which a Ketu has
risen, the Nakshtra rising at Sandhya time, the Nakshtra which has
been subjected to Vedha, the Nakshtra for which there has been Plan-
etary war and the Ascendant with a planet-all these Doshas are to
be avoided while doing some auspicious deed like marriage etc.

35

CHAPTER 7
THE COMING IN OF THE BRIDE AFTER MARRIAGE

AUSPICIOUS TIME FOR THE COMING IN OF THE NEW-
LY WEDDED BRIDE: The coming in of the bride (to her husband’s
home) after marriage is regarded as auspicious during the 15 days
from the day on which the marriage is celebrated on the even days
(2/4/6/8/10/12/14) and if the days are odd then on 5/9/7th day.

If the coming in of the bride may not occur during the 16 days
after the day of marriage, then it is regarded auspicious during one
month on odd days (17/19/21/23/25/27/29th day); then  one
month during one year in the odd months and then after one year in
the odd years.

If the time of the coming in of the bride to her husband’s home
exceeds five years, then the coming in may be at any time accord-
ing to one’s own will,

Notes: The coming in of the newly wedded bride to her hus-
band’s home for the first time after marriage is really a part of mar-
riage. According to ‘Muhurta Martanda’ it is the time of the bride's
first meeting with her husband. In some provinces of India the
‘Baraat’ or the marriage party returned after marriage without the
bride because the bride used to be too young to go to her husband's
home. In the present times marriages are done al mature age. There-

226 Muhurta Chinta Mani

fore the coming in of the newly wedded bride occurs at the same
time when the marriage has taken place.

AUSPICIOUS TITH! AND NAKSHTRA FOR THE FIRST
COMING IN OF THE BRIDE TO HER HUSBAND’S HOME: The
first coming in of the bride to her husband’s home is auspicious in
the Nakshtras named as Dhruwa (Uttaraphalguni, Uttarashadha,
Uttarabhadrapada and Rohini), those named as Kshipra (Hasta,
Ashwini and Pushya), those named as Mridu (Mngashira, Rewati,
Chitra and Anuradha), Shravana. Dhanishtha, Moola, Magha and
Swati, on the other Tithis and days except the Rikta Tithis (4/9/14)
and Tuesday and Sunday.

SPECIAL CONSIDERATION REGARDING THE LIVING OF
THE BRIDE IN HER HUSBAND’S HOME IN THE FIRST YEAR
AFTER MARRIAGE: If after the marriage the bride lives in her hus-
band's home in the month of first Jyeshtha, she destroys the elder
brother of her husband, if she lives there in the first Adhi massa, she
destroys her husband, if in the first Ashadha she destroys her mother-
in-law, if in the first Pausha, she destroys the father-in-law, and if
in the first Kshaya Maasa she destroys her own self. And if after the
marriage she lives in her father’s house in the month of Chaitra she
destroys her father.

Notes: If the woman does not have ‘Jetha’ (husband’s elder
brother), father-in-law, mother-in-law and father, then there is no
Dosha or defect. Here the destruction of the relatives Jetha, father-
in-law etc means that evi] may happen te them in future.

4

3

CHAPTER 8

THE BRIDE’S SECOND COMING IN TO HER
HUSBAND’S HOME (CALLED DWIRAGMANA)

THE MUHURTA FOR THE DWIRAGMANA (The bride's po-
ing to her husband’s home for the second time after marriage): The
Muhurta (auspicious time) for the bride’s going to her husband’s -
home for the second time after her first coming in there is, in odd
years, when the Sun is in Aquarius, Aries, Scorpio,-when the Sun and
Jupiter are in a benefic house from the sign of Virgo, on auspicious
days, in the Ascendants of Gemini, Pisces, Virgo, Libra and Taurus,
and in the Nakshtras named as Laghu (small) (Hasta, Ashwini and
Pushya), those named as Dhruwa (Uttaraphalguni, Uttarashadha,
Uttarabhadrapada, and Rohini), those named as Chara or Movable
(Swat, Punarvasu, Shravana, Dhanishtha and Shatabhisha), those
named as Mridu or Delicate (Mrigashira, Rewati, Chitra, Anuradha)
and that of Moola-in these 17 Nakshtras.

228 Muhurta Chinta Mani

THE CONSIDERATION OF VENUS ON THE BRIDE’S GO-
ING TO HER HUSBAND’S HOME FOR THE SECOND TIME: [f
at the time of going on a journey Venus happens to be in front and
on the right side, then the child on his first journey, and the preg-
nant woman and the newly wedded woman should not go on the jour-
ney, because the first journey of the child, and the journey of the
pregnant woman and of the newly wedded woman cause respective-
ly death of, abortion of and sterility in the person concerned.

Notes: Venus rises in the east or in the west. When the Spashta
(Longitude) of Venus is more than the Spashta (Longitude) of the
Sun, then she rises in the west. In this condition if the journey is
done towards the west then Venus will be in front, and if it is done
towards the south then she is on the right side. Therefore, leaving
the West and the South the journey towards the East and the North
may be undertaken. On the other hand if the Spashta of Venus is
less than that of the Sun, then Venus rises in the morning in the east.
In this condition she is in front in the journey towards the east and
on the right side in that towards the north. Therefore, leaving the east
and the north, journcy towards the west and the south may be un-
dertaken. It will be auspicious. The woman who is pregnant and also
who has been newly delivered of a child should always have this con-
sideration of Venus.

THE REMOVAL OF THE DOSHA OR DEFECT OF VENUS
WHEN SHE IS IN FRONT: At the time of going from one city to
another, on the coming of some emergency condition in the country,
when going to join a marriage (in the marriage party), in the pilgrim-
age to some temple (or other holy place) in the condition when one
is subjected to the oppression of the king and has to go to another
place and the coming in for the first time of the newly wedded brice
to her husband’s home- in these conditions-Venus’s being in front
does not cause any Dosha or Defect.

229 The Bride’s- Second Coming in to her Husband’s Home

THE OTHER REMOVAL OF THE DOSHA OR DEFECT OF
VENUS WHEN SHE IS IN FRONT: If the rising or emerging, of
the breasts of a married woman happens to be in her father’s house
and if she has menses there, then the Dosha or Defect of Venus’s
being in front is not there. And to the issues of the Gotra or family
of Bhrigu, Angira, Vatsa, Vashishtha, Kashyap, Atri, Bharadwaj of
these seven sages-there does not occur the Dosha or Defect of Ve-
nus’s being in front.

Notes: In the ancient Times the marriage of a daughter was
done before puberty or before the emerging of breasts and starting
of menses. The Dosha or Defect of Venus’s being in front has been
said for the bride of that age.

35

CHAPTER 9
THE DOING OF YAJNAS

THE DOING OF YAJNA OR THE WORSHIP OF FIRE THE
MUHURTA (AUSPICIOUS TIME) FOR AGNYADHAAN OR WOR-
SHIP OF FIRE: The doing of Agnihotra (Yajna) is auspicious at the
time when the Sun is in Uttarayana (moving towards the North) on
the other Tithis except those of Rikta (4/9/14) in the Nakshtras named
as Mishra or mixed (Vishakha and Kritika), in those named as
Dhruwa or Sthira (the 3 Uttaras and Rohini), in those of Rewati,
Mrigashira, Jyeshtha, and Pushya-in these 10 Nakshtras.

The doing of Yajna or Havana is inauspicious if the Moon,
Mars, Jupiter and Venus are in their debilitation signs, are combust,
are defeated in the Planetary war and are in the enemy signs.

Notes: The worship of fire (the Havana bearing the name of
Jyotishtoma etc) which is done through the Vedic and the Smarta
deeds is known by the name of Agnyadhan or Agnihotra. For the
Yajna after doing the worship of fire it is put into the Samidhas (piec-
es of wood), it is also ‘Agnyadhana’. The Agnyadhana Yajna is. re-
garded .as the Mouth. It ts done by men with their wives.

a

39

CHAPTER 10

THE CORONATION OF A KING

THE MUHURTA FOR CORONATION: The auspicious
Muhurta for the Coronation of a king is when the Sun is in
Uttarayana {moving towards the North and is in the signs from Cap-
ricoin to Gemini), Jupiter, the Moon and Venus are not combust,
Mars, the Sun, the Lord of the Natal Ascendant, the Lord of the
present Dasha of the would-be-king and the Lord of the Janama Rashi
or sign are endowed with strength.

Coronation should not be done in the Chaitra month, on the
Rikta Tithis (4/9/14), in the night on Tuesday and in the Adhimaas.

Notes: The sacred bathing along with the chanting of Mantras
(spells) according to the specific Vedic manner of a king’s son is
named as ‘Rajyabhisheka’. Coronation or putting on the Crown is the
English equivalent to ‘Rajyabhisheka’. There is no need of selecting
a Muhuria for the Rajyabhisheka of a Prince if it takes place after
the death of the king.

233 The Coronation of a King

THE NAKSHTRAS AND THE AUSPICIOUS LAGNAS OR
ASCENDANTS FOR THE CORONATION: The Rajyabhisheka or
Coronation of a King is auspicious in the Nakshtras of Jyeshtha,
Sharvana, those named as Kshipra (Hasta, Ashwini and Pushya), those
named as Mridu (Mrigashira, Rewati, Chitra and Anuradha), those
named as Dhruwa or Sthira (the 3 Uttaras and Rohini), in the As-
cendant rising with its head (Gemini, Leo, Virgo, Libra Scorpio and
Aquarius) or the King’s natal Ascendant and in the Ascendant of the
3/6/10/t1 Houses from the Janama Rashi or sign, when the Malefic
Planets are in the 3/6/11 Houses and when there are Benefic Planets
in the Angles, the Trines and 11/2/3 Houses.

THE EFFECTS OF THE LAGNA OR ASCENDANT AND
THE REMOVAL OF THE DOSHAS OR DEFECTS: If there are
Malefic Planets in the Ascendant at the time of the Rajyabhisheka
the king will be affected by diseases; if there is a malefic planet in
the 8th House, then there will be the occurrence of death; if there is
a Malefic Planet in the  House, then there will be trouble and pain
to the King’s son; if there are Malefic Planets in the 2nd and the
120 House the King will suffer from poverty, if there is a Malefic
Planet in the Oth House, he will be subjected to indolence or lazi-
ness; if there are Malefic Planets in the 4th and the 7th Houses, the
King will be without rights, and if there are Benefic Planets in the
Angles then it is auspicious in every way.

THE YOGA OR COMBINATION OF IMMOVABLE PROP-
ERTY ON THE BASIS OF THE RAJYABHISHEKA LAGNA: If
Jupiter is in the Ascendant or in a Trine, Mars in the 6th House and
Venus in the 10th House, then the king is endowed with Rajya

234 Muhurta Chinta Mani

Lakshmi (Royal wealth) and happiness. And if Saturn is in the 3rd,
the Sun in the Iith, and Jupiter, in the 10th or in the 4th House,
then the King’s land (Kingdom) always remains immovabie or fixed,
that is, there always remains stability in the kingdom of the king.

CHAPTER 11
JOURNEYS AND TRAVELS

To those Kings (persons) whose ‘Janama Kundali’ or horoscope
is known according to their time of birth, the Muhurta (auspicious
time) for journeys and travels should be told through the medium of
their Janama Kundalis or horoscopes, and those who have no know!l-
edge of the time of their birth should be instructed about the Muhurta
of a journey according to the knowledge of the auspicious and the
inauspicious situations of ‘“Shakun’ or ‘Omens’ and of Horary Sys-
tem.

Notes: The Muhurta or auspicious time selected for attacking
an enemy which happens to be an as auspicious Tithi, on an auspi-
cious day, in an auspicious Nakshtra, in an auspicious Ascendant is
a very important travel Muhurta for a king. The travel Muhurta for
commercial purposes, for a pilgrimage or for other deeds is an ordi-
Nary one.

In this Chapter the author has described the Muhurtas for a
king. But the king is merely a representative. The word ‘King’ does
not refer merely to a king, but to an ordinary man as well, as the
Muhurta is meant for everybody

236 Muhurta Chinta Mani

THE QUESTION INDICATING AUSPICIOUS EFFECTS IN A
JOURNEY : If the Janama Rashi or the Janama Lagna is there in
the Horary Ascendant or if the Lords of both these signs are in the
Lagna or Ascendant or any Sign happening to be in the 3/6/10/11
Houses from the Janama Rashi, or the Janama Lagna is also there in
the Horary Ascendant, then the journey of the king gives him victo-
ry.

Notes: In this Shloka 6 Yogas or combinations are formed due
to the difference of Janama Rashi and the Janama Lagna.

I. Suppose the Janama Rashi of the king is Pisces, that is, the
Moon in the horoscope is in the sign of Pisces. At the time when
the question is asked this sign happens to be in the Ascendant or in
the First House. This will form one Yoga.

II. Another Yoga is formed if the Rashi in the Ascendant hap-
pens to be in the Ascendant also at the time when the question is
asked.

IT]. If the Lord of the Janama Rashi is situated in the Ascen-
dant of the Horary Chart

IV. If the Lord of the Ascendant in the Horoscope is situated
in the Ascendant of the Horary Chart.

V. The Janama Rashi is, as we supposed, in Pisces. Then if the
Signs in the 3/6/10/11 Houses from Pisces, that is, the signs of Tau-
rus, Leo, Sagitiarius and Capricom happen to be there in the Ascen-
dant of the Horary Chart.

VI. Suppose the natal Ascendant is Sagittarius, then the signs
that are there in the 3/6/10/11 Houses from the Ascendant, that is,
Aquarius, Taurus, Virgo and Libra are in the Ascendant in the Horary
Chant.

240 Muhurta Chinta Mani

9. THE AUSPICIOUS AND INAUSPICIOUS EFFECTS OF
TITHIS AND NAKSHTRAS IN A JOURNEY : A Journey is not aus-
picious if it is undertaken on the Tithis of 6th, 12th, 8th, the Pratipada
or the First Tithi of the Shukla Paksha (or the bright half of the
Month), Amavasya or the 30th Tithi, Pauranmasi or the 15th Tithi
and on the Tithis named as Rikta (4/9/14). 1 (the Journey) is auspi-
cious if it is undertaken in the Nakshtras of Ashwini, Punar Vasu,
Anuradha Mrigashira, Pushya, Rewati, Hasta, Shravana and
Dhanishtha.

Notes: If the Tithis which have been rejected as inauspicious

are Sidhaha etc due to the Yoga or combination of day etc, then the *

journey wiil be auspicious. Vashishtha clearly says in this respect -
That is,
The Tithis which form the evil Yogas like Dagdha etc. should
altogether be rejected. In connection with Nakshtras also it should be
kept in mind that if in the 9 Nakshtras which have been told as aus-

Picious for undertaking a journey there are formed evil Yogas or
Combinations like Yamaghanta etc. they should be rejected.

241 Journeys and Travels

10. THE CONSIDERATION OF *VAAR SHOOLA’ OR
SHOOLA ON THE DAY AND NAKSHTRA SHOOLA IN A JOUR-
NEY : The wise person who is desirous of getting wealth, victory
and life should not go in the Eastern direction in the Jycshtha
Nakshtra and on Monday and Saturday, in the Southern Direction in
the Poorvabhadrapada Nakshtra and on Thursday, in the Western Di-
rection on Sunday and Friday and in the Rohini Nakshira, and in the
Northern Direction in the Uttara phalguni Nakshtra and on Tuesday
and Wednesday.

Notes: The word ‘Shoola’ means a disturbing thing or the thing
which puts obstacles on the way of doing something. Therefore, “Vaar
Shoola’ etc should be taken to mean the failure of some purpose of
3 journey in a certain direction on a certain day or in a certain
Nakshtra etc.. The word Shoola also mean intense pain. Therefore,
if a journey is undertaken in a Nakshtra or on a day which has
‘Shoola’, the person undertaking the journey gets intense pain.

11. THE KAALA SHOOLA (SHOOLA RELATING TO TIME)
ON THE BASIS OF THE DIVISION OF DAY AND NIGHT AND
THE NAKSHTRA: Journey should not be undertaken in the
‘Poorvanha’ (Forenoon. If the Dinmaana is divided into 3 equal parts,
then the first part is called ‘Poorvanha’, the second one ‘Madhyanha’
and the third one ‘Apranha’) in the Nakshtras named as Dhruwa and
Mishra (the 3 Uttaras, Rohini, Vishakha and Kritika}; in the
Madhyanha (Noon) in the Nakhstras named as Tikshana (Moola,
Jyeshtha Ardra, and Ashlesha); and in the Apranhha (afternoon) in
the Nakhstras named as Laghu (Hasta, Ashwini, Pushya and Abhijita)

And the undertaking of a journey should not be done in the
Poorva-Ratri (forenight. If the Ratrimaana is divided into 3 parts, then
the first part is called Poorvaratri, the second one Madhya Ratri, and
the third one Antima-Ratri) in the Nakhstras named as Mitra
(Mrigashira, Rewati, Chitra, Anuradha); in the Madhya Raatri (Mid-
night) in the Nakshtras named as ‘ugra’ (Poorvaphalguni
Poorvashadha, Poorvabhadrapada, Bharani, Magha), and in the Antima
Ratri (Afternight) in the Nakshtras named as Chara or Movable
(Swati, Punarvasu, Sharavana, Dhanishtha and Shatabhisha)

But journey is auspicious in all times in the Nakshtras of
Sharavana, Hasta, Pushya and Mrigashira).

12. THE GHATIS OF THE KROORA OR CRUEL
NAKSHTRAS WHICH SHOULD BE REJECTED: The 16 first (of
the beginning) ghatis of the Nakshtras of the three Poorvas, 21 ghatis
of the Kritika Nakhstra, 11 ghatis of Magha, 7 ghatis of Bharani, and
14 ghatis of the Swati, Vishakha, Jyeshtha and Ashlesha Nakshtras
Should be rejected as inauspicious for doing any auspicious deed.

Notes: Here the ghatis of the Nakshtras which should be re-
jected as inauspicious are so if we suppose the value of the

Nakshtras to be 60. Therefore, the real value of these ghatis should
be found out according to the proportion.

243 Journeys and Travels

As a matter of fact the above stated Nakshtras are not auspi-
cious for making journeys in their periods. Therefore if it is neces-
sary to make a journey in their periods, those ghatis should be avoid-
ed and the journey should be taken after their expiry.

13. THE GHATIS WHICH SHOULD BE REJECTED AC-
CORDING TO ANOTHER VIEW: In a journey for the fulfilment of
one’s desire of getting victory the first half of the Nakshtras of
Kritika, Magha and Swati, and the 2nd half of the Nakshtras of
Chitra, Ashlesha and Bharani should be rejected as inauspicious, and
according to the view of Shukracharya, the Nakshtras of Swati and ‘
Magha should be completely rejected as inauspicious.

14. THE 1526, MRITA, KARTARI AND GRASTA NAMES
OF THE NAKSHTRAS: Thirteen (13) Nakshtras traversed by Rahu
are named as ‘Jeeva paksha’ and they are auspicious, Thirteen(13)
Nakhstras which are to be traversed by Rahu are named as Mrita
Paksha’ and they are inauspicious. The Nakhstra in which Rahu is
Situated is named as ‘Kartari’ and the 15th Nakshtra from the
Nakshtra named as ‘Kartari’ is named as Grasta.

Notes: The imaginary Planet Rahu has retrograde motion. There-
fore the Nakshtras ahead of the Nakshtra in which Rahu is situated
are the ‘Traversed Nakshtras, and those which are behind him will
be called the ‘To be traversed’ Nakshtras. Thus if Rahu is in the
Swati Nakshtra then, Vishakha, Anuradha and so on which are ahead
of him will be the Traversed Nakshira’s, and Chitra, Hast:,

244 Muhurta Chinta Mani

Uttaraphaiguni and so on which are behind him will be the ‘to be
traversed Nakshtras’. In this way the 13 Nakshtras from ‘Swati’ to
Rewati will be the “Traversed Nakshtras’ which will be named as
‘Jeeva Paksha’: and the 13 Nakshtras behind Swati upto Bharani will
be the ‘to be traversed Nakshtras’ and will be named as ‘Mrita
Paksha’. Swati will be called ‘Kartari’ and the 15th Nakshtra from
Swati which is Ashwini will be called ‘Grasta’.

15. THE EFFECTS OF THE NAKSHTRAS WHICH ARE
NAMED AS JEEVA PAKSHA ETC-.: If the Sun is in the Nakshtras
named as ‘Mrita Paksha’ and the Moon is situated in those named
as ‘Jeeva Paksha’ then the journey is auspicious. In the reverse con-
dition there is loss in the journey. And if during the time of the jour-
ney both the Sun and the Moon are in the Nakshtras named as ‘Jeeva
Paksha’ the journey is auspicious. The Nakshtras named as ‘Grasta’
are more auspicious in comparison to those named as ‘Mrita Paksha’
and those named as ‘Kartari’ are more auspicious than those which
are named as ‘Grasta’.

And if the ‘Chandra Yayi’ (the king who goes out to conquer
his enemy) and ‘Surya Sthhayi’ {the king who is sitting in his cas-
tle) both these are in ‘Jeeva Paksha’, then both the kings make a
mutual treaty. If the Moon be in ‘Jeeva Paksha’, then the ‘Yayi’ gets
victory; and if the Sun is there in ‘Jeeva Paksha', then it is the
*Sthayi" who gets victory.

Notes: This shioka implies the following things:

|. If the Sun is in ‘Mrita Paksha’ and the Moon is in ‘Jeeva
Paksha’, the journey proves to be auspicious effects giving.

2. If the Moon be in ‘Mrita Paksha’ and the Sun be in ‘Jeeva
Paksha’, the journey is the giver of inauspicious effects.

3, If both the Sun and the Moon are in ‘Jeeva-Paksha’, then
the journey is the giver of auspicious effects.

245 | Journeys and Travels

4. Contrary to it, if the Sun be in ‘Mrita Paksha’, then the Jour-
ney proves to be greatly destructive, it brings total destruction.

5. From the point of View of auspiciousness ‘Mrita Paksha’ is
at the lowest level. The writer says that ‘Grasta Nakshtra’ is more
auspicious than ‘Mrita Paksha’, and ‘Kartari Nakshtra’ is more aus-
picious than the ‘Grasta onc’.

6. One who attacks another country (state) with the desire of
getting victory is called ‘Yayi’ or one who files a case in the court
in order to get what belongs to him and is his right is called vadi or
plaintiff. The Planet who has been appointed to be the Lord of these
is the Moon, One who is attacked upon is called ‘sthayi’ (stable).
And one against whom a case is filed in the Court is referred to as
‘Defendant’. The Planet who is the Lord of these is the Sun. There-
fore, here the journey-makers have been said to be of two kinds: one
is ‘Yayi’ and the other is ‘Sthayi’ or one is plaintiff and the other is
Defendant.

7. If both the Moon and the Sun be in ‘Jeeva Paksha’, then
both the kings or both the plaintiff and the Defendant make treaty
with each other, that is, the situation of both the Sun and the Moon
in ‘Jeeva Paksha’ does not indicate the defeat of either of them.

8. If the Moon be in ‘Jeeva Paksha’ and the Sun be in ‘Mrita
Paksha’, then Yayi or plaintiff gets victory and the Sthyayi or the
Defendant gets defeated.

9. If the Sun be in ‘Jeeva Paksha’ and the Moon be in ‘Mrita
Paksha’, then it is the ‘Sthayi’ or the Defendant who gets victory.

10. If both the Sun and the Moon be in ‘Mrita Paksha’ then
both the kings or both the plaintiff and the Defendant get defeated.

246 Muhurta Chinta Mani

16, 17. NAKSHTRAS NAMED AS AKULA, KULA-KULA
AND KULA: The Nakshtras of Swati, Bharani, Ashlesha, Dhanistha.
Rewati, Hasta, Anuradha, Punarvasu, those named as Dhruwa (the 3
Uttaras and Rohini), the odd Tithis (1/3/5/7/9/11/13/15) and the days
of Monday, Saturday and Thursday, each of these is named 1
‘Alaa’, the Nakshtras of Moola, Shatabhisha, Ardra and Abhijita, the
2/6/10 Tithis and the day of Wednesday each of these is named as
‘Kulakula’; and the Nakshtras of the 3 Poorvas, Ashwini, Pushya.
Magha, Mrigashira, Shravana, Kritika, Vishakha, Jyeshtha and Chitra,
the days of Friday and Tuesday and the [2/8/14/4 Tithis, each of
these is named as ‘Kula’.

If the war is begun in the Nakshtra, the Tithi and the Day
named as ‘Akula’ (or if the case is filed in these) then it is the Yayi
(the plaintiff if it is a case in the Court} who gets victory, if it is
begun in the Nakshtra, the Tithi and the Day named as ‘Kula’, then
it is the ‘Sthayi’ (the Defendant in a case in the court) who gets vic-
tory, and if it is begun in the Nakshtra, the Tithi and the Day named
as ‘Kulakula’, then both make treaty of peace between them.

18. (On the Way) THE SPECULUM OF PATHI-RAHU :
Dharma (Religion), Artha (Economic condition), Kama (Sexual De-
sire), and Moksha (Relief from the cycle of birth and death), these
four ways are resorted to in order to divide the Nakshtras in the Pathi-
Rahu speculum in which Seven Nakshtras - Ashwini, Pushya,
Ashlesha, Dhanishtha, Shatabhisha, Vishakha, Anuradha are said to
be of the ‘Dharma’ Marga (Path); Seven Nakshtras - Bharani, Poorva
bhadrapada, Jyestha, Shravana, Punarvasu. Magha and Swati, - are

Pratipada (1) of both the Pakshas (Shukla and Krishna), and in the
other months like ‘Magha’ etc from the second Tithi (2) etc should
be written 12 Tithis in 12 Brackets (After writing upto 12th Tithi,
should be written Pratipada etc.). The Tithis of 13, 14, 15 should be
understood like those of 3/4/5.

Notes: In order to know the auspicious and inauspicious Tithis
in the various months for making journey in the direction of East
etc. the writer gives direction to make a table. There will be in the
table 12 cells for each of the 12 Months beginning from the month
of Pausha. In the Cells of the Month of Pausha the Tithis which will
be written will be from the Pratipada (1) Tithi to the 12th Tithi; in
those of the Magha month from the 2nd Tithi to the 12th Tithi, and
then the cell which remains vacant will have the Pratipada Tithi in
it; in those of the Phalguna month from the 3rd Tithi to the 12th
Tithi, and then in the 2 celis which remain vacant will have the
Pratipada and the 2nd Tithi in them. This order of writing will be
there to the last month, that is, the month of ‘Marga Shirsha’.

The speculum is given Shloka 23
