24. THE KNOWLEDGE OF AUSPICIOUS AND INAUSPI-
CIOUS MUHURTAS THROUGH NUMBERS: The sum of numbers
of Tithi, day and Nakshtra should be put in three places and these
should be divided respectively by 7, 8 and 3. If after dividing the
remainder is zero in the First Place, then the person making the jour-
ney will get much grief in the journey, if the remainder is zero in
the Second Place, then there will be 1055 of wealth (Money) to him;
and if the remainder is zero in the Third Place,then there occurs the
death of the person going on the journey. if after dividing the re-
mainder is some number in all the three places, then the person go-
ing on the journey will get comfort and success.

Notes: Here is an illustration: Suppose on the 5th Tithi of any
month there is the Hasta Nakshtra on Wednesday. Then, the Num-
ber of the Tithi is 5, of the day is 4, and of the Nakshtra is 13. The
total is 5+4+13 = 22. After dividing it by 7 the remainder is 1; by
8, 6, and by 3, 1, Here the remainder in all the 3 places is some
number (1, 6, 1), therefore, the person making the journey will get
comfort and success in the journey.

25. THE DOSHAS (DEFECTS) OF MAHADALA AND
BHRAMA : If after dividiag by 7 the number which is arrived at
from the Nakshtra in which the Sun is there to that in which the
Moon is there, the remainder comes to be 2 and 7 (that is, zero),
then there is the Dosha (Defect) named as ‘Mahadala’: and if the re-
mainder comes to be 3 and 6, then there is the Dosha named
“Bhrama’. Both these Doshas are to be avoided while making a jour-
ney.

252 Muhurta Chinta Mani

26. THE YOGA (COMBINATION) NAMED HIMBER : To the
number which is asriygd al by counting from the Nakshtra in which
the Sun is there to that in which there is the Moon is to be added
the number which ts: arrived at by counting from the Pratipada Tithi
of the Paksha (Shukla or Krishna) to the Tithi Number of the day
on which the journey is 10 be made. If after dividing the Sum thus
arrived at the remainder happens to be 7, then there is the Yoga
named as Himber. This Yoga is excellent for making a journey.

Notes: There is the description of the 0835 named ‘Dhabar’,
‘Jelaka’ and ‘Gaurava’ in the Sanskrit Commentary of the present
book named as‘Peeyush Dhara’ which is as follows :

(i) DHABAR YOGA :

If after multiplying by 3 the number which is arrived at by
counting from the Nakshira in which the Sun is there to that in which
there is the Moon, and after adding the date of the journey to it and
dividing the Sun by 7 the remainder happens to be 3, then there is
the Yoga named “Dhabar’. The Yoga named “Dhabar’ is better than
that named Himber and it is excellent for doing all kinds of deeds.
Some astrologers cali it “Ghabar’ also.

(ii) TELAKA YOGA :

that is, to the number which is arrived at by counting from the
Nakshtra in which the Sun is there to that in which there is the Moon
should be added the numbers of the Tithi and of the day (added to-
gether) on which the journey is to be made. If after dividing the Sum
by 7 the remainder happens to be 5, then there is the ‘Telaka Yoga’.
This Yoga is auspicious for the thieves.

253 Journeys and Travels
(iii) GAURAVA YOGA:

that is, to the number which is arrived at by counting from the
Nakshtra in which the Sun is there to that in which there is the Moon
should be added the numbers of the Tithi and of the day (added to-
gether) on which the journey is to be made. If after dividing the Sum
by 12 the remainder happens to be 9, then there is the ‘Gaurava
Yoga. If this ‘Yoga is there at the time of entrance, then it is auspi-
cious.

27. THE GHAATA CHANDRA DOSHA :: If the Moon of the
persons whose Rashi be Aries (in whose horoscope the Moon is in
Aries) be there in the First House (that is, the Moon be in Aries it-
self), of those whose Rashi is Taurus be there in the Sth House (that
is the Moon be there in the Virgo Sign), of those whose Rashi is
Gemini be there in the 9th House; of those whose Rashi is Cancer
be there in the 2nd House; of those whose Rashi is Leo be there in
the 6th House; of those whose Rashi is Virgo be there in the !0th
House, of those whose Rashi is Libra be there in the 3rd House, of
those whose Rashi is Scorpio be there in the 7th House, of those
whose Rashi is Sagittarius be there in the 4th House, of those whose
Rashi is Capricorn be there in the 8th House, of those whose Rashi
is Aquarius be there in the 11th House; and of those whose Rashi is
Pisces be there in the 12th House: then there occurs the Dosha named
‘Ghaata Chandra’ (the Pain giving Moon). This ‘Ghaata Chandra’
should be avoided by a person when he goes to join a king's ser-
vice (or government service), to make a quarrel, to fight a battle. 
does not need to be avoided in other things.

254 Muhurta Chinta Mani

28, 29. THE QUARTERS OF THE NAKSHTRAS WHICH
SHOULD BE AVOIDED IN GHAATA CHANDRA : The natives
having Anes as their Rashi (the sign in which the Moon is situated
in the horoscope) should reject as inauspicious the first Quarter of
the Kritika Nakshtra; those having Taurus, the second quarter of
Magha; those having Gemini, the 3rd Quarter of Shatabhisha; those
having Cancer, the 3rd Quarter of Magha; those having Leo, the Ist
Quarter of Dhanishtha; those having Virgo, the 3rd Quarter of Ardra
those-having Libra, the second Quarter of Moola: those having Scor-
pio, the 4th Quarter of Rohini; those having Sagittarius, the 3rd Quar-
ter of Poorva bhadrapada; those having Capricom the 4th Quarter of
Magha, those having Aquarius, the 4th Quarter of Moola; and those
having Pisces, the 3rd Quarter of Poorvabhadrapada. This has been
said by the Munis (Thinkers).

30. THE GHAATA TITHIS : The Poorna 11185 (5/10/15) for
the natives having Taurus, Virgo and Pisces as their Rashis; the
Bhadra Tithis (2/7/12) for those having Gemini and Cancer as their
Rashis, the Nanda Tithis (1/6/11) for those having Scorpio and Ar-
ies as their Rashis; the Rikta Tithis (4/9/14) for those having Capri-
corm and Libra as their Rashis; and the Jaya Tithis (3/8/13) for those
having Sagittarius, Aquarius and Leo as their Rashis - these Tithis
are Ghaata (pain-giving) Tithis. These Tithis are inauspicious and
should be rejected when going on a journey.

31. THE GHAATA DAYS : Tuesday for the natives having
Capricorn as their Rashi; Saturday for those having Taurus, Leo and
Virgo as their Rashis, Monday for those having Gemini as their Rashi;

255 Journeys and Travels

Sunday for those having Aries as their Rashi, Wednesday for those
having Cancer as their Rashi; Friday for those having Sagittarius,
Scorpio and Pisces as their Rashis, and Thursday for those having
Libra and Aquarius as their Rashis - (each of these days) - is the
Ghaata Day. Journey is inauspicious on a Ghaata day.

32, THE GHAATA NAKSHTRAS : For the natives having Ar-
ies and so on as their Rashis the Nakshtras respectively Magha, Hasta,
Swati, Anuradha, Moola, Shravana, Shatabhisha, Rewati, Bharani,
Rohini, Ardra and Ashlesha these are ‘Ghaata’ Nakshtras and the
making of Journcy should be avoided in them,

33. THE GHAATA ASCENDANTS : For the natives having
Aries and so on as their Rashis the Ascendants respectively 1, 2, 4,
7, 10, 12, 6, 8, 9, 11, 3 and 5 are Ghaata Ascendants. The learned
should avoid them when they make a journey.

Notes: The Sanskrit word ‘Ghaata’ or ‘Ghaataka’ means ‘kill-
ing’ or that which kills. Therefore, the Ghaata Tithis and so on are
harmful or inauspicious and should be rejected.

34. THE CONSIDERATION OF ‘YOGINI’ : Yogini remains
in the Eastern direction on the 9th and the Pratipada Tithis (9/1), in
the Agneya direction (South East) on the 3/11 Tithis, in the South-
em direction on the 5/13 Tithis, in the Nairiritya direction (South
West), on the 4/12 Tithis, in the Western direction on the 6/14 Tithis,

256 Muhurta Chinta Mani
in the Vayava direction (North West) on the 7/15 Tithis, in the North-

em direction on the 2/!0 Tithis, and in the Ishan (East North) direc- .

tion on the 8th and the Amavasya Tithis. Yogini is not auspicious if
she remains in the front or on the  side.

Notes: Suppose one has to make journey on the Pratipada or
on the 9th Tithi. Then to the person going in the Eastern direction
Yogini will be in the front side, and to that going in the Southern
direction she will be on the left side because on these Tithis Yogimi
remains in the Eastern direction.

35. THE DOSHA OF KAALA AND PAASHA : There is
‘Kaala’ on the days Sunday and so on in the reverse order from the
Northern direction, that is, on Sunday Kaala is in the Northem di-
rection, on Monday in the Vayavya direction (North-West), on Tues-
day in the Western direction, on Wednesday in the Nairiritya direc-
tion (South West), on Thursday in the Southern direction, on Friday
in the Agneya direction (South East), and on Saturday in the East-
ern Direction. There is the position of ‘Paasha’ in the reverse side
of ‘Kaala’. In the night ‘Kaala’ and ‘Paasha’ are in the reverse di-
rection, that is, Paasha is there in the night where there is Kaala in
the day and there is ‘Kaala’ in the place of Paasha. ‘Kaala’ and
‘Paasha’ should not be in the front side in journey and in war.

257 Journeys and Travels

36. PARIGHA DANDA YOGA : Beginning with Kritika 7
Nakshtras are to be established (put) in each of the Four Directions
East and so on. In this figure the line joining the Vayavya (North-
West) direction and the Agneya direction (South East) is referred to
as ‘Parigha Danda’. In a journey there should be no violation of the
Parigha Danda. The meaning is that in the Nakshtras in the opposite
side to that side of which these Nakshtras divided by ‘Parigha Danda’
are journey should not be made.

Notes: The consideration of journey on the basis of ‘Parigha
Danda’ may be easily understood from the following speculum:
THE SPECULUM OF PARIGHA DANDA

Dhanishthe, Shatabhis
Poorvabhadrapada, Uttarabhadrapada,
Rewati, Ashwini and Bharani

Kritika, Rohini,
Mrigashira, Ardra,
Punarvasu, Pushya
Ashlesha

East

Uttarashadha, Abhijita,
Shravana

Vishakha, Swati, Chitra, Hasta,
Uttaraphalguni, Poorvaphalguni, Magha

Nairiritya South Agneya

The Nakshtras in this speculum are 28 as the Abhijita Nakshtra
has also been included. Beginning with the Kritika Nakshtra, seven
Nakshtras in each direction or side are placed in such a way that the
Nakshtras from Kritika to Ashiesha are in the East side, those from
Magha to Vishakha are in the South side, those from Anuradha to

” 258 Muhurta Chinta Mani

Abhijita are in the West side and those from Dhanishtha to Bharani
in the North side. Journey should be made only in those Nakshtras
so that there should be no violation of the line which has been drawn
from the Vayavya Angle (North West) to the Agneya Angle (East-
South). Thus in the Nakshtra in the East and the North side no jour-
ney is to be made in the West and the South side and in those in
the West and the South side journey will not be made in the East
and the North side.

The journey in that direction or side in the Naksktras which are
there in that direction is auspicious. Thus the journey in the seven
Nakshtras Kritika and so on in the East side will be auspicious but
that in the West or the South will be inauspicious. The tine which
has been drawn in the middle in the ‘Parigha cycle’ is referred to as

fafersitser  |

37, THE REMOVAL OF THE DOSHA OF ‘PARIGHA
DANDA’ The king (or any person) should make the journey in the
Agneya direction (side) in the Nakshtras of the East side, in the
Nairiritya direction in those of the South side, in the Vayavya direc-
tion in those of the West side and in the Ishan direction in those of
the North side. If the work is of urgent necessity, then leaving the
consideration of Dika Shoola, if the Ascendant of the Direction in
which the joumey is to be made is auspicious or favourable, the jour-
ney may be made even though the Parigha Danda is violated.

Notes: The journey of the main direction (such as the East Di-
rection) is the journey also of its Angle (such as of Ishan or of
Agneya) and the shoolas of the Directions will be the shoolas of the
Angles.

The directions of the 12 signs Aries and so on are fixed. Thus
the direction of the signs of Aries, Leo and Sagittarius is East, of
Taurus, Virgo and Capricorn is South, of Gemini, Libra and Aquari-
us is West, of Cancer, Scorpio and Pisces is North. This thing may

259 Journeys and Travels

be expressed in this way also - Aries, Leo and Sagittarius are the
Ascendant of the East, Taurus, Virgo and Capricorn of the South,
Gemini, Libra and Aquarius of the West, and Cancer, Scorpio and
Pisces of the North. When there is not the aspect of or conjunction
with the Malefic Planets and when there is the aspect of or conjunc-
tion with the Benefic Planets or when there is the Shadavarga of the
Benefic Planets in these signs, then the Ascendant of the related Di-
rection is said to be auspicious or favourable.

38. THE NAKSHTRAS OF THE JOURNEY IN ALL THE
FOUR DIRECTIONS : In the Nakshtras of Anuradha, Hasta, Pushya
and Ashwini the journey in all the Four Directions is auspicious

If a Retrograde Planet is in an Angle, or if there is the
Shadavarga of a Retrograde Planet in the Ascendant, then journcy in
such an Ascendant is prohibited (that is, it is inauspicious) and the
day of a Retrograde Planet is also prohibited.

39. THE AUSPICIOUS OR FAVOURABLE ‘AYANA’' IN A
JOURNEY : If the Sun and the Moon are in ‘Uttarayana’, then the
journey in the East and the North direction may be (auspiciously)
made; if both are in Dakshinayana then it is the journey in the West
or the South which may be (auspiciously) made. Lf the situation of
both is in different ‘Ayanas’, then the journey in that direction of
which is the Sun may be made in day time, and the journey in that
direction of which is the Moon may be made at night time. Death
(of the person undertaking the journey) occurs if journey is made con-
trary to the above condition.

260 Muhurts Chinta Mani

40. THE DOSHA WHICH OCCURS IF VENUS IS IN THE
FRONT SIDE IN THREE WAYS :

(i) THE DIRECTION (East or West) in which Venus rises will
bring her (Venus) in front of the person who is making journey in
that direction and therefore it will be left (the journey will not be
made)

(ii} According to the order of the North and the South sphere
(Venus is in the North sphere in the 6 signs Aries and so on and in
the South sphere in the 6 signs Libra and so on) Venus happens to
be in the front side to the person who makes a journey in front of
that sphere in which Venus is there. It is also to be rejected in mak-
ing & journey,

(iii} The seven Nakshtras from Kritika to Magha are said to be
the Dika-Nakshtras of the East Direction (Dika-Nakshtras, that is,
Nakshtras relating to Dika or Direction), the 7 Nakshtras from Magha
10 Vishakha of the South Direction, the 7 Nakshtras from Anuradha
to Shravana of the West direction, the 7 Nakshtras from Dhanishtha
to Bharani of the North Direction. In these Nakshtras journey should
not be made in that direction in which Venus is there according to
her situation.

41. VENUS’S DOSHA OF RETROGRESSION AND ITS EX-
CEPTION : If Venus is Retrograde, combust and is in her debilita-
tion sign, then the king who makes a journey (that is, who goes to
fight against his enemy) at that time becomes subjected to his ene-
my; but in such condition if Mercury is favourable, that is, he is be-
hind the traveller (the king) then he defeats his enemy, but if Mer-
cury is in front of him then the traveller king does not get victory.

42. THE EXCEPTION TO VENUS'S BEING IN FRONT AND
THE DOSHA OF VENUS’S BEING COMBUST : As long as the

261 Joumeys and Travels

Moon remains from Rewati to the First Quarter of the Kritika
Nakshtra, Venus is said to be blind. In such a situation the Dosha
of Venus’s being in front or on the right side does not occur. If, even
when the journey has been commenced in auspicious Muhurta (time),
Venus gets combust in mid way or happens to be in front, then the
travaller king should stay in mid way as long as Venus does not rise
and does not come on the left side.

Notes: According to an old view Venus remains blind as long
as the Moon remains from Rewati to the end of Mrigashira.

43. REJECTION OF JOURNEY IN THE AQUARIUS ASCEN-
DANT : The learned should reject as inauspicious, at the time of jour-
ney, the Aquarius Ascendant and the Navamsha of the Aquarius in
any Ascendant because there is loss of money at every step of the
king who travels in these Yogas.

44. INAUSPICIOUS AND AUSPICIOUS ASCENDANT : The
person who makes a journey in the Pisces Ascendant and when there
is the Navamsha of Pisces in any ascendant gets lost on the way (that
is, he strays from his way). And if the Lord of the natal Ascendant
and the Lord of the Rashi at the time of birth, both of these are
benefic planets and they are in the Ascendant then the journey is aus-
picious or if the Natal Ascendant or the Rashi at the time of birth is
in the Ascendant at the time of birth, even then the journey is aus-
picious.

45. OTHER INAUSPICIOUS ASCENDANTS : If during the
time of journcy the 8th sign from the Natal Ascendant or the Rashi

262 Muhurta Chinta Mani

at the time of birth, or the 6th sign from the natal Ascendant or the
Rashi at the time of birth of the enemy king is there in the Ascen-
dant, or the Lords of these signs are there in the Ascendant, then the
traveller king is subjected to death.

46. AUSPICIOUS ASCENDANT AND JOURNEY BY A
BOAT: If at the time of journey the Ascendant or the Moon is in
his Vargottama Navamsha, then the journey is the giver of desired
effects. If they are in a watery sign (Cancer, Capricorn, Aquarius and
Pisces) or the Navamsha of the Ascendant is of a Watery sign, then
journey by boat is auspicious and it serves all ends or completes all
deeds.

47. THE ‘DIGDWAR’ ASCENDANT : The journey which is
commenced at the time when the Ascendant of the Digdwar (Indica-
tor of the Directions or sides) rises is the giver of wealth and of vic-
tory or success, but the journcy made in the Ascendant opposite to
the Direction or side is the bringer of loss, destruction and fear from
the enemy.

Notes: Aries, Leo and Sagittarius for the journey which is made
in the Eastern Direction, Taurus, Virgo and Capricorn for that made
in the Southern direction, Gemini, Libra and Capricom for that made
in the Western direction, and Cancer, Scorpio and Pisces for that
made in the Northern direction are called the Digadwar Ascendants,
and Gemini, Libra and Aquarius for the journey which is made in
the Eastern direction, Cancer, Scorpio and Pisces for that made in
the Southern direction, Aries, Leo and Sagittarius for that made in
the Western direction, and Taurus, Virgo and Capricorn for that made
in the Norther direction are the opposite Ascendants.

263 Journeys and Travels

48. THE SECOND AUSPICIOUS ASCENDANT: If the Janama
Rashi of the person making a journey is occupied by Benefic Plan-
eis or the 8th sign from the Natal Ascendant or the Janama Rashi of
the enemy is there in the Ascendant at the time of the journey or
the Rashi which is named as Veshi (The second sign from that sign
in the horoscope of the person making the journey in which the Sun
is there is referred to as Veshi sign) is there in the Ascendant ai the
time of the journey then the journey is the giver of victory or suc-
cess of making a journey in the Ascendant which has the Raj Yogas
described in the Jataka shastras is the giver of victory or success.

Notes: In the above shloka (shioka 48) Shri Rama Daivagya has
recognised the following Ascendants as auspicious at the time of a
journey:

1, If the traveller’s Janama Rashi (the Rashi or sign in which
the Moon was there at the time of birth) is occupied by Benefic Plan-
ets. According to a learmed commentator it is that Rashi or sign which
is occupied by Benefic Planets in the horoscope that has been regard-
ed as auspicious for the Ascendant at the time of journey.

(ii) If the Rashi or sign which is the 8th from the Natal As-
cendant or the Natal Rashi (Janama Rashi) of the traveller's (king's)
or the claimant’s enemy is the Ascendant at the time of journey will
bring victory or success.

(iii) If th: Ascendant at the time of the journey is of that Rashi
or sign which is the second from that sign in which the Sun is there
in the horoscope of the traveller, then also the journey will bring vic-
tory or success.

(iv) Any such Ascendant which rises ai the time of making a
journcy that causes the formation of Raj Yogas described in the an-
cient shastras.

265 Journeys and Travels

the Ascendant which is there at the time of a journey a Planet is in
the same direction of which he is the Lord, then it forms the Lalatika
Yoga. Here it becomes clear that if the Lord of a direction is in any
other of the 3 Angles than the Angle related to his own direction,
the jourey is auspicious. Thus if the Sun, (the Lord of the Eastern
direction) is in the Ascendant (the House related to the Eastern Di-
rection), then the journey should be rejected. The situation of the Sun
in any other of the Angles will be auspicious for a journey.

52, AUSPICIOUS TIME FOR MAKING JOURNEY : The king
should start on a journey from his home and should move towards
the destination in the Mrigashira Nakshtra, in the Ardra Nakshtra he
should stay on the way and then should restart on the journey in the
Punarvasu Nakshtra, by doing so he gets victory over his enemies.

In the same way he should start from home in the Anuradha
Nakshtra, should stay on the way in the Jyeshtha Nakshtra and then
should restart on the journey in the Moola Nakshtra; by doing so he
conquers his enemies.

Notes: It is proper for.the king that for getting victory over his
enemy he should start on journey in the Mrigashira Nakshtra and the
jourmey, should remain continued as long as this Nakshtra remains.
and when in the journey there begins the Ardra Nakshtra he should
stay in some safe place during the time of this Nakshtra and then
again at the beginning of the Punarvasu Nakshtra the journey is to
be resumed. If he does so he will certainly get victory.

In the same way in the second yoga Shriram Daivagya says that
the king gets victory if he starts on the journey in the Anuradha
Nakshtra, stays on the way in some safe place during the period of
the Jyeshtha Nakshtra and then resumes the journey in the Moola
Nakshtra.

266 Muhurta Chinta Mani

53. OTHER YOGAS WHICH PROCURE VICTORY : The king
who is desrious of getting victory should start in the Hasta Nakshtra,
should stay for rest during the Swati and Chitra Nakshtras and then
should resume his journey in the Vishakha Nakshtra.

And if the journey is commenced in any of the Nakshtras of
Dhanishtha, Rewati and Pushya and it is resumed after staying in his
own terntory for one night, then such journey procures the gain of
lands in the war. ,

54. THE STRENGTH OF TIME : A journey ‘is auspicious in
the morning in ali the other directions than in the Eastern, in the
Godhuli (the dusk) in all the other ones than in the Western, at mid-
night in all the other ones than in the Northern, in the Abhijita
Nakshtra in all the other ones than in the Southern.

55. THE NAMES OF THE TWELEVE HOUSE, THE ASCEN-
DANT ETC. : The names of the 12 Houses the Ascendant etc are
respectively, Deha (body), Kosha (accumulated wealth}, Dhatushka
(arms and weapons), Vahana (Conveyance), Mantra (thinking and
counselling power), Ari (Enemy), Marga (Way) Ayu (Longevity),
Hridaya (Heart}, Vyapaar (profession), Agama (gains) and Vyaya (ex-
penditure).

Notes: Here the names of 12 Houses are related to those mat- |
ters which are studied in the various tespective Houses. Thus, ‘Deha’
or body is studied from the Ascendant, In the same way ‘Kosha’
Means accumulated wealth, ‘Dhatushka’ means arms and weapons. |
These are studied respectively from the 2nd and the 3rd House. Other
Houses also have names indicating matters which are studied from them.

267 Journeys and Travels

56. AUSPICIOUSNESS AND INAUSPICIOUSNESS IN THE
ASCENDANT AT THE TIME OF JOURNEY ACCORDING TO
PLANETRY POSITION: In a journey the situation of Benefic Plan-
ets in the Angles and Trines from the Ascendant and the situation
of Malefic Planets in the 3/11/6/10 Houses from there is auspicious.
The Moon in the 1/12/6/8 House from the Ascendant, Saturn in the
10th House, Venus in the 7th House, and the Ascendant Lord in the
7/12/6/8 House - these (combinations) are inauspicious.

57. THE STRENGTH OF COMBINATIONS, NAKSHTRAS
ETC. FOR THE KING AND BRAHMINS ETC. : Success has been
predicted from the Raj yogas forming in the Ascendant during the
journey for the kings or the Kshtriyas, from the strength of the
Nakshtras for the Brahmins, for the thieves on the basis of good
omens and due to Muhurtas for the other general people.

Notes: If there is an urgent recessity and immediacy and aus-
picious Muhurta for the journey is not there, then the Muhurta should
be decided according to the class (or castes) described in the Shloka.
Thus the Kshtriyas should make a journey in such necessity if in the
Ascendant at the time of the journey the situation of the Planets is
“Yoga karaka" (that is, it forms an auspicious combination). Similar-
ly, the person belonging to the Brahmin caste may make a journey
in such necessity after consulting an auspicious Nakshtra, the thieves
after seeing an auspicious omen at the time of commencing a jour-
ney, and the genneral people after consulting an auspicious Muhurta.

58. THE YOGA OR COMBINATION LEADING TO VICTO-
RY : If from the Ascendant at the time of journey the Sun be in the

270 Muhurta Chinta Mani

67. If in the Ascendant at the time of journey there be Jupiter
in the 7th House, there be the Moon in the 11th House, the Sun,
Venus and Mercury be gone in the 10th House and Saturn and Mars,
that is, Malefic Planets should be there in the 3rd House, then the
king gets victory.

68. By making journey in an Ascendant in which there is the
situation of Jupiter and the Moon in the Ascendant, the Sun be gone
in the 6th House, Mercury in the 5th House and in the 10th House
there be Saturn, and Venus be gone in the 4th House, the king gets
victory.

69. The journey of the king in a Raj Yoga Karaka Ascendant
in which Mercury being endowed with strength be gone in the As-
cendant, in an Angle there be Jupiter and in any of the 12/3/6/9 Hous-
es there be the Moon in emaciated condition, is the giver of victory.

70. The king who makes a journey in an Ascendant having the
combination of Planets in which the 9th, 8th and the 7th House, are
without any Malefic Planets and Venus being situated in the 4th or
3rd or the 111 House gets the aspect of Jupiter who is gone in an
Angle, gets a great wealth.

271 Journeys and Travels

71. If Mercury being sitiated in any of the 6/1/10/4 House gets
the aspect of Benefic Planets and the 12/1/7 Houses are without any
Malefic Planets, even then the king becomes victorious.

72. THE YOGAS PROCURING KINGDOM . If Jupiter be sit-
uated in the Ascendant, in the 10th or the 11th House there be the
situation of the Malefic Planets, then by making journey in an As-
cendant having such combination of Planets, the king has the obtain-
ment of kingdom. Or by making a journey in an Ascendant having
a combination in which Mercury and Venus be situated in the 7th
House, in the 4th House there be the situation of the Moon there is
the obiainment of kingdom for the king, this has been said by alt

73. AGAIN THE YOGAS GIVING VICTORY : If Venus be
in the 6th House, Jupiter in the Ascendant and the Moon in the 8th
House, the king gets victory. Or, if Mercury and Venus be in the
4th House and the Moon be in the 7th House or the Moon being
between Mercury and Venus be in the 4th House, that is, on one side
of the Moon who is gone in the 4th House there be Mercury and on
the other side there be Venus, even then the king gets victorious.

74. If in the Ascendant there be Venus, in the 7th House there
be Jupiter, in the 6th House Mars, in the 4th House Mercury and in
the 3rd House Saturn, the king gets victory.
Or, tf on Thursday Mercury and the Sun be there in the 6th

273 Journeys and Travels

But in the present shioka in the combinations named as ‘Yoga’, and
*Yogadhiyoga’ Mercury in the 4th House and Venms in the 7th House
both are capable of producing auspicious situations. In such a para-
doxical condition recognition is to be given to the more powerful rule.
Therefore, here the combinations named ‘Yoga’ and “Yogadhiyoga’
being more powerful, the less powerful yoga of Lalatika will be re-
jected.

In the “Peeyusha’ commentary of the present book in the situ-
ation of Mercury, Jupiter and Venus being in Angles and Trines, 108
kinds of combinations have been referred to, but from the practical
point of view it is not possible to write about all of them here.

77. VIJAYADASHMI : The 10th Tithi of the Shukla Paksha
of the Month of Ashwina is known by the name of ‘Vijayadashmti’.
This Vijayadashmi is the giver of success in all matters. If there oc-
curs the Shravana Nakshtra aiso on the day of ‘Vijaya dashmi’, then
it is the giver of an extremely auspicious effects, and it is the giver
of victory and maker of treaty in the journey of the king undertak-
ing it.

78. THE TIME OF JOURNEY ACCORDING TO THE PURI-
TY OF THE CONSCIENCE AND GOOD OMEN : If the Conscience
(of the person undertaking the journey) is pure and there is good
omen, then having known the strength of the Ascendant, that is, if
the Ascendnat is strong, then the journey made in such a time gives
success. The purity of conscience is more excellent than the other
factors. Therefore, if there is no purity of conscience, journey should
not be made.

274 Muhurta Chinta Mani

79. THE TIME WHICH SHOULD BE REJECTED AS INAUS-
PICIOUS FOR A JOURNEY : Journey should not be made if some-
one’s yogyopaveeta’ samskar is to be done in the family, if there is
the establishing of a god or goddess, if there is some festival and if
there is the period of ‘Asauch’ or ‘Sutaka-Pataka’ (the whole family
is regarded as impure and unclean for a certain period if there oc-
curs the birth or the death of some one in the family). If there ts
the untimely dazzling lightning, there is heavy rains and there is frost,
then journey should not be made for 7 days.

80. LEAVING THE CONSIDERATION OF DIKASHOOLA
ETC, WHEN THE PAY OF STARTING ON A JOUNEY AND
THAT OF REACHING THE OTHER VILLAGE IS THE SAME :
If the king has to start from one village and to enter the other vil-
lage on the same day after travelling, then in such a situation the
learned (astrologers) Pandits should have no consideration of the
Nakshtra shoola, day shoola, of Venus’s being in the front side and
of Yogini.

81. THE MUHURTA RELATING TO GOING ON A JOUR-
NEY AND ENTERING THE CITY IN WHICH THE KING WANTS
TO GO : If the king is in the position of makig journey and enter-
ing the city in which he wants to go on the same day, that is, he
can reach his destination on the day of starting, then the Pandits (As-
trologers) should consider the Muhurta of time of entering and not
of starting.

275 Joumcys and Travels

82. THE TRINAVAMI DOSHA IN THE JOURNEY : There
should be no going out (going on a journey again after returning)
on the  Tithi, in the 9th Nakshtra and on the 9th day from the
Tithi on which one has retumed from a journey and has entered
home, And there should be no entering one’s home on the 9th Tihi,
in the 9th Nakshtra and on the 91h day from the Tithi of going out
on a journey.

Notes: When one has returned from a journey one should keep
in mind that the day on which one starts on a journey again should
not be the 9th, the Nakshtra of the new Journey should not be the
9th one from that Nakshtra in which one has returned home. It is
the 9th Tithi, the 9th Nakshtra and the 9th Day which are inauspi-
cious. It is the Dosha of the 9th Tithi the 9th Nakshtra and the 9th
day which is called the ‘Trinavami’ Dosha.

83. DUTIES BEFORE GOING ON A JOURNEY : Before go-
ing on a journey it is proper that the journcy is begun after doing
‘havana’ or ‘yagya’, worshipping the gods, saluting the Brahmins,
worshipping the Planetary Lord of that Direction in which the jour-
ney is to be made, giving alms to the Brahmins and properly medi-
tating on the Lord of the Direction in which one is going.

276 Muhurta Chinta Mani

84-85, THE ‘DOHAAD’ FOR THE PACIFICATION OF THE
DOSHA ARISING DUE TO NAKSHTRAS IN A JOURNEY :
Dohaad: The thing to eai which a pregnant woman has a very strong
desire is referred to as Dohaad): In Ashwini the pulse of Urd and
Barley, in Bharani Mollasses and Rice, in Kritika ‘Urd’, in Rohini
the Curd of cow milk, in Mngashira the Ghee made from Cow milk,
in Ardra cow milk, in Punarvasu meat of deer, in Pushya blood of
deer, in Ashlesha ‘Kheer’ (rice and raisins, coconut etc. boiled in
milk), in Magha the meat of papiha (a kind of bird), in
Poorvaphalguni the meat of deer, in Uttaraphalguni the meat of hare
or rabit, in Hasta the rice of Saathi in Chitra the rice of Kangani (a
kind of rice) in Swati pua, in Vishakha the Chitra Vichitra bird (a
kind of bird), in Anuradha excellent fruit, in Jyeshtha the meat of
Tortoise, in Moola the meat of Sarika or Maina (a kind of bird) in
Poorvashadha the meat of Goha (a particular creature, it is a reptile),
in Uttarashadha the meat of Sahi (porcupine), in Abhijita Moong
(A pulse), in Shravana Kichhri (rice and pulse boiled together), in
Dhanishtha rice and Moong, in Shatabhisha bread or.cake of barley,
in Poorvabhadrapada fish and rice, in Uttarabhadrapada kichhhri and
in Rewati rice and curd - it is these things after eating, or seeing, or
touching which one should start on a journey, that-is, one should have
the consideration whether these are worth eating (eatable) or not ac-
cording to one’s caste or region and then at the time of starting on
journey one should eat that thing which is eatable for him, and should
only see or touch that thing which is not eatable.

86. THE DOHAAD OF DIRECTION : In the journey in the
Eastern direction one should start on it after consuming ghee (but-
ter), in the southern direction after eating Mollasses and Rice, in the
Western direction after eating fish, and in the Northern direction af-
ter drinking milk.

277 Journeys and Travels

87. THE DOHAAD OF DAY : Journey on Sunday should be
begun after eating Rasala (it is made from the mixture of curd, sug-
ar, pepper, ginger and Ilaichi) on Monday  eating kheer, on Tues-
day after drinking kanji, on Wednesday after drinking boiled milk,
on Thursday after taking curd, on Friday after drinking fresh milk
(unboiled) and on Saturday after taking rice mixed with Mollasses.

88. TITHI DOHAAD : Journey is to be made after using (eat-
ing or drinking) or seeing or touching Aakpatra (A vegetable also
known as Aak and Madaar) on the Pratipada, the water in which
grains of rice for cooking have been rinsed and cleaned, on the 2nd,
ghee, on the 3rd, imli (the fruit of a tree), on the 4th. Moong. on
the Sth, the water in which gold has been cleaned, on the 6th. Pua
(made from wheat flour mixed in water and sugar and cooked in oil
or ghee) on the 7th, Bijaura lemon (a kind of Lemon) on the 8th,
water, on the 9th, urine of cow on the 10th, barley on the | 1th. kheer
on the 12th, jaggery, on the 13th, blood, on the 14th and Moong on
the Pauranmaasi or on the Amavasya Tithi.

89. HOW TO GO ON JOURNEY : At the time of journey first
of all one should walk 32 steps on the right foot, then one should
ride the means of conveyance which is popular in that direction in
which the journey is to be made and then should go in the desired
direction after giving Mollasses, Ghee, Gold and Copper vessel to a
learned Astrologer.

278 Muhurta Chinta Mani

90. MEANS OF CONVEYANCE FOR GOING iN THE VAR-
IOUS DIRECTIONS : The king has to make the journey in the East-
ern direction on elephant back, in the Southern direction on a chariot,
in the Western direction on horse back and in the Northern direction
on a cart or Palanquin drawn or carried by men.

91. THE PLACE WHERE FROM JOURNEY SHOULD BE
BEGUN : The king should begin his journey from a temple (or from
that room in which worship of gods and goddesses is performed), or
from the teacher’s room or from his own room or from the room of
his principal wife (If he has many wives), after cating the grains
which have remained after doing “Yagya’ or Havana, with the per-
mission of Brahmins and seeing things of good or auspicious omens
and singing religious songs which are sung for having good time.

92. THE THINGS WHICH ARE TO BE DONE IF ONE GETS
LATE FROM THE PRESCRIBED TIME IN THE JOURNEY: IF onc
gets late due to some unavoidable reasons in starting on the journey,
then in the time fixed for starting, Brahmins should roll yogyopaveeta
(the sacred thread worn by Pious Hindus); Kshtrtyas, Weapons,
Vaishhyas, honey; and Shudras, fruit of Amla or coconut or the thing
which a person likes, - (these things should be roiled) in the direc-
tion in which the journey is to be made and then the journey should
be commenced after doing the necessary work.

279 Joumeys and Travels

93. THE DEFINITION OF A JOURNEY : According to the
sage named Garga going from one house to another is referred to as
journey. The Muni or Sage named Bhrigu defines a journey as go-
ing into the premises of another village from one’s own village. In
the view of Muni Bharadwaja going to that extent to which the ar-
tow shot by one may reach, is called journey and Muni Vashishtha
says that going out of the village is a journey.

94. THE MEASURE OF GOING ON A JOURNEY : Some
Acharyas regard as going on a journey if one goes 500 Dhanusha or
bow (or the distance of 2000 hands or of 1000 yards) some others,
if one dees 200 Dhanusha or (800 hands or 400 yards) and still some
others, if one does only 10 Dhanusha or bow (40 hands or 20 yards)
from his place to the destination (the place. where one wants to go);
but the person going on the journey should make efforts to go in
that very direction from his place in which he wants to go.

95. THE TIME DURING WHICH ONE MAY STAY WHILE
MAKING A JOURNEY AND PROHIBITION OF COPULATION
OR SEXUAL INTERCOURSE : The king who is independent should
not stay, in the course of a journey, for 10 days at one place, a feu-
dal Lord (a small king) for seven nights, and an ordinary person for
3 days. And if he has to stay for more days, then he should restart
on the.journey on an auspicious day. And the king who is desirous

280 Muhurta Chinta Mani

of getting victory over his enemies should net indulge in |
or sexual intercourse for 7 nights before starting on the journey. if,
this is not possible, he should not indulge in sexual intercourse at
feast on the day on which he makes the journey

96. THE THINGS WHICH SHOULD BE LEFT WHILE MAK-
ING JOURNEY : For three days before the day fixed for making
journey the journey maker should not drink milk, 5 nights before it '
he should not get his hair or beard cut by the barber, on the day of
joumey he should not consume honey, should not rub oil on his body
and should not vomit by taking medicine for cleaning the body (bow-
els).

97. THE THINGS WHICH SHOULD BE SPECIALLY LEFT
WHILE MAKING JOURNEY: If someone makes a journey after eat-
ing oil, jaggery, salt and cooked meat, he returns as a patient of some
disease, and one who makes a journcy after insulting a woman (or |
his wife) and a Brahmin is subjected to death

98. THE DOSHA OF UNTIMELY RAINING AND ITS
CHARACTERISTICS : If there is rain fall in the four months of
Pausha, Magha, Phalguna and Chaitra, it is referred to as untimely
raining. in this untimely raining as long as there are no marks of foot-
steps of animals and men on the earth, there occurs no Dosha, that
is, it is only when due to rains there is so much mud on the earth
that marks of the footsteps of animals and men are made that there
occurs the Dosha of untimely raining

28) Journeys and Travels

99. THE CONSIDERATION OF JOURNEY WHEN THERE
OCCURS THE DOSHA OF UNTIMELY RAINING : If there occurs
less rain there is less Dosha and if there occurs more rains there is
greater Dosha. When there occurs thunder or there begins rainfall! the
king should get prepared the golden images of the Sun and of the
Moon and these should be given to a Brahmin as alms.

If there occurs adverse omens at the time of journey, then jour-
ney may be made after giving ghee and gold at one’s own will.

Notes: There occurs no Dosha if the rainfall happens to be on
the midway.

100-101, AUSPICIOUS OMENS AT THE TIME OF START-
ING ON A JOURNEY : If at the time of going on a joumey is seen
in front Brahmin, horse, elephant, fruit, curd, cow, mustard, lotus,
clean clothes, prostitute, musical instrament, peacock, Papiha (a bird),
Mongoose, a tied animal (red-coloured ox has been said to be more
auspicious), flesh, auspicious words, flower, sugarcane, pitcher filled
with water, umbrella, wet clay, virgin or unmarried girl, gem, pugree, |
clean white ox, wine, woman having her son in her lap, burning fire,

282 Muhurta Chinta Mani

spectacles, kajal-surma (soot), washerman with washed clothes, fish,
ghee (butter), throne, a dead body over which there is no weeping,
flag, honey, hegoat, weapon, gorochana (material for worship),
Khanjana {a bird), Palanquin, Sound of Veda (4 Vedas are the holy
books of the Hindus), auspicious deed, auspicious song (hymn) and
rod with which an elephant is driven - any of these - then it is a
good omen and auspicious-effects-giving in a journey. And if some
one is seen with an empty pitcher coming from behind, it is also an
auspicious omen.

102,103: INAUSPICIOUS OMENS IN A JOURNEY : If at the
time of journey one sees in front of him barren woman, skin (of dead
animal), husk, bone, snake, salt, cinder (burning), fuel, impotent man,
excretion, oil, lunatic, fat, medicine, enemy, a sanyasi (one who has
renounced the world and has devoted himself to the worship of God
to get salvation) having long hair, grass, a diseased person, naked
person (but not an infant), man who has applied ‘ubtana’ on his body,
men with loose hair, depraved (outcaste), one having lopped limbs,
man oppressed with hunger, blood, the blood of a woman fallen due
to menses, chameleon, the burning of one’s own house, battle of cats,
sneezing, man wearing saffron-coloured clothes, jaggery, bate (of cot-
ton}, mud, widowed woman, man with bent (curved) body, quarrel
in the family, falling off of clothes from the body, the fight of a he-
buffalo, black grains (Mollasses, urd), cotton, vomitting, sound of ass
or donkey, very angry person, pregnant woman, man who has got
his hair cut, wet clothes, bad words (abuse or curse), blind deaf,

283 Journeys and Travels

woman in menses-it is inauspicious

104, OTHER AUSPICIOUS OMENS: Goha (Godha, a creature),
Jahaka (a creature which shrinks when it sees some one), pig, snake,
rabbit or hare, the seeing of these or the recitation through one’s own
mouth or through some one’s mouth their names or their sound is
auspicious; but if these creatures make sound themselves, it is
inauspicios. It is reversed if the creatures are monkey and bear, that
is the secing and the hearing of the sound uttered by them is auspi-
cious. But the recitation of their names by one’s own self or by some
other person is inauspicious. At the time of crossing a river and reach-
ing the other bank, running away with fear, entering the newly con-
structed house, war and searching for a lost article, the above stated
auspicious omens are inauspicious and inauspicious ones are auspi-
cious, that is, the inauspicious omens (described in shloka no. 102
and 103) alone in these matters are excellent or auspicious. But it is
the auspicious omens which have been mentioned for the journey
made in order to see the king, that should be regarded as auspicious.

105: THE OMENS HAPPENING TO BE ON THE LEFT SIDE
AT THE TIME OF JOURNEY: If Cuckoo, chhipakali or lizzard (a
Teptile), female pigeon, female pig, rala (3 bird), pingla or bhairvi (a
bird), musk rat, female jackal and the birds having male names are
seen on the left side, then these are auspicious.

106. THE OMENS HAPPENING TO BE ON THE RIGHT

284 Muhurta Chinta Mani

SIDE: If Chhikkar, a deer having female name, Pikkaka (8 bird),
Bhaas {a bird) Shrikantha (a bird), monkey, Ruru (A deer), bird hav-
ing female name, Crow, Bear and Dog are seen at the right side at
the time of journey, these are auspicious.

107. GENERAL OMENS OF THE RIGHT SIDE: If during
journey a Mriga (a deer, except Ruru) and a bird come on the right
side of the journey-maker, it is auspicious. If deer in odd member
are seen going, it is specially auspicious. And the braying of .an ass
on the left side is also auspicious.

108. THE REMOVAL OF INAUSPICIOUS OMENS: If when
an inauspicious omen occurs in a journey for the first, time, the jour-
ney should be begun again after staying for the duration of 4 breaths
(1 breath = 4 seconds). If an inauspicious omen occurs for the sec-
ond time also, then the journey should be begun again after staying
for 16 Breath and it will be auspicious, but if an inauspicious omen
occur for the 3rd time aiso, then the journey should not be made at
all.

109. THE MUHURTA FOR ENTERING ONE’S HOME AF-
TER RETURNING FROM JOURNEY: Afier returning from journey
entering one’s home is auspicious in the Nakshatras named as Mridu
or delicate (Mrigashira, Rewati, Chitra, Anuradha) and in those named
as Dhruwa or fixed (All the 3 Uttaras and Rohini).

If one enters one's home in the Nakshtras named as Kshipra
(Hasta, Ashwini and Pushya) and in those named as Chara or Mov-
able (Swati, Punarvasu, Shravana, Dhanishtha, Shatabhisha) one has

285 Journcys and Travels

to make the journey again. If one enters in the Nakshtra of Vishakha,
it destroys one’s wife, if in Kritika, one’s home, if in the Nakshtras
named as Tikshna or sharp (Moola, Jyeshtha, Ardra, Ashlesha) one’s
son, and if in those named as ugra or intense (Al] the 3 Poorvas,
Bharani and Magha), one’s own self.

Notes: Our Acharyas have mentioned four kinds of Praveshas
or entering:

1, ‘Apoorva Pravesha’ (Entering for the first Time) Entering a
newly constructed house after constructing it is said 10 be ‘Apoorva
Pravesha’ .

2. Supoorva Pravesha: After a journcy which was made to fight
a war entering one’s palace in the capital is said to be ‘Supoorva
Pravesha’

3. Dwandabhaya Pravesha: Dwandabhaya Pravesha is that one
when due to the fear of fire or of any other calamity one leaves one
house and enters another.

4. Navabadhu Pravesha: The entering of a newly wedded bride
into the bridegroom’s house for the first time is referred to as
Navabadhu Pravesha: Shri Rama Daivagya has described in great de-
tail in separate chapters about the ‘Apoorva Pravesha’ and the
‘Navabadhu Pravesha’. Here he has managed to tell us of that
Pravesha or entering when after defeating his enemy the King again
enters the palace or castle of his capital, or of that Pravesha when
an ordinary person after the sucessful completion of his journcy re-
turns home and enters his house.

110. THE RECAPITULATION OF THE DOSHAS DE-
SCRIBED IN THE CHAPTERS RELATING TO MARRIAGE AND
TO JOURNEY: The Doshas arising due to Ayana, Nakshtra, Month,
Tithi and day, the being of shoola, Mercury and Venus in the front

35

CHAPTER 12
LIVING IN A CITY OR VILLAGE

1. THE CONSIDERATION OF LIVING IN A CITY OR VIL-
LAGE FROM THE VIEWPOINT OF AUSPICIOUSNESS OR IN-
AUSPICIOUSNESS, If the Rashi (Sign) of the Village (or the city)
in which the native lives is 2/9/5/11/10 from the Rashi of the native
according to the name by which he is known, then it is auspicious
for that person to tive there.

The number of the Varga (the group or class of letters to which
the first letter of the native’s name belongs) is doubled and to the
product is to be added the number of the Varga of the name of the
village (or the city) and the sum thus arrived at is to be divided by
8, the remainder thus obtained is the ‘Kakini' of the native. And the
number of the Varga of the village (or the city) is doubled and to
the product is to be added the number of the Varga of the native
and the sum thus arrived at is to be divided by 8, the remainder thus

289 Living in a City or Village

obtained is the  of the village (or the city). Of these two
Kakinis that one which is greater (of the native or of the village) is
the giver of Wealth. ( There fore, the Kakini of the native should be
greater than that of the Village)

Now Consideration (with regard to living) is made according
to the RASHIS. Those having Brahmin Varna Rashi should make the
door (entrance) of their houses facing the Eastern direction, those of
Vaishya Varna Rashi, the Southern direction, those of Shudra Varna
Rashi , the Western direction and those of Kshtriya Varna Rashi, the
Northern direction.

Notes: Every village or city is not suitable or auspicious for liv-
ing for every person. In order to know whether iiving in a village
or a city will be auspicious or not , the writer has given two meth-
ods in the shloka

Method No. 1. First, the Rashi of the name of the person and
of the village or the city are found out according to the first letter
of their names. Suppose there is a person named Shr Rama Kumar
Sharma, He wants to live in Delhi. The Rashis according to the names
of Shri Ram Kumar Sharma and of the city of Dethi are respectively
Libra and Pisces

Now the rule to find out whether living in a city or village will
be auspicious or not is as follows:- If the Rashi of the city (or the
village) is the 2/9/5/11/10 from the Rashi, according to the popular
name of the person having a wish to live in that city, then living in
that city will be auspicious for him

If the Rashi, according to the name, of the city is 1/3/4/6/7/8/
12 from the Rashi of the Person, the effects of these are respective-
ly enmity, loss, disease. jloss, enmity, disease and disease

Here the sign of Pisces {the Rashi of the city of Delhi) is the
6th from Libra, the Rashi of Mr. Rama Kumar Sharma. Therefore
living in Delhi for him is inauspicious. The effects of his living in
Delhi will be that he will have losses there

The way of knowing the 'Kakinis' of the native and of the Vil-
lage or City according to the 2nd method is as follows : the number
of the 'Varga' of the first letter of the name of the native is doubled
and to this the number of the Varga of the first letter of the Village
or City is added. The Sum thus obtained is divided by 8. The re-
mainder indicates the number of the native's Kakini.

293 Living in a City or Village

Similarly, the number of the varga of the first letter of the name
of the City or Village is doubled, to this is added the number of the
Varga of the first letter of the native’s name and the sum thus ob-
tained is divided by 8. The remainder will be the number of the
Kakini of the City or the Village.

Here is an illustration: suppose there is some person named Mr.
Darshan Lal Jain. He wants to live and settle in Agra. Now the first
letter of the name of Mr. Darshan Lal Jain is in the ‘Ja' varga whose
number is 5, and the first letter of the name of the City is in the
‘Aa’ varga the number of which is 1. The varga number of Mr.
Darshan Lal Jain will be doubled and to this will be added the varga
number of Agra and the sum thus obtained will be divided by 8

§x2+1+$%8=11+8. Here the remainder will be 3. This is the
Kakini number of Mr. Darshan Lal Jain. In the same way, the varga
number of Agra (1) will be doubled and to this will be added the
varga number of Mr. Darshan Lal Jain and the sum thus obtained
will be divided by 8

1x2+5+8=7+8. Here the remainder will be 7. This is the
‘Kakini' number of Agra. Here we see that the Kakini oumber of the
city is greater than that of the person who wants to settle there. It
means that it is the city which will be rich and not the person. Mr.
Darshan Lal Jain, because of having fewer Kakini nuraber, will be
poor and will remain in debt. Therefore, settling and living in Agra
will not be profitable for Mr. Darshan Lal Jain. It will be harmful
for him from the economic point of view.

In the latter half of the shloka there is the expression '‍
 Gisefa:. It means that ‘richer’ will be that whose 'Kakini is the
greater. Commentators have differently interpreted this sentence. Some
of them have explained it in this way: that whose Kakini is the greater
becomes “indebted”. But this view is not accepted by the sages. It is
altogether misleading

294 Muhurta Chinta Mani

2. CONSIDERATION WITH REGARD TO LIVING IN
A CITY ACCORDING TO RASHI: The persons whose Rashi is
Taurus, Leo, Capricorn and Gemini should not make their residen-
tial house in the middle part of the city, In the same way the person
whose Rashi is Scorpio should not construct his residential house in
the eastern part, he whose Rashi is Pisces, in the Agneya direction,
the person of Virgo Rashi in the Nairiritya, of Sagittarius, in the
Western, of Libra, in the Vayavya, of Aries in the Northern, and of
Aquarius in the Ishan. In the same way  (Aa varga) is strong
in the Eastern Direction  (Ka varga) in the Agneya Angle, 
(Cha varga) in the Western,  (Ta varga) in the Nairiritya An-
gle,  (Ta varga) in the Westren,  (Pa varga) in the Vayavya
Angle,  (Ya varga) in the Northern, and wat (Sha varga) in
the Ishan Angle, Every varga is the enemy to the varga which is the
Sth from it, (Therefore one should not make his residence in the di-
rection of the varga which is the 5th from his varga. Thus the direc-
tion of  (Aa varga) is the Eastern, so the persons of this varga
should not make their residence in the direction of wat (Ta varga)
which is the Sth from  (Aa varga).

Notes: the Lords of the 8 vargas are respectively Garuda,
Marjara, Singha, Swan, Sarpa, Mushaka, Mriga and Mesha. Every
varga has enmity with the varga which is the 5th from it because
the lord of every varga is the enemy of the Lord of the varga which
is the Sth from it. Thus the Lord of  (Aa varga) is Garuda. It
(Garuda) is the enemy of the Lord of 741 (Ta varga) which is sarpa,
as we know that Garuda eats away sarpa. Here the writer points out
that one should not make one's residence in the city or village of
the varga which is the Sth from it, and in the auspicious city or vil-
lage also one should not live in the direction, nor make the door of
his house in the direction of the varga which is the 5th from it.

295 Living in a City or Village

3,4. THE LENGTH AND THE BREADTH OF THE HOUSE
1 (one) is to be subtracted from the number of the ishta Nakshtra,
the remainder then is to be multiplied by 152. Again, in the same
way, 1 (one) is to be subtracted from the number of the Ishta ‘Aya’
and the remainder should be multiplied by 81. Both the products
should be added together and then 17 is to be added to the sum.
The sum thus obtained is to be divided by 216. The number which
is arrived at as remainder will be called ‘ Grahapinda’ that is the area
of the house or the product of the length and the breadth of the house
(which is to be constructed). If this ‘Grahapinda' is divided by the
Ishta (desired) Breadth, we can get the length. And if the ‘Grahapinda’
is divided by 8, then the remainder may be 1,2,3 and so on. These
remainders will be respectively the 8 'Ayas’ namely 1. Dhwaja, 2.
Dhooma, 3 Singha, 4. Shwana, 5 Vrasha, 6 Khara, 7. Gaja, and 8.
Dhwanksha.

Notes: The product of the Length and the Breadth of the house
has been referred to as ‘Grahapinda’ And in every house there are 8
'Ayas' the names of which are Dhwaja, Dhooma, Singha, Shwana,
Vrasha, Khara, Gaja and Dhwanksha. Of these the 4 odd ‘Ayas' name-
ly Dhwaj, Singha, Vrasha 2nd Gaja are auspicious. There will be
the 27 Nakshtras of the house also as there should be some Nakshtra
for the name of every person. In the chapter on marriage we have
read that at the time of marriages the ‘Melapaka' or Equation of the
bridegroom and the bride is considered properly. In the same way
there should be the consideration of the Melapaks of the house and
of the owner of the house. In the 'Melapakas' of the Bridegroom
and the Bride, the ‘Nadis’ of the two should be different. But here,
in the construction of a house one and the same Nadi of 'Vastu' and
of the owner of the house has been regarded as the most excellent
one.

The following points are to be kept in mind while thinking of
the Ishta (desired) Nakshtra.

1. Of the Nakshtras it is only the Nakshtras of Ardra,
Punarvasu . Pushya, Ashlesha, Magha, Poorvaphalguni, Shatabhisha,
Proovabhadrapada, Uttrabhadrapada that are auspicious. Therefore, it
is these 9 Nakshtras which are auspicious in reference to the con-
struction of 'Grahapinda’. The person desirous of constructing a house
has to equate one of these 9 Nakshtras in the same way as is done
while seeing the 'Melapaka' of the bridegroom and the bride

256 Muhurta Chinta Mani

(described in the chapter on marriage) the Nakshtra with which
Melapaka (Equating) is done will be referred to as the Ishta (desired)
Nakshtra. Acharya Vashishtha has referred to it in the following
statement:

that is, (while constructing a house) Rashi, koota and so on should
be considered in the same way as they are done in the case of the
marriage of the bridegroom and the bride. The Ishta Nakshtra for con-
structing the ‘Grahapinda' is that one with which the Nakshtra of the
name of the owner of the house has the right calculation. If the
Melapaka is proper with these 9 Nakshtras, then it is excellent, oth-
er wise the Ishta Nakshtra will be that one with which the Melapaka

is proper.

2. The Kritika, Rohini and Mrigashira, (these 3 Nakshtras), the
9 Nakshtras from Uttaraphalguni to Poorvshadha and the 3 Nakshtras
Rewati, Ashwini, Bharani these 15 Nakshtras are medium in auspi-
ciousness.

3. Utarashadha, Shravana and Dhanishtha these 3 Nakshtras
should be altogether rejected

4. The Ishta Nakshtra and the Nakshtra of the owner of the
house should not be the same

5. It is inauspicious if the Nakshtra of the house is in the East
to that of the owner of it.

It is onty that Nakshtra (of the house) that has the greater num-
ber of qualities similar to those of the Nakshtra of the owner of the
house ( According to chapter 6 shiokas from 21 to 36), and that has
the same Nadi which is to be selected as the Abhishta (desired)
Nakshtra. The Abhishta (desired) ‘Aya’ should always be odd
(1/3/5/7)

The example of finding out 'Grahapinda’: Suppose according to
the 'Chapter’ on Marmiage’ in the "Melapaka’' of the Nakshtra of Shri
Karan Singh which is Mrigashira 34 Gunas or qualities are the same
or are in harmony with the Abhishta (desired) Nakshtra of Ardra.
There fore taking the Ishta Nakshira of Ardra as 6 and the Ishta ‘Aya’
as 3 we make ‘Grahapinda’.

297 Living in a City or Village

If 1 is subtracted from the Ardra Nakshtra 6, the remainder is
5. if 152 is multiplied by the remainder, the product comes to be
760. If 1 is subtracted from the number of the Ishta ‘Aya’ (3), the
remainder is 2, when 81 is multiplied by 2, the product comes to be
162, When both the products are added together the sum obtained is
760+162=922. If 17 is added to it then the sum obtained is 939. If
it (939) is divided by 216, the remainder is 75. It is this (75) which
will be the Pinda or the area given by the Ishta Nakshtra and the
‘Ishta Aya’ In the same way many pindas may be made from the other
Ishta Nakshtras and the Ishta 'Ayas'. If we want to keep 10 as the
length in this pinda, then the breadth will be 755 10=7.5. Again if
the length is to be kept 15, then the breadth will be 75+ 15 =5. In
the same way by taking the breadth as. 6 we can find out the length
which will be 75 +6 = 12.5 and so on.

If the length and the breadth of the desired house in such an
‘Aya Pinda' is not suitabie for the person concemed, then 216 is added
again and again to the Pinda of 75 and in this way many Pindas will
be arrived at. Thus by adding 216 to 75 we get 507 Pinda and so
on. Here the measure of the length and the breadth has been taken
on the basis of hand according to the ancient measurement system,
It can be arrived at with meter and the other systems in the same

way.

5. THE CONSIDERATION OF THE DOOR OF THE HOUSE
ACCORDING TO AYA: If the Aya is Dhwaja (the ‘Aya’ which is
got according to the method described in the above shloka) then doors
should be made in all directions, if it is Singha, then in the Eastern,
the Southern, and the Northern direction, if it is Vrasha, then in the
Eastern direction, if it is Gaja, then in the Southern direction. OR a
Brahmin should make the door of his house in the Western direc-
tion, a Kshtriya in the Northern direction, a Vaishya in the Eastern
direction and a Shudra in the Southem direction.

298 Muhurta Chinta Mani

6. THE TIME WHICH IS TO BE REJECTED WHEN THE
CONSTRUCTION OF A HOUSE IS BEGUN: If from the Janama
Rashi of the owner of the house the Sun, the Moon, Jupiter and Ve-
nus are weak, that is, if they are in a malefic house, or if they are
combust or if they are in their debilitation signs, then there occurs
the destruction respectively of the owner of the house, of his wife,
of comfort and wealth.

If the Nakshtra in which the Moon be there at the time of be-
ginning the construction of the house and the Nakshtra of Vastu or
house happen to be in the front side, then the owner of the house is
not able to live in it and if these happen to-be in the back side, then
there occurs theft in that house

Notes: The consideration of the Moon Nakshtra and of the
Vastu or House Nakshtra being in the front side or in the back side
should be made according to the 36th shloka of the chapter on ‘Jour-
neys and Travels'. According to this shloka, if the Nakshtra of the
Moon and the Nakshtra of the house is there in the 7 Nakshtra from
the Kritika or the Anuradha Nakshtra respectively, the door of the
house should not be in the Eastern and the Western side respective-
ly ,and if these are there in the 7 Nakshtras from the Magha or the
Dhanishtha, it (the door of the house) should not be made in the
Southern and the Northern side respectively.

7. THE CONSIDERATION OF ' VAYAYA' IN THE BEGIN-
NING OF THE CONSTRUCTION OF THE HOUSE: The remain-
der which is obtained on dividing by 8 the number of the Ishta
Nakshtra of the house is the 'Vayaya' (the expenditure). To this ob-
tained number of "Vayaya' is to be added the number of the fetters
of the house Dhruwa, Dhanya etc. described in a shioka ahead. and
the number of the Pinda (described in the 3d and the 4th shlokas):
if on dividing the sum obtained by 3 the remainder is 1, then there
is the Ansha (share) of Indra if it is 2, that of Yama (Kritanta), and
if it is 3, that of Bhoopa (king). Of these the Ansha (share) of Yama
is inauspicious.
Notes: Here is an example to find out as to whose Ansha is

299 Living in a City or Village
there. Suppose the Ishta Nakshtra is Mrigashira whose number is 5,
on dividing it by 8 the remainder is 5. This is 'Vayaya’. Again, sup-
pose the name of the house is 'Sumukha’, the number of letters in
this name is 3. If 5 is added to 3 if gives 8. Again, if Pinda 75 is
added to 8 it gives 83, and if 83 is divided by 3 the remainder is 2,
2 is the Ansha (share) of Yama (Kritanta) which is inauspicious.

8. TO FIND OUT THE GRAHA DHRUWANKA (CONSTANT
OF THE HOUSE): There is the Dhrmwa (constant) of 1,2,4 and 8
in the directions East etc. If to the sum of the numbers of the
Dhruwas (constants) of those directions in which the doors, 1,2,3, or
4 what so ever number of these is to be made in the various direc-
tions 1 is added, and there are the 'Gharas’ (houses) having names
like Dhruwa etc.

If there is door in the house in the Eastern direction, then there
is 1 Dhruwa, if it is in the Southern direction there are 2 Dhruwas,
if it is in the Western direction there are 4 Dhruwas, and if it is in
the Northern direction, there are 8 Dhruwas.

Notes: For example, suppose one has to make doors in his
house in the Eastern and Norther Directions. Then to the sum of
the Dhrowas of the East and of North 1 and 8 = 9, 1 is added which
gives 10. Therefore, according to shloka no. 9 and 10 ahead the name
of the Graha will be Ugra'

THE NUMBER OF LETTERS IN THE NAMES OF THE
GRAHA. (HOUSE): According to shloka no. 8 if there is any of the
numbers 15,12,8,16,9,11 and 14, then the name of the house will be
of 3 letters; and if the numbers are 1,2,4,5,6,10,3 and 13, it will be
of 2 letters. and if the number is 7, it will be of 4 Setters.

300 Muhurta Chinta Mani

10. THE 16 NAMES OF THE GRAHAS (Houses): (1) Dhniwa,
(2) Dhanya, (3) Jaya, (4) Nanda, (5) Khara (6) Kant, (7) Manorama,
(8) Sumukha (9) Durmukha, (10) Ugra, (11) Ripuda, (12) Vittada,
(13) Nasha, (14) Akranda, (15) Vipula, (16) Vijaya, these are respec-
tively the names of the 16 Grahas.

Notes: According to the meaning of their names the Grahas,
Khara (evil minded person), Durmukha (ugly faced), Ugra (sharp),
Ripuda (enemy), Nasha (Destruction), Akranda (that which brings mis-
ery) are inauspicious. Therefore, consideration of it should be made
before constructing a house. What should be the name of the Graha,
it may be understood in the following way: suppose in a house there
are doors in the West, North, and South. Now by adding | to the
sum of the Dhruwas 2+4+8=14 we get 15. Therefore, according to
shloka 10, the name of this house should be 'Vipula’. This name is
auspicious.

11,12, TO FIND OUT THE ‘AYA' VAAR, ETC. OF A HOUSE
ACCORDING TO ANOTHER VIEW. The Pinda or the Area of the
house is put in 9 places and in each place it should be multiplied
respectively by 9/9/6/8/3/8/8/4/8. then the product should be divided
respectively by 8/7/9/12/8/27/15/27/12 ; the remainder will be respec:
tively Aya, Vaar, Ansha, Dhana, Rina, Nakshtra, Tithi, Yoga, Ayu.

If the Nakshtra of the Graha (house) obtained in this way is
the same as is the Nakshtra of the owner of the house. then there
occurs the death of the owner of the house.

Notes: This shloka tells us how to find out the Aya. vaar etc.
of a house. The way to find out these is as follows:

1. The Pinda (75) of the Graha is put in 9 places. If the Pinda
put in the first place is multiplied by 9 and the product is divided

301 Living in a City or Village

by 8, we get 3 as remainder. Therefore the 'Aya' should be taken to
be 'Singha'

2. If the Pinda (75) put in the 2nd place is multiplied by 9 and
the product is divided by 7, the remainder obtained is 3, and so the |
Vaar{day) should be Tuesday.

3, When the Pinda(75) put in the 3rd place is multiplied by 6
and the product is divided by 9, the remainder obtained is zero, there-
fore, the Ansha (Navamsha) is 9.

4, When the Pinda (75) put in the 4th place is multiplied by 8
and the product is divided by 12, the remainder is zero, and so the
Dhana (Money) is 12.

5. When the Pinda (75) put in the 5th place is multiplied by 3
and the product is divided by 8, the remainder obtained is 1, and
therefore, the ‘Rina’ is 1.

6. When the Pinda (75) put in the 6th place is multiplied by 8
and the product is divided by 27, the remainder obtained is 6, and
therefore, the Nakshtra is Ardra.

7. When the Pinda (75) put in the 7th place is multiplied by

8 and the product is divided by 15, the remainder obtained is zero,
and therefore, the Tithi should be Pauranmasi {or the 15th Tithi)

8. When the Pinda (75) put in the 8th place is multiplied by
4 and the product is divided by 27, the remainder obtained is 3, and
therefore, the ‘Yoga’ is ‘Ayushmaana'

9. When the Pinda (75) put in the 9th place is multiplied by 8
and the product is divided by 120, the remainder obtained is zero,
and therefore, the Ayu should be 120 years.

In the construction of house it is the odd Aya, the vaars (days)
of the Benefic Planets, their Rashis (signs) and their Navamshas, the
‘Deergha Ayu' which are auspicious.

302 Muhurta Chinta Mani

13,14. THE CONSIDERATION OF 'VRASHA VAASTU' IN
THE BEGINNING OF THE CONSTRUCTION OF HOUSE : By
counting 3 Nakshtras from that Nakshtra in which the Sun is situ-
ated the numbers are put in the head of the image of the size of
“Vrasha' (ox). If the construction of the house is begun in these
Nakshtras, the house is put on fire; after this 4 Nakshtras ahead are
to be imagined in the front legs , if the construction is begun in these
Nakshtras, the effect of it is zero, after this 4 Nakshtras ahead in the
rear legs, the effect of which is in the form of the stability of the
owner in that house; after this 3 Nakshtras in the back are helpful
in the obtainment of wealth; after this 4 Nakshtras are to be imag-
ined in the right womb, there is the obtainment of profit in these
Nakshtras; then 3 Nakshtras are to be imagined in the tail, if the con-
struction of house is begun is these Nakshtras, there occurs the de-
struction of the owner of the house, after this 4 Nakshtras are of the
left womb, the effect of these is poverty, after this 3 Nakshtras should
be imagined in the mouth of the ‘Vrasha’ (ox), if the construction of
house is begun in these Nakshtras, it proves to be pain giving.

15. THE DETERMINATION OF THE DIRECTION OF THE
DOOR OF THE HOUSE, THE NAKSHTRA OF THE CONSTRUC-
TION OF THE HOUSE AND THE MUHURTA FOR ENTERING
THE DELIVERY HOUSE : The construction of the door of the
house in the East and the West side is auspicious in the month of
Phalguna (February -March) if the Sun be in the Aquarius sign. in
that of Shravana (July-August) if he (the Sun) be in the Leo or the
Cancer sign, in that of Pausha (December-January) if be be in the
Capricorn sign, and the construction of the door of the house in the
North and the South side is auspicious in the month of Baisakha
(April-May) if the Sun be in the Aries- Taurus sign. in that of
Margashirsha if he be in the Libra-Scorpio sign,

303 Living in a City or Village

The beginning of the construction of house is auspicious in
these 13 Nakshtras—the Nakshtras named as Mridu or (Delicate),
(Mrigashira, Rewati, Chitra and Anuradha) those named as Dhruwa
or constant {the 3 Uttaras and Rohini), the nakshtras of Shatabhisha,
Swati, Dhanishtha, Hasta and Pushya.

It is excellent or auspicious if the beginning of the consiruc-
tion of Delivery House is done in the Punarvasu Nakshtra, and if en-
tering the Delivery House is done in the Shravana and the Abhijita
Nakshtra

16, THE CONSIDERATION OF THE BEGINNING OF THE
CONSTRUCTION OF HOUSE ACCORDING TO ANOTHER VIEW
: According to the view of an astrologer the beginning of the con-
struction of house is excellent or auspicious in the month of Chaitra
(March) if the Sun be in the Aries sign, in that of Jyeshtha (May
June) if he be in the Taurus sign, in that of Bhadrapada if he be in
the Leo sign, in that of Ashwin if he be in the Libra sign, in that of
Kritika if he be in the Scorpio sign, in that of Pausha if he be in
the Capricorn sign, in that of Magha if he be in the Aquarius sign,
and the beginning of the construction of house is inauspicious in the
month of Kartika if the Sun be in the Virgo sign, in that of Magha
if he be in the Sagittarius sign. Here the counting of the months
should be madc from the Pratipada Tithi (1) of the Krishna Paksha
(the dark half of the month), that is, after the Pauranmasi of Phalguna
to the Pauranmasi of Chaitra there is the 'Chandsa Chaitra Maasa’ and
from the Pauranmasi of Chaitra to the Pauranmasi of Baisakh there
is the Chandra Baisakh Maasa. Thus here the Chandra Maasa is not
counted form one AMavasya to another Amavasva.

Notes: According to the above two shiokas the beginning of
the construction of house has been said to be inauspicious if it is done
when the Sun be in the signs of Gemini, Virgo. Sagittarius and Pi-
sces. When the Sun is situated in the sign of Gemini, there is the

304 Muhurta Chinta Mani

possibility of the occurrence of the month of Jyeshtha and Ashadha,
when he be in that of Virgo, of Bhadrapada and Ashwin, when he
be in that of Sagittarius, of Pausha and Magha, and when he be in
that of Pisces of Phalguna and Chaitra. Therefore ,it has been spe-
cially pointed out that if in the month of Chaitra (from the Pauranmasi
of Phaiguna to the Pauranmasi of Chaitra) the Sun happens to move
into the sign of Aries, it is only then that even in the month ot
Chaitra too the beginning of the construction of house should be done.
If the Sun be in the sign of Pisces even the month of Chaitra should
be rejected for the construction of house. And if the Sun happens to
move into the sign of Taurus, it is auspicious to construct a house
in the month of Jyeshtha. If the sun be in the sign of Gemini, the
construction of house is inauspicious in the months of both Jyeshtha
and Ashadha. But if the Sun remains in the sign of Cancer then
Ashadha is auspicious for the construction of house. The month of
Bhadrapada is auspicious for the construction of house as long as the
Sun remains in the sign of Leo, if he moves into the sign of Virgo,
it is not auspicious. The month of Ashwin is auspicious for the con-
striction of house if the Sun moves into the sign of Libra, it is not
so if he remains in that of Virgo; and if the Sun remains in the sign
of Virgo even Kartika is inauspicious. If the Sun be in the signs of
Libra and Scorpio, the construction of house is inauspicious in the
month of Margashirsha. The month of Pausha is regarded auspicious
for the construction of house so long as the Sun should be in the
sign of Capricom. If the sun be’gone in the sign of Sagittarius then
the months of Pausha and Magha both are inauspicious. In the same
way the month of Phalguna is also left if the Sun moves into the
sign of Pisces

The conclusion is that here in the construction of house it is
not the Chandra Maasa (Lunar Month) but the Surya Maasa (Solar
month) which is considered as of greater importance. In the begin-
ning of the construction of house it is the Shukla Paksha which should
be chosen, in the Krishan Paksha it may be done by the Sth Tithi

17. INSTRUCTION WITH REGARD TO THE DIRECTION
OF THE DOOR ON THE BASIS OF TITHI The house facing the

305 Living in a City or Village

Eastern direction or side should not be constructed from the Pauran
masi (15th Tithi) to the 8th Tithi of the Krishna Paksha, that facing
the Northern direction from the 9th Tithi of the Krishna Paksha to
its 14th Tithi, that facing the Western direction from the Amavasya
(the 30th Tithi) to the 8th Tithi of the Shukla Paksha, and that fac-
ing the Southem direction from the 9th to the 14th Tithi of the Shukla
Paksha. The meaning is that the beginning of the construction of
house should be done leaving the Tithis which are inauspicious for
the direction in which the door of the house is to be made.

18. AUSPICIOUS TITHIS AND VAARS (DAYS) ETC. FOR
THE BEGINNING OF THE CONSTRUCTION OF HOUSE : The
beginning of the construction of house is regarded as auspicious on
the days and Tithis other than the days of Tuesday and Sunday, the
Tithis named as Rikta Tithis (4/9/14), those of Amavasya and
Pratipada (the 8th Tithi according to symbol), in the other Ascendants
than the Lagna or Ascendant called ‘Chala’ or Movable (1/4/7/10),
when the Benefic Planets are in the other House than the 12th and
the 8th House and when the Maiefic Pianets are in the 3/6/11 Houses,
in all the Nakshtras except the Panchaka Nakshtras.

Notes: The 5 Nakshtras Dhanishtha and so on are referred to
as the Panchaka Nakshtras. In the 15th shloka of this very chapter
Dhanishtha, Shatabhisha, Uttarabhadrapada and Rewati, these four
Nakshtras have been said to be auspicious for the construction of
house. It is only the Nakshtra of Poorva bhadrapada that has been
said to be inauspicious, this confusion is removed when we have in
our mind the following statement of the sage Mandavya.

According to this shloka, the leaving of the Panchaka Nakshtras
is in reference to the establishing of ‘Chaukhata’ (the base wood of
the door) after the beginning of the construction; and these Nakshtras
are to be left only when the house is to be made of wood alone, as

306 Muburta Chinta Mani

in the 48th shloka of chapter 2 there is the clear statement with re-
gard to the collection of wood in the Panchakas which has been pro-
hibited. The reference to it is there in the words "

19. THE CONSIDERATION OF RAHU MUKHA IN THE
BEGINNING OF HOUSE : In laying the foundation stone of the
abode of a god (or of gods) or of a temple the Sun's being in 3 signs
from the sign of Pisces, of a man in 3 signs from the sign of Leo,
and in the beginning of the digging of a tank in 3 signs from the
sign of Capricorn there is the mouth of Rahu in the opposite direc-
tions respectively from the direction of Ishan Angle (Ishan, Vayavya,
Nairititya and Agneya) and there is the back of Rahu in the oppo-
site direction to it, that is in the Angle of the back side. In laying
the foundation stone the direction or side of back is auspicious, that
is, if the mouth be in the Ishan Angle, the foundation stone should
be put in the Agneya Angle, if it be in the Vayavya Angle, in the
Ishan Angle; if it be in the Nairiritya Angle, in the Vayavya Angle.
If it be in the Agneya Angle, in the Nairiritya Angle.

Notes : The consideration of direction is necessary when the
foundation stone of a temple or of a house is laid or the digging of
a tank is begun. If at the time when the foundation stone of a tem-
ple is laid the Sun is of the signs of Pisces, Aries or Taurus then
the mouth of Rahu is towards the Ishan Angle (East-North), if he
(the Sun) be of Gemini, Cancer or Lea, towards the Vayavya Angle
(North-West), if he be of Virgo, Libra, and Scorpio, towards the
Nairintya Angle (South-West), if he be of Sagittarius, Capricom and
Aquarius, towards the Agneya Angle (ast-South). if a house is to
be constructed and if at the time of laying its foundation stone the
Sun is of Leo, Virgo or Libra, the mouth of Rahu is towards the
Ishan Angle, if he be of Scorpio, Sagittarius or Capricom, towards
the Vayavya Angle, if he be of Aquarius, Pisces or Aries, towards
the Nairiritya Angle; if he be of Taurus, Gemini, or Cancer, towards
the Agneya Angle. If at the time of digging a tank, a well etc. the
Sun is of Capricorn, Aquarius or Pisces, then the mouth of Rahu is

307 Living in a City or Village

towards the Ishan Angle; if he be of Aries, Taurus or Gemini, to-
wards the Vayavya Angle; if he be of Cancer, Leo or Virgo, towards
the Nairritya Angle; if he be of Libra, Scorpio or Sagittarius, towards
the Agneya Angle. Therefore, at the time of digging the foundation
of a temple or of a house or at the time of digging a well or a tank
the digging should be started leaving the side of Rahu's mouth, it
should be started from Rahu's back side. Here is the speculum show-
ing the directions in which Rahu's mouth remains.
