20. THE CONSIDERATION WITH REGARD TO THE CON-
STRUCTION OF WELL IN SIDE A HOUSE: If the well is con-
structed in the middle part of the Vastu (the land on which the house
is to be constructed) it causes the loss of wealth of the owner, if in
the Ishan Angie, the owner is strengthened; if in the East side, it
causes increase in the prandeur of the owner, if in the Ayneya An-
gle, loss (death) of his son; if in the Nairiritya Angle, it causes the

308 Muhurta Chinta Mani

death of the owner himself, if in the West side, it is auspicious, if
in the Vayavya Angle, it causes pain to the enemy; and if in the
North side, it is auspicious for the owner of the house.

21, THE CONSIDERATION WITH REGARD TO MAKE
KITCHEN, LATRINE AND BEDROOMS ETC. IN THE HOUSE :
There should be in the Eastern part of the Vastu (the land on which .
the residential house is to be built) place for bathing (for bath room)
for kitchen in the Agneya Angle, for bed rooms in the South side,
for keeping arms and weapons (if it is a royal palace, but if it is an
ordinary man's house, place for keeping instruments, sewing machine
motor etc.), for dining room in the West side, for store room (for
storing grains etc.) in the Vayavya Angle, for treasury in the North
side, and a place for the abode of gods or temple in the Ishan An-
gle. Respectively in the middle of the two rooms mentioned above
there should be place for chuming curd to make butter, for store room
to store Ghee (butter), for study room, for making a room in which
one may cry and weep (in a royal palace), for making a room in
which sexual intercourse may be done, for keeping medicines and
other household articles, which are needed in daily life

Notes: The places in the house should be made in the follow-
ing way: in the middie of the Eastern side and the Agneya Angle
there should be place (room) for chuming milk, curd and butter, be-
tween the Agneya Angle and the Southern side place for latrine;, be-
tween the Nairiritya Angle and the Western side place for study room,
between the Western side and the Vayavya Angle place for weeping
and crying | between the Vayavya Angle and the Northern side
place for having sexual intercourse, between the Northern side and
the Ishan Angle place for keeping medicines; and between the Ishan
Angle and the Eastern side place for keeping necessary things

The figure which will show how these places (rooms) should
be got made in the house is being given here for clear understand-
ing.

22. THE LONGEVITY OF THE HOUSE : If in the Ascendant
at the time when the construction of the house is begun Jupiter is
posited in the Ascendant, the Sun and Mercury in the 7th House, Ve-
nus in the 4th House and Saturn in the 3rd House, then the longev-
ity of the house is 100 years. And if there is Venus in the Ascen-
dant, the Sun be in the 3rd House, Mars be in the 6th House, and
Jupiter be in the 5th House, then in such combination of planets the
longevity of the house will be 200 years.

311 Living in a City or Village

26. THE EFFECTS OF NAKSHTRAS AND DAYS ON THE
BEGINNING OF THE CONSTRUCTION OF HOUSE: If Jupiter hap-
pens to be in any of the Nakshtras of Pushya, those named as Dhruwa
{all the 3 Uttaras and Rohini), Mrigashira, Shravana, Ashlesha and
Poorva shadha, and the day is of Thursday, the house the construc-
tion of which is begun in such yoga is the giver of house, issues
and kingdom.

And if Venus happens to be in any of the Nakshtras of
Vishakha, Ashwini, Chitra, Dhanishtha, Shatabhisha, and Ardra, and
the day too is Friday, then the house the constriction of which is
begun in such yoga is the giver of wealth and grains

27. OTHER INAUSPICIOUS AND COMFORTS AND SON-
GIVING YOGAS, If Mars be in any of the Nakshtras of Hasta.
Pushya, Rewati. Magha, Poorvashadha and Moola and the day be of
Tuesday, the house the construction of which is begun in such yoga,
gives to the owner affliction from fire and pain to his son.

And if Mercury happens to be in any of the Nakshtras of
Rohim. Ashwim. Uttaraphalgunt. Chitra and Hasta and the day hap-
pens to be of Wednesday. the house the construction of which is be-
gun in such yoga is the giver of house. comforts and son

312 Muhurta Chinta Mani

28. THE YOGA THE CONSTRUCTION BEGUN IN WHICH
MAKES THE HOUSE THE ABODE OF GHOSTS, SPIRITS ETC.:
If Saturn happens to be in any of the Nakshtras of Poorva
bhadrapada, Uttarabhadrapada, Jyeshtha, Anuradha, Swati and Bharani,
and the day happens to be of Saturday, then the house the cons-
truction of which is begun in such yoga becomes the abode of de-
mons,’ ghosts and pretas (spirits).

29. THE CONSIDERATION WITH REGARD TO THE MAK-
ING OF DOOR ACCORDING TO ANOTHER ACHARYA: Four
Nakshtras from that Nakshtra in which there is the Sun are on the
head of the door; if the door of the house is constructed in these
Nakshtras, the owner gets Laxmi or wealth. After this 8 Nakshtras
are there in the four corners af the house, if ihe door is constructed
in any of these Nakshtras, the owner has to wander out of his house.
After this 8 Nakshtras are there in the four branches of the door. If
the door is constructed in these Nakshtras which are there in the
branches, then the owner always gets comforts in that house. After
this 3 Nakshtras are there in the foot of the door. If the construction
of the door is begun in these Nakshtras, .it causes the death of the
owner. And after this 4 Nakshtras are there in the middle of the door.
If the construction of the door is begun in these Nakshtras the owner
gets comforts.

CHAPTER 13

CONSIDERATIONS WITH REGARD TO ENTERING A
NEWLY CONSTRUCTED HOUSE ETC.

1. THE MUHURTA FOR ENTERING A NEWLY CON-
STRUCTED HOUSE; Entering the house after the king's returning
from journey and entering the newly constructed house for the first
time is auspicious in the months of Jyeshtha, Magha, Phalguna and
Baisakha when the Sun is in ‘Uttarayana’, in the Nakshtras happen-
ing to be in the front of the door of the house, in the Nakshtras
named as Dhruwa (the 3 Uttaras and Rohini) and those named as
Mnridy or Delicate (Mrigashira, Rewati, Chitra and Anuradha), and in
the Upachaya (3/6/10/11) and the Sthira or Fixed Sign (2/5/8/11) As-
cendant from the Janama Rashi and Natal Ascendant of the owner

Notes: After the Completion of a long journey made by a king
or by an ordinary person entering one's house or entering a newly
constructed house is auspicious when the Sun is in Uttarayana and
the months are Jyeshtha, Magha, Phalguna and Baisakha. If the house
is covered with Straws or grass (and it is newly built) entering may
be made in Dakshinayana too. Entering the house is auspicious in
the Nakshtras named as Mridu or Delicate and Dhnuwa or Fixed and
in those which happen to be in the front of the door of the house,
Entering the house (for the first time) in the Nakshtra named as
Digdwar (stated in the 38th shloka of chapter 11) Nakshtra, as it is

314 Muhurta Chinta Mani

the Nakshtra of that direction-East, South, West and North whichev-
er it is-towards which there is the main door of the house in ques-
tion, is auspicious.

lf a dilapidated or old house is reconstructed entering it may
be done in the Nakshtras of Pushya Dhanishtha , and Shatabhisha.

Entering a new house is auspicious in the Lagna, which is the
3rd, 6th, 11th from the Natal Ascendant or the JanamaRashi, and in
Ascendants of the Fixed Signs.

At the time of entering a newly constructed house many
‘Doshas' stated in the Chapter on Marriage should also be avoided

2. SPECIAL RULES WITH REGARD TO ENTERING AN
OLD DILAPIDATED HOUSE: Entering an old, dilapidated, newly
constructed house because of the fear of fire etc. is excellent or aus-
Picious in the months stated above and in the month of Kartika,
Margashirsha, and Shravana, and in the Nakshtras of Shatabhisha,
Pushya, Swati and Dhanishtha also, and no consideration should be
made of the combustion of Jupiter and Venus when entering an old,
dilapidated house.

3,4, CONSIDERATION WITH REGARD TO THE PACIFICA-
TION OF EVILS AFFECTING THE NEWLY CONSTRUCTED
HOUSE BEFORE ENTERING IT: Before entering a newly con-
structed house there should be done the Worship of the 17 Nakshtras
namely, those named as Mridu or Delicate (Mrigashira, Rewati, Chitra
and Anuradha) those named as Dhruwa or Fixed (the three Uttaras
and Rohini), those named as Kshipra (Hasta. Ashwini and Pushya),
those named as 'Chara' or Movable (Swati, Punarvasu Shravana,
Dhanishtha and Shatabhisha) and the Nakshtra of Moola, and Bhoota

315 Considerations with Regard to Entering a Newly Constructed House etc.

Bali (a religious rite) should be performed. After this in an auspi-
cious Ascendant in which there are Benefic Planets in the 9/5/1/4/7/
10/11/2/3 houses and Malefic Planets are in 3/6/11/ houses and the.
4th and the Sth house are without any Planet and the Rashi (sign)
which is the 8th in the owner's Natal Ascendant and the Janama Rashi
should not be in the Ascendant at the time of entering the house, it
is auspicious to enter a newly constructed house. Sunday and Tues-
day, the Tithis named as Riktas (4/9/14) the Ascendants which are
named as ‘Chara’ or Movable (1/4/7/10), Amavasya, and the month
of Chaitra, these should be left for the Muhurta of entering a newly
constructed house. The Bhakooia of the owner of the house also
should be auspicious. In such auspicious Muhurta the owner should
enter the house taking pitcher filled with water and Brahmins ahead
of him.

5. THE CONSIDERATION OF THE SUN'S BEING ON THE
LEFT SIDE OF THE LAGNA AT THE TIME OF ENTERING THE
HOUSE: If the Sun be Sth Houses ahead from the 8th house from
the Ascendant which is fixed for entering the house that is, if the
Sun is posited in the 8/910/11 or 12 house (from the Ascendant at
the time of entering the house), then he (the Sun) is on the  side
for the person entering a house facing the Eastern Direction. And the
Sun is on the left side for the person entering a house facing the
Southern Direction if he (the San) is posited in the 5/6/7/8 or 9 House
from the Ascendant at the time of entering the house. If the Sun is
posited in the 2/3/4/5 or 6 House he is on the  side for the per-
son entering a house facing the Western Direction. The Sun’s being
on the left side is auspicious at the time of entering a house. For
the person entering 3 house facing the Northern Direction the Sun is
on the left side if he is posited in the t1/12/1/2/ or 3 House. Enter-
ing a newly constructed house is auspicious if it is dome on Poorna
Tithis (5/10/15) in a house having its door facing the Eastern
Direction, on the Nanda Tithis (1/6/11) in that facing the Southern
Direction, on the Bhadra Tithis (2/7/12) in that facing the Western
Direction, on the Jaya Tithis (3/8/13) im that facing the Northern
Direction. Entering on the Rikta Tithis (4/9/14) should be altogether
rejected.

316 Muhurta Chinta Mani

6. THE CONSIDERATION OF 'KUMBHA CHAKRA'
(Kumbha= Pitcher. Kumbha Chakra is explained in the shloka) Count-
ing from the Nakshtra in which the Sun is there to the Nakshtra at
the time of entering the house, one Nakshtra is supposed to be in
the mouth of the imagined shape of a pitcher, Lf entering is done on
that day the house catches fire and is burnt. Then 4 Nakshtras after
this are in the East. Due to entering in these Nakshtras the owner is
not able to live in that house. Form the Sun's Nakshtra the Nakshtras
having numbers from 6 to 9 are in the Southern Direction from the
pitcher. If entering is done in these Nakshtras, the house always re-
mains filled with wealth. The Nakshtras from 10 to 13 from the Sun's
Nakshtra are in the Western Direction from the pitcher. If the enter-
ing is done in these Nakshtras the owner is always getting Laxmi or
Wealth. The 4 Nakshtras after these are in the Northern Direction,
they cause quarre! and dispute ( if entering is done in them), then 4
Nakshtras in the womb cause the destruction of the owner, then 3
Nakshtras are in the bottom of the pitcher the effect of which is en-
abling the owner to live in the house for a long time. After this 3
Nakshtras are supposed to be in the throat of the pitcher, these
Nakshtras make the living of the owner in the house eternal (that is,
till his death)

7. THE THINGS TO BE DONE AFTER ENTERING: In this
way the king should enter his house in an auspicious Ascendant along
with an umbrella , flowers. Sound of the Mantras from the Vedas
etc.’ and having entered there should honour the architects, astrolo-
gers, those pandits who know the manner of worshipping the Vastu
and of Bhoota Bali, and citizens by giving them land, gold, clothes

etc. in charity.

INTRODUCTION TO THE FAMILY OF THE WRITER:

1. INTRODUCTION TO THE GRANDFATHER: There was
an excellent Brahmin named Chintamani in a Village named Dharma
Pur (Matri Pur) near the river Narmada in the region of Vidarbha.
He had deep study in 'Vyakarana Mahabhasya’ written by Shesha
Naga. He (Shri Chintamani ji) was at the top among astrologers and
was the writer of ail the 3 skandas (branches) of Astrology. He was
specially honoured by the kings of his country, The talent (knowl-
edge) of this Acharya named Chintamani was deep and all embrac-
ing in ‘Nyaya’ “Alankar Mimansa" and "Vedantas" etc.

2. The Daivagya (Astrologer) named shri Ananta, Son of Shri
Chintamani who shone like the Sun among the learned astrologers
of the Earth, whose feet were worshipped by all the Daivagyas (as-
trologers) of his times and who was learned in all the shastras or clas-
sics, became famous in the whole world due to the conduct which
was according to the prestige of the tradition of his father. Shr (Mr.)
Ananta Daivagya against the learned persons begetting disrespect for
predictive Astrology created (wrote) the charming ‘Jataka Paddhati'
(Jataka System) which was able to destroy the aims of these learned
persons, and for the happiness of the gentle, he wrote a clear and

318 Muhurta Chinta Mani

charming commentary on the book named ' Kamadbena ' which ex-
plains the method of making ‘Panchanga’ according te the principle
of ‘Brahma’ and the principles of the Aryas

3. The younger brother of Shri Neelkantha Daivagya son of
Ananta Daivagya was very liberal minded; he bore the name of
Rama’. He, Rama Daivagya, paid obeisance on the lotus feet of Shri
Ganesha ji and in the extremely excellent city of Kashi wrote this
book named 'Muhurta Chintamani' in the 1522 Shaka Era, 1657
Vikrami Era and 1600 Christian Era.
